VALUE-ADDED SERVICES TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Value-Added Services Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, browse, purchase, redeem, receive, send, pay for or otherwise use value-added services made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By using any Value-Added Service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Acceptable Use Policy, Community Terms and any service-specific terms, provider terms, network terms, merchant terms, utility terms or voucher terms applicable to the selected service.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools and service modules.

“VAS” or “Value-Added Services” means digital or electronic services, products, vouchers, tokens, payments or benefits made available through the Platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform or purchases, receives or redeems VAS.

“Service Provider” means any third-party provider, network operator, mobile network, electricity provider, municipality, utility provider, voucher issuer, payment provider, biller, merchant, aggregator, content provider or partner responsible for supplying or fulfilling a VAS product.

“VAS Transaction” means any purchase, payment, redemption, recharge, token generation, voucher issue, bill payment, subscription payment, top-up or other transaction relating to VAS.

“Wallet” means the digital wallet facility made available to registered users on the Platform.

2. Types of VAS Services

The Platform may allow users to purchase or access VAS, including but not limited to:

airtime;

mobile data;

SMS bundles;

voice bundles;

electricity tokens;

water or utility payments;

bill payments;

prepaid vouchers;

shopping vouchers;

fuel vouchers;

gaming vouchers;

streaming or entertainment vouchers;

education vouchers;

transport vouchers;

rewards or benefit vouchers;

subscription services;

digital content;

any other VAS introduced on the Platform from time to time.

Not all services will be available to all users, communities, churches, areas or devices.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect provides a digital platform through which users may access and pay for VAS supplied by third-party Service Providers.

Unless expressly stated otherwise in writing, Inkolo Connect is not the actual supplier, network operator, electricity provider, utility provider, municipality, voucher issuer, biller or merchant responsible for the underlying VAS.

The contract for the supply of the VAS is between the user and the relevant Service Provider, network, biller, utility, voucher issuer or merchant, unless expressly stated otherwise.

Inkolo Connect may facilitate:

service display;

user selection;

payment processing;

Wallet deduction;

transaction submission;

token or voucher display;

transaction history;

support routing;

refund or reversal requests where available.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee successful fulfilment by third-party Service Providers.

4. User Responsibility

The user is responsible for ensuring that all information entered for a VAS Transaction is correct before confirming payment.

This includes, where applicable:

mobile number;

network operator;

recipient name;

electricity meter number;

account number;

biller name;

municipality or utility;

voucher type;

voucher value;

product bundle;

subscription type;

beneficiary details;

payment amount.

Once a VAS Transaction is submitted, it may not be reversible, refundable or cancellable.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for losses caused by incorrect information entered by the user.

5. Airtime and Data

When purchasing airtime, data, SMS bundles, voice bundles or mobile products, the user must confirm:

the correct mobile number;

the correct network;

the correct product;

the correct bundle amount;

the correct recipient.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible if the user purchases airtime or data for the wrong number, wrong network or wrong recipient.

Airtime and data products are supplied according to the rules of the relevant mobile network operator.

Expiry dates, bundle rules, rollover rules, usage limits, promotional rules, network coverage and service quality are controlled by the mobile network operator and not by Inkolo Connect.

6. Electricity Tokens and Utility Payments

When purchasing electricity or making utility payments, the user must confirm:

the correct meter number;

the correct municipality or electricity provider;

the correct amount;

the correct tariff type;

the correct property or account details.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible where a user enters the wrong meter number, purchases for the wrong property, selects the wrong provider or pays the wrong account.

Electricity tokens and utility payments are supplied by the relevant municipality, utility provider, electricity provider, token provider, vending provider or aggregator.

Inkolo Connect does not control tariffs, fees, free basic electricity allocation, meter blocking, debt deductions, arrears deductions, municipal system errors or token acceptance by the meter.

The amount of electricity received may be less than the amount paid if the utility provider deducts arrears, service fees, levies, debt recovery, tariff charges or other permitted deductions.

7. Vouchers and Digital Codes

Voucher purchases may result in a digital code, PIN, token, barcode, reference number or redemption instruction being issued.

The user is responsible for keeping voucher codes confidential and secure.

Inkolo Connect is not liable if a voucher code is lost, stolen, shared, redeemed by another person or used incorrectly after it has been delivered to the user.

Voucher use is subject to the voucher issuer’s terms, including expiry dates, redemption locations, exclusions, value limits, product restrictions and refund rules.

Vouchers are generally not exchangeable for cash unless expressly permitted by the voucher issuer.

8. Bill Payments

Where bill payment services are made available, the user is responsible for ensuring that the correct biller, account number, reference number and payment amount are entered.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that the biller will allocate the payment correctly if the user provides incorrect details.

Bill payments may take time to reflect with the biller, and the user remains responsible for avoiding late-payment penalties, disconnections, interest, fees or service interruptions.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for late allocation, disconnection, penalties or account disputes caused by biller delays, incorrect user details or third-party processing delays.

9. Payment and Wallet Deductions

VAS may be paid for using:

the user’s Wallet;

card payment;

bank payment;

QR payment;

any other approved payment method made available on the Platform.

A VAS Transaction may only be processed if payment is successful and sufficient funds are available.

The user authorises Inkolo Connect to deduct the transaction amount, platform fee, service fee, payment fee or any applicable charge from the user’s Wallet or selected payment method.

Payment through the Platform does not guarantee successful delivery of the VAS where fulfilment depends on a third-party Service Provider.

10. Fees and Charges

Inkolo Connect, Payment Partners and Service Providers may charge fees for VAS Transactions.

Fees may include:

platform fees;

service fees;

transaction fees;

payment processing fees;

vending fees;

network fees;

utility fees;

biller fees;

voucher fees;

reversal or refund fees.

Fees may be included in the price or displayed separately before confirmation.

By confirming a VAS Transaction, the user accepts the applicable fees.

11. Transaction Confirmation

A VAS Transaction is considered submitted once the user confirms the transaction and payment is processed.

A transaction confirmation, receipt, reference number or status message does not always mean that the third-party Service Provider has fully completed fulfilment.

Some transactions may reflect as pending, processing, delayed, failed, reversed or disputed.

The Platform transaction history is provided for user convenience and reconciliation but may be subject to correction where technical errors, reversals or provider updates occur.

12. Failed, Delayed or Pending Transactions

VAS Transactions may fail, delay or remain pending due to:

incorrect user details;

network downtime;

mobile operator failure;

utility provider failure;

municipality system issues;

aggregator delays;

Payment Partner delays;

bank delays;

insufficient funds;

fraud checks;

compliance checks;

technical errors;

power outages;

internet failures;

system maintenance;

third-party provider downtime.

Inkolo Connect will make reasonable efforts to display the transaction status and assist with support queries, but Inkolo Connect does not guarantee instant fulfilment.

13. Refunds and Reversals

Refunds or reversals are not automatic.

A refund or reversal may be considered where:

payment was deducted but the transaction failed;

the Service Provider confirms non-fulfilment;

duplicate deduction occurred;

technical error occurred;

the transaction is eligible for reversal under provider rules.

A refund or reversal may not be available where:

the user entered incorrect details;

airtime or data was sent to the wrong number;

electricity was purchased for the wrong meter;

a voucher code was issued;

a voucher was redeemed;

the Service Provider fulfilled the transaction;

the product is non-refundable;

the user changed their mind;

the service provider rules do not allow reversal.

Inkolo Connect is not required to refund users from its own funds where the VAS was fulfilled by a Service Provider or where the error was caused by the user.

Approved refunds may be paid back to the user’s Wallet or original payment method, subject to provider rules, payment partner rules, fraud checks and processing timelines.

14. Incorrect Recipient, Number, Meter or Account

The user accepts full responsibility for checking recipient details before confirming a transaction.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

airtime sent to the wrong number;

data sent to the wrong number;

electricity bought for the wrong meter;

bills paid to the wrong account;

vouchers bought in the wrong amount;

payments made to the wrong biller;

products bought for the wrong network;

user typing errors;

user misunderstanding of product selection.

Inkolo Connect may try to assist where possible, but recovery or reversal is not guaranteed.

15. Product Availability

VAS products may be changed, removed, delayed, suspended or discontinued without notice.

Service availability may depend on:

mobile network availability;

electricity provider availability;

municipality system availability;

voucher issuer stock;

biller participation;

Payment Partner availability;

platform configuration;

user location;

user verification status;

service provider rules.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that every VAS product will always be available.

16. Product Pricing

VAS pricing may change at any time due to Service Provider pricing, network pricing, utility tariffs, voucher issuer pricing, fees, taxes, levies or platform charges.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for changes made by third-party Service Providers.

The price displayed at confirmation is the price applicable to that transaction, unless a technical or pricing error is identified.

Inkolo Connect may cancel, reverse or correct transactions affected by obvious pricing errors, system errors or provider errors.

17. Product Rules and Expiry

VAS products may have specific rules, including:

expiry dates;

usage limits;

network restrictions;

redemption restrictions;

geographic restrictions;

once-off use;

non-transferability;

no cash conversion;

minimum and maximum values;

promotional rules;

service provider terms.

The user is responsible for reading and complying with all product rules.

Inkolo Connect is not liable where a user fails to use a product before expiry or breaches provider rules.

18. Fraud Prevention and Compliance

Inkolo Connect may monitor VAS Transactions for fraud, abuse, suspicious activity, money laundering, chargeback risk, voucher abuse, account takeover, unauthorised card use or other unlawful activity.

Inkolo Connect may:

delay transactions;

block transactions;

reverse transactions;

freeze Wallet access;

request verification;

limit transaction values;

suspend accounts;

report suspicious activity;

refuse service.

Users may not use VAS to launder money, commit fraud, resell unlawfully, abuse promotions, exploit system errors, purchase prohibited products or assist unlawful activity.

19. Transaction Limits

Inkolo Connect, Payment Partners and Service Providers may impose minimum and maximum transaction limits.

Limits may apply per transaction, per day, per month, per user, per Wallet, per product, per network or per risk category.

Limits may be changed at any time for risk, compliance, operational or provider reasons.

20. No Resale Without Permission

Users may not resell VAS products, vouchers, tokens or digital codes unless expressly authorised by Inkolo Connect and the relevant Service Provider.

Unauthorised resale may result in suspension, forfeiture of benefits, reversal of transactions or legal action.

21. No Warranty by Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect does not warrant that:

any VAS will always be available;

any transaction will be instant;

any provider system will be online;

any product will meet the user’s expectations;

network coverage will be available;

electricity meters will accept tokens;

billers will allocate payments immediately;

voucher issuers will have stock;

third-party systems will operate without error.

VAS products are supplied subject to the applicable Service Provider’s terms and system availability.

22. User Support

Users may contact Inkolo Connect support for VAS Transaction queries.

The user may be required to provide:

transaction reference;

date and time;

product selected;

amount paid;

mobile number, meter number, biller reference or voucher reference;

proof of payment;

screenshots;

any other information reasonably required.

Inkolo Connect may need to refer the query to the relevant Service Provider, Payment Partner, network, municipality, utility or voucher issuer.

Resolution times may depend on third-party response times.

23. Service Provider Decisions

The relevant Service Provider may make final decisions regarding:

whether a transaction was fulfilled;

whether a token was generated;

whether airtime or data was delivered;

whether a voucher was issued;

whether a bill was paid;

whether a refund or reversal is allowed;

whether a product expired;

whether a code was redeemed.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for decisions made by Service Providers, networks, utilities, billers or voucher issuers.

24. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

user registration;

VAS purchases;

payment processing;

Wallet deductions;

transaction records;

fraud prevention;

support;

dispute resolution;

compliance;

reporting.

Personal information may be shared with Service Providers, Payment Partners, banks, networks, utilities, municipalities, billers, voucher issuers, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse another person’s mobile number, account number, meter number, identity information or payment information.

25. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee uninterrupted access to VAS.

The service may be unavailable because of:

scheduled maintenance;

emergency maintenance;

technical failures;

bank downtime;

Payment Partner downtime;

Service Provider downtime;

mobile network failure;

utility system failure;

municipality system failure;

internet failure;

power failure;

cyber incidents;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss caused by service interruption, downtime or delayed transactions.

26. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

incorrect mobile numbers;

incorrect meter numbers;

incorrect account numbers;

wrong network selection;

wrong product selection;

failed VAS Transactions;

delayed VAS Transactions;

provider system downtime;

mobile network failures;

electricity provider failures;

municipality failures;

biller allocation delays;

voucher expiry;

voucher theft after delivery;

voucher redemption by another person;

user error;

unauthorised account access;

fraud or scams;

payment reversals;

chargebacks;

Wallet deductions;

third-party provider decisions;

loss of income;

loss of opportunity;

emotional distress or inconvenience.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific VAS Transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

27. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s VAS Transaction;

incorrect details entered by the user;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraud or unlawful use;

unauthorised resale;

misuse of vouchers or tokens;

payment disputes;

chargebacks;

unauthorised card use;

disputes with recipients, networks, utilities, billers, municipalities, voucher issuers or Service Providers.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

28. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to VAS where the user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

commits fraud;

misuses vouchers or tokens;

performs excessive reversals or chargebacks;

uses incorrect or false information;

resells without permission;

creates legal, financial, operational, security, reputational or compliance risk;

violates Service Provider rules;

violates payment rules.

Inkolo Connect may also suspend or discontinue any VAS product or service at any time.

29. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these VAS Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of VAS after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

30. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

31. Acceptance

By using VAS on the Platform, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator;

accept that VAS products are supplied by third-party Service Providers;

accept responsibility for checking all numbers, meter numbers, account numbers, products and amounts before confirming payment;

understand that VAS Transactions may not be reversible once submitted;

accept responsibility for their own use of vouchers, tokens, airtime, data, electricity and bill payments;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from user error, provider failure, incorrect details or misuse of the service.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user purchases any VAS product:

I understand that Inkolo Connect only facilitates the transaction and that the selected product is supplied by the relevant service provider, network, utility, biller or voucher issuer. legalAcceptances.documentSnapshot:
CATCH A RIDE SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Catch a Ride Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, request, offer, accept, provide, pay for, contribute towards, communicate about, cancel, complete or otherwise use the Catch a Ride service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Catch a Ride service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Community Rules, Safety Rules, and any additional policies published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its app, website, dashboards, wallet systems, payment systems, communication tools, location tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community and church member-management service operated through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital application, dashboard, website, wallet, communication tool or service module.

“Catch a Ride” means the platform feature that allows registered members to request, offer, arrange, communicate about or contribute towards rides, lifts, transport assistance or member-to-member travel.

“Driver” means a registered user who offers or agrees to transport another user.

“Passenger” means a registered user who requests, accepts or receives a ride.

“Ride” means a lift, trip, journey, transport arrangement, shared route, pickup, drop-off or travel arrangement between users.

“Free Ride” means a ride where no payment, fee, reward or contribution is requested or paid.

“Cost-Sharing Ride” means a ride where the Passenger contributes towards actual trip-related costs such as fuel, tolls, parking or other reasonable expenses, without the Driver making a profit.

“Paid Ride” means any ride where the Driver receives payment, reward, profit, fee, fare, commission or compensation beyond reasonable cost-sharing.

“User” means any person who uses the Catch a Ride service as a Driver, Passenger or viewer.

2. Purpose of the Catch a Ride Service

The Catch a Ride service is intended to help registered members connect with each other for transport assistance within their community, church, organisation or approved platform network.

The service may be used for:

member-to-member lifts;

shared travel;

community ride coordination;

free lifts between members;

cost-sharing arrangements;

approved paid rides where legally permitted;

transport support for community activities;

approved future mobility services offered through the platform.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the digital connection tool only. They do not own, operate or control the vehicles used by users.

3. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a taxi operator, bus operator, transport company, chauffeur service, vehicle owner, employer, insurer, emergency service, tour operator, public transport operator or transport regulator, unless expressly stated in writing.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee:

that a Driver is available;

that a Passenger will accept a ride;

that a Driver will arrive;

that a Passenger will be ready;

that a vehicle is roadworthy;

that a Driver is licensed;

that a Driver has the required permit;

that a Driver has insurance;

that a ride will be safe;

that a route will be correct;

that the fare or contribution is fair;

that the Driver or Passenger will behave properly;

that the ride will be completed.

Any ride is arranged directly between the Driver and Passenger. Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform facility.

4. Important Legal Notice: Free, Cost-Sharing and Paid Rides

Users must understand the difference between free rides, cost-sharing rides and paid rides.

A Free Ride is a voluntary lift where the Driver receives no payment, reward or contribution.

A Cost-Sharing Ride is a private arrangement where the Passenger contributes only towards actual costs such as fuel, parking or toll fees.

A Paid Ride may be treated as passenger transport for reward and may require licences, permits, public operating authorisation, professional driving permits, insurance and compliance with transport laws.

South African law regulates public transport and e-hailing services, and paid passenger transport may require operating licences. The National Department of Transport has indicated that e-hailing is recognised as a transport service type and that operators must comply with applicable licensing requirements.

Each Driver is solely responsible for ensuring that they comply with all laws, permits, licences, insurance requirements and vehicle requirements before offering any ride, especially any paid ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that any Driver is legally authorised to provide paid transport.

5. No Unlawful Transport Services

Users may not use Catch a Ride to operate an unlawful taxi, e-hailing, shuttle, scholar transport, courier, delivery, tourism, public transport or commercial transport service.

A Driver must not offer paid rides unless the Driver has all legal authorisations required for that type of transport.

Where a Driver transports passengers for payment without the necessary authority, the Driver accepts full responsibility for all legal, financial, insurance, regulatory, criminal and civil consequences.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, block or remove any Driver or Passenger if the platform suspects unlawful transport activity.

6. User Eligibility

To use Catch a Ride, a user must:

be registered on the Duranki platform;

provide accurate personal information;

pass any required verification checks;

comply with platform rules;

comply with community rules;

comply with South African law;

not be suspended or banned from the service.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require identity verification, selfie verification, document uploads, vehicle documents, licence copies, insurance confirmation, proof of address or additional checks before allowing a user to act as a Driver.

7. Driver Requirements

A Driver must:

hold a valid driver’s licence for the vehicle used;

be legally entitled to drive in South Africa;

ensure the vehicle is licensed and roadworthy;

ensure the vehicle has valid licence discs and registration;

ensure the vehicle is safe and mechanically sound;

ensure the vehicle has enough fuel or charge for the trip;

comply with all road traffic laws;

comply with speed limits;

not drive under the influence of alcohol, drugs, medication or fatigue;

not overload the vehicle;

not transport more passengers than the vehicle is legally allowed to carry;

ensure all passengers use seatbelts where available;

treat passengers respectfully;

not harass, threaten, abuse or endanger passengers;

ensure they have suitable insurance cover;

ensure they hold any required permit, public operating licence or professional driving permit where applicable.

Drivers must check their motor insurance because carrying fare-paying passengers or driving for reward may affect insurance cover. South African insurance guidance commonly warns that lift clubs, paid passengers or commercial use may affect claims if not disclosed to the insurer.

8. Passenger Responsibilities

A Passenger must:

provide accurate pickup and drop-off information;

be ready at the agreed pickup time;

behave respectfully;

wear a seatbelt where available;

not damage the vehicle;

not distract the Driver;

not carry unlawful, dangerous or prohibited items;

not harass, threaten, abuse or endanger the Driver;

not request unsafe driving;

not request unlawful stops, routes or conduct;

pay any agreed lawful contribution or fee through the approved platform method where required;

check that the vehicle and Driver match the platform information before entering the vehicle.

Passengers use rides at their own risk and must apply reasonable personal safety precautions.

9. Vehicle Safety

Drivers are solely responsible for the safety, roadworthiness, maintenance and legal compliance of their vehicles.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not inspect vehicles unless expressly stated in writing.

Drivers must not use vehicles with:

faulty brakes;

defective tyres;

broken lights;

unsafe doors;

cracked windscreens affecting visibility;

steering defects;

fuel leaks;

engine problems that create danger;

expired licence discs;

known mechanical faults;

any condition that makes the vehicle unsafe or unlawful.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may request vehicle photos or documents, but this does not make them responsible for verifying safety or roadworthiness.

10. Driver Availability and Live Map

The platform may allow Drivers to switch availability on or off and may show available Drivers on a live map.

The live map, location display, estimated distance, estimated arrival time or route information may be inaccurate, delayed or unavailable.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that:

the Driver’s location is accurate;

the Driver is still available;

the Driver will accept a request;

the Driver will arrive at the estimated time;

the route is correct;

the ride will be completed.

Users must not rely on Catch a Ride for emergencies, urgent medical transport or critical transport needs.

11. Ride Requests and Acceptance

A Passenger may request a ride from an available Driver.

A Driver may accept or reject a ride request.

A ride is not confirmed until the Driver accepts and the platform or the parties confirm the arrangement.

Drivers and Passengers may cancel rides, but repeated cancellations, misuse or abuse may result in restrictions or suspension.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for missed appointments, missed work, missed flights, missed events, loss of income or inconvenience caused by failed or cancelled rides.

12. Payments, Contributions and Wallet Use

Where Catch a Ride allows payment, contribution or wallet transfer, users must use the approved platform payment method unless otherwise permitted.

Payments may include:

fuel contribution;

toll contribution;

parking contribution;

agreed ride fee;

platform service fee;

wallet fee;

payment-processing fee;

cancellation fee, if applicable.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may charge platform fees, deduct service fees, delay settlement, hold funds, reverse payments or block wallet activity where fraud, disputes, chargebacks, unlawful activity or risk is suspected.

Payment through the platform does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees the Driver, Passenger, vehicle, ride, safety, legal compliance or successful completion of the trip.

13. No Cash Payments Where Platform Payment Is Required

Where the platform requires wallet or in-app payment, users may not bypass the platform by arranging cash, EFT or outside payment.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for any loss, fraud, robbery, dispute or non-payment resulting from payment outside the platform.

Users who bypass platform payments may be suspended or permanently banned.

14. Insurance

Drivers are solely responsible for ensuring that their insurance policy covers the way they use their vehicle.

This includes cover for:

private use;

lift club or cost-sharing use;

fare-paying passengers;

commercial use;

public transport use;

e-hailing use;

passenger liability;

third-party liability;

accident damage;

theft or hijacking.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide vehicle insurance, passenger insurance, driver insurance, accident cover or public liability cover unless expressly stated in writing.

A Passenger should not assume that a Driver is insured.

A Driver should not assume that their normal private vehicle insurance covers paid or contribution-based rides.

15. Accidents, Injury, Death and Emergency Events

Users understand and accept that transport carries inherent risks, including accidents, injury, death, crime, hijacking, assault, theft, vehicle failure, unsafe driving, road hazards and third-party conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for injury, death, loss, damage, trauma, medical costs, legal costs, repair costs or any other claim arising from a ride, except where liability cannot lawfully be excluded.

In the event of an emergency, users must contact emergency services, police, medical assistance or roadside assistance directly.

Catch a Ride is not an emergency response service.

16. Safety Rules

Drivers and Passengers must comply with the following safety rules:

do not drive or ride if you feel unsafe;

check the Driver, Passenger and vehicle details before the ride;

use the in-app communication tools where possible;

do not share unnecessary private information;

do not carry weapons or unlawful items;

do not use abusive, sexual, threatening or discriminatory language;

do not request unsafe driving;

do not overload the vehicle;

do not transport intoxicated or dangerous passengers where this creates risk;

do not transport minors without proper consent;

do not accept rides that appear suspicious;

report unsafe behaviour immediately.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend any user who breaches safety rules.

17. Minors and Children

Minors may not request, offer, accept or provide rides without the involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian.

Drivers may not transport children alone unless:

the child’s parent or guardian has authorised the ride;

the Driver is legally permitted to do so;

the arrangement complies with all platform rules;

the Driver complies with any child-safety requirements;

the platform permits that type of ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may prohibit child transport, scholar transport or minor-related rides unless additional verification, consent and compliance processes are completed.

18. Prohibited Conduct

Users may not use Catch a Ride to:

commit fraud;

rob, assault, threaten or harass another user;

transport illegal goods;

transport drugs, weapons or stolen property;

transport passengers unlawfully for reward;

bypass transport laws;

impersonate another person;

use a fake profile;

use a vehicle that does not match the profile;

use another person’s driver account;

create false ride requests;

submit fake complaints;

submit fake payment proof;

evade platform fees;

misuse location data;

stalk or track another user;

discriminate against users unlawfully;

damage the reputation of Duranki, Inkolo Connect or any community.

19. Ratings and Reviews

The platform may allow Drivers and Passengers to rate each other.

Ratings help improve community safety, but they are not a guarantee that a user is safe, honest, reliable or lawful.

Users must provide honest and fair ratings.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove false, abusive, defamatory or manipulated reviews.

Repeated poor ratings may result in account restrictions or suspension.

20. Background Checks and Verification

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require identity checks, document checks, vehicle checks or criminal-record-related checks where legally permitted.

However, verification does not guarantee that a user is safe, trustworthy, insured, qualified or legally compliant.

Users remain responsible for their own safety decisions.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse Driver access if verification is incomplete, expired, inconsistent, suspicious or failed.

21. Location Data

Catch a Ride may use GPS, live location, route data and trip information.

Users agree that location data may be processed for:

ride matching;

pickup and drop-off;

route display;

safety;

fraud prevention;

dispute resolution;

service improvement;

compliance;

support.

Location data may be inaccurate due to signal issues, phone settings, network delays or device failure.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by inaccurate location data.

22. Privacy and Communication

Users may communicate through in-app chat, phone, notifications or other platform tools.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may monitor, store, review or disclose ride-related communications and data where necessary for safety, fraud prevention, legal compliance, dispute handling or enforcement of these Terms.

Personal information will be processed in line with the platform Privacy Policy and applicable data-protection laws.

23. Community and Church-Based Use

Where Catch a Ride is used within a church, community or Inkolo Connect environment, the service is intended to support trusted member-to-member assistance.

However, being part of the same church, branch, community or organisation does not guarantee that a Driver or Passenger is safe, honest, reliable, insured or legally compliant.

Churches, pastors, bishops, KZNCC, community leaders and administrators are not responsible for rides unless they personally provide, arrange or control the specific ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may allow community administrators to view certain reporting information, but this does not make them liable for the conduct of users.

24. Disputes

Drivers and Passengers must first try to resolve disputes directly and respectfully.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing:

ride records;

payment records;

cancellation history;

GPS logs;

chat messages;

complaints;

ratings;

uploaded evidence.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a court, arbitrator, insurer, transport regulator, police service or debt collector.

Any platform decision to refund, reverse, suspend, block or release funds is a risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

25. Service Suspension

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate access to Catch a Ride at any time where a user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

drives unlawfully;

drives unsafely;

receives serious complaints;

is suspected of fraud;

is suspected of criminal conduct;

misuses the wallet;

bypasses platform payments;

creates legal, safety, reputational or financial risk;

provides false documents;

uses another person’s account or vehicle.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may also suspend the entire Catch a Ride service, or certain functions, where required for compliance, safety, legal or operational reasons.

26. No Employment Relationship

Drivers are independent users.

Nothing in these Terms creates an employment relationship, agency relationship, partnership, joint venture or representative relationship between Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any Driver or Passenger.

Drivers are not employees of Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Drivers are responsible for their own taxes, permits, licences, expenses, insurance, vehicle maintenance and legal obligations.

27. No Warranty

The Catch a Ride service is provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not warrant that:

the service will always be available;

the service will be error-free;

the map will be accurate;

the Driver will arrive;

the Passenger will pay;

the vehicle will be safe;

the ride will be lawful;

the ride will be comfortable;

the ride will be completed;

disputes will be resolved in a user’s favour.

28. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates are not liable for any claim, loss, damage, injury, death, cost or expense arising from:

any ride;

road accidents;

reckless or negligent driving;

unsafe vehicles;

unlawful transport;

lack of insurance;

failed pickups;

late arrivals;

cancelled rides;

non-payment;

payment disputes;

passenger behaviour;

driver behaviour;

theft, robbery, hijacking or assault;

property damage;

injury or death;

inaccurate GPS or location data;

platform downtime;

third-party provider failures;

breach of law by a user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of business, missed appointments, missed flights, emotional distress or reputational harm.

Where liability cannot legally be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fee actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific ride giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

29. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of Catch a Ride;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

the user’s driving;

the user’s passenger conduct;

an accident;

injury or death;

lack of licence, permit or insurance;

unlawful paid transport;

fraud or criminal conduct;

misuse of personal information;

disputes with another user;

claims by passengers, drivers, insurers, regulators, law enforcement or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

30. Reporting Safety Issues

Users must immediately report serious issues, including:

unsafe driving;

harassment;

assault;

theft;

fraud;

fake profiles;

vehicle mismatch;

intoxicated driving;

unlawful paid transport;

child-safety concerns;

dangerous conduct.

Where a crime, accident or emergency occurs, users must contact SAPS, emergency medical services or the relevant authority directly.

Reporting to Duranki or Inkolo Connect is not a substitute for reporting to law enforcement or emergency services.

31. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform or sent by notification.

Continued use of Catch a Ride after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

32. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

33. Acceptance

By using Catch a Ride, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect are only platform facilitators;

accept that rides are arranged directly between users;

accept that transport carries safety and legal risks;

accept responsibility for their own conduct, legal compliance and safety decisions.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before requesting or offering a ride:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Catch a Ride Terms and Conditions. I understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform connection tool and are not responsible for the driver, passenger, vehicle, route, safety, insurance, permits, payment disputes, accidents, injury, loss or damage arising from any ride.

Suggested Driver Checkbox Text

Before a user switches their driver availability on:

I confirm that I have a valid driver’s licence, that my vehicle is roadworthy and legally licensed, that I will comply with all traffic and transport laws, and that I am responsible for my own insurance, permits and legal compliance. I understand that paid transport may require additional licences or authorisations.

Suggested Passenger Safety Warning

On the ride request screen:

For your safety, confirm the driver, vehicle and registration number before entering the vehicle. Do not enter if anything feels unsafe. Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not operate the vehicle and do not guarantee the driver, passenger, vehicle or ride.

KEYTCHA PROPERTIES TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Keytcha Properties Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, browse, list, advertise, rent, let, buy, sell, enquire about, apply for, view, pay for, communicate about or otherwise use the Keytcha Properties service made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By using Keytcha Properties, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Acceptable Use Policy, Community Terms and any additional service-specific terms, property listing terms, lease terms, sale agreement terms, agent terms or third-party provider terms applicable to the transaction.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools and service modules.

“Keytcha Properties” or “Keytcha” means the property service module on the Platform that allows users to list, browse, enquire about, apply for, rent, let, buy, sell or communicate about properties.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses Keytcha Properties, including property owners, landlords, tenants, buyers, sellers, estate agents, property managers, applicants, occupants, viewers and service providers.

“Lister” means any user who uploads, advertises, lists or promotes a property on Keytcha Properties.

“Landlord” means the owner or authorised person who offers a property for rental.

“Tenant” means the person who rents, applies to rent or occupies a property.

“Seller” means the owner or authorised person who offers a property for sale.

“Buyer” means the person who enquires about, offers to buy or buys a property.

“Agent” means an estate agent, letting agent, property practitioner, broker, property manager or authorised representative involved in a property transaction.

“Property Listing” means any advertisement, description, photo, video, price, rental amount, sale price, location, property condition, availability, rule, feature, document or related content uploaded to Keytcha Properties.

“Property Transaction” means any rental, sale, purchase, lease, viewing, deposit payment, application, offer, agreement, negotiation, occupation, cancellation, refund or related property arrangement.

2. Purpose of Keytcha Properties

Keytcha Properties is a digital property listing and connection service.

The service may allow users to:

list properties for rent;

list properties for sale;

search for rental accommodation;

search for properties to buy;

enquire about properties;

communicate with landlords, sellers, agents or property managers;

arrange viewings;

submit rental applications;

submit property documents;

make approved payments through the Platform;

access property-related services;

manage property-related communications.

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform and technology tools only.

Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect is not the landlord, seller, estate agent, property practitioner, property manager, conveyancer, attorney, landlord representative, tenant representative, buyer representative or guarantor of any property transaction.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect acts as a platform facilitator.

Inkolo Connect may assist by:

displaying Property Listings;

allowing users to search for properties;

allowing users to communicate through the Platform;

facilitating document uploads;

facilitating payment requests where available;

displaying transaction history;

routing support queries;

providing digital dashboard tools.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee:

that any property exists;

that any property is available;

that any property is legally owned by the Lister;

that the Lister is authorised;

that a landlord will accept a tenant;

that a seller will accept an offer;

that a buyer will qualify for finance;

that a tenant will pay rent;

that a property is safe;

that a property is free from defects;

that a property complies with laws;

that a listing is accurate;

that any transaction will be concluded.

Users use Keytcha Properties at their own risk.

4. No Estate Agency or Property Practitioner Service Unless Expressly Stated

Unless expressly confirmed in a separate written agreement, Inkolo Connect does not act as an estate agent, property practitioner, rental agent, selling agent, mandate holder, broker, conveyancer, attorney or property manager.

Inkolo Connect does not provide property valuation, legal advice, conveyancing advice, rental advice, investment advice, tax advice or financial advice.

Any estate agency, property practitioner, legal, conveyancing, bond, inspection, valuation or property management service is provided by the relevant third-party provider, not by Inkolo Connect.

Where an Agent lists or manages a property through Keytcha, that Agent remains responsible for their own licensing, registration, mandate, compliance, trust account obligations and professional duties.

5. User Eligibility

To use Keytcha Properties, a user may be required to:

register on the Platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide accurate contact details;

accept these Terms;

pass fraud and compliance checks;

provide property documents where requested;

provide proof of authority to list a property where requested.

Inkolo Connect may refuse, suspend, restrict or terminate access where a user fails verification, provides false information, uploads suspicious listings, commits fraud, creates risk or breaches these Terms.

6. Property Listing Responsibility

The Lister is fully responsible for each Property Listing.

The Lister must ensure that the listing is:

true;

accurate;

lawful;

not misleading;

current;

not fraudulent;

authorised by the property owner;

properly priced;

correctly located;

clear about property condition;

clear about availability;

clear about rental or sale terms;

clear about deposits, fees or costs;

clear about rules, restrictions or exclusions.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for false, outdated, inaccurate, misleading or unauthorised Property Listings.

7. Proof of Ownership or Authority

Inkolo Connect may request proof that a Lister has the right to list a property.

Proof may include:

title deed;

municipal account;

lease mandate;

agent mandate;

owner authorisation;

company resolution;

identity document;

proof of address;

property management agreement;

any other document reasonably required.

Inkolo Connect may remove or suspend a listing if proof is not provided or if authority is disputed.

Requesting proof does not mean Inkolo Connect guarantees ownership, authority or legality of the listing.

8. Prohibited Property Listings

Users may not list properties that:

they do not own or have authority to list;

are part of a scam;

are falsely advertised;

are not available;

are listed with fake photos;

use stolen images;

misrepresent location;

misrepresent price;

misrepresent condition;

involve illegal occupation;

involve unlawful discrimination;

involve unsafe or illegal accommodation;

involve overcrowding in breach of law;

involve illegal subletting;

involve illegal deposits or fees;

are prohibited by law, court order or contract;

create legal, safety, reputational or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect may remove prohibited listings and suspend or ban users involved in fraudulent or unlawful listings.

9. Rental Listings

Where a property is listed for rental, the Lister or Landlord must clearly disclose:

rental amount;

deposit amount;

lease period;

occupation date;

included and excluded costs;

water and electricity arrangements;

municipal charges;

body corporate or estate rules;

pet rules;

parking availability;

number of occupants allowed;

furnished or unfurnished status;

inspection requirements;

cancellation rules;

any other important rental condition.

The lease agreement is concluded between the Landlord and Tenant.

Inkolo Connect is not a party to the lease unless expressly stated in writing.

10. Sale Listings

Where a property is listed for sale, the Lister or Seller must clearly disclose:

sale price;

property description;

property size;

erf or unit details;

ownership type;

rates and taxes;

levies;

known defects;

occupation terms;

agent details, where applicable;

whether transfer duty or VAT applies, where known;

any special conditions;

whether the listing is subject to a mandate.

The sale agreement is concluded between the Seller and Buyer.

Inkolo Connect is not a party to the sale agreement unless expressly stated in writing.

11. Accommodation and Short-Term Stays

Where Keytcha allows short-term accommodation, shared accommodation, room rentals, student accommodation or temporary stays, the Lister must clearly disclose:

stay period;

check-in and check-out rules;

house rules;

deposit;

cleaning fees;

cancellation rules;

refund rules;

number of occupants allowed;

shared facilities;

safety rules;

access rules;

any restrictions.

Inkolo Connect does not inspect or guarantee accommodation safety, suitability, legality, cleanliness or availability.

12. Tenant and Applicant Responsibility

Tenants and applicants are responsible for checking all property details before applying, paying or signing.

A Tenant or applicant must:

inspect the property where possible;

confirm the Landlord or Agent’s authority;

read the lease agreement;

understand rental, deposit and fee obligations;

ask about utilities and extra costs;

check rules and restrictions;

ensure the property suits their needs;

provide truthful application information;

not submit fake documents;

not occupy a property unlawfully.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Tenant will be accepted or that any rental application will succeed.

13. Buyer Responsibility

Buyers are responsible for conducting their own checks before making an offer or buying.

A Buyer should verify:

ownership;

title deed;

property condition;

defects;

zoning;

municipal accounts;

rates and taxes;

levies;

body corporate rules;

building plans;

occupation rights;

bond approval;

transfer costs;

attorney and conveyancer details.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee property ownership, title, condition, value, finance approval or successful transfer.

14. Viewings

Users may arrange property viewings through the Platform.

Users attend viewings at their own risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

cancelled viewings;

no-shows;

unsafe viewing locations;

theft;

assault;

harassment;

property damage;

personal injury;

travel costs;

loss of time;

incorrect viewing information.

Users should take reasonable safety precautions when attending or hosting viewings.

15. Deposits

Deposits may be required for rentals, accommodation or other property transactions.

Unless expressly stated in writing, deposits are paid to the Landlord, Agent, property manager, accommodation provider or relevant Service Provider, not to Inkolo Connect.

If the Platform facilitates deposit payment, Inkolo Connect acts only as a payment facilitator.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for deposit refunds, deposit disputes, deductions, damages, unpaid rent, breach of lease or disagreements about property condition.

Deposit handling must comply with the lease agreement, applicable law and the relevant Landlord, Agent or Service Provider terms.

16. Rent and Other Payments

Where rent or property-related payments are made through the Platform, users must check all details before payment.

Inkolo Connect may facilitate payment, wallet deduction, payment request, receipt display or transaction history.

Payment facilitation does not make Inkolo Connect the Landlord, Agent, property manager, collection agent or guarantor.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

unpaid rent;

late rent;

incorrect payment references;

payments to wrong recipients;

payment disputes;

eviction disputes;

lease breaches;

deposit disputes;

rent increases;

service charge disputes.

17. Refunds and Cancellations

Refunds and cancellations are governed by the relevant lease agreement, sale agreement, booking agreement, service provider terms or applicable law.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee refunds.

Inkolo Connect is not required to refund users from its own funds where the dispute is between a Landlord, Tenant, Buyer, Seller, Agent or Service Provider.

Where a payment was made through the Platform, Inkolo Connect may assist with transaction records or support routing, but refund decisions remain subject to the relevant agreement and provider rules.

18. Property Condition and Defects

The Lister must disclose known defects, risks and important property conditions.

Buyers and Tenants must inspect the property and ask questions before signing or paying.

Inkolo Connect does not inspect properties and does not guarantee that a property is:

safe;

clean;

habitable;

defect-free;

structurally sound;

legally compliant;

accurately described;

suitable for the user’s needs;

free from damp, leaks, pests or damage;

approved for occupation.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for property defects, hidden defects, maintenance issues, repairs, damages, health risks or habitability disputes.

19. Utilities, Levies and Municipal Charges

Users are responsible for understanding who pays for:

water;

electricity;

refuse;

sewerage;

rates;

levies;

internet;

security;

maintenance;

prepaid meter charges;

arrears;

municipal accounts.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for utility disconnections, incorrect billing, arrears, municipal disputes, levy disputes, meter disputes or service interruptions.

20. Body Corporate, Estate and Community Rules

Properties may be subject to body corporate, estate, homeowners association, landlord, municipality or community rules.

The Lister must disclose important rules where known.

The Tenant or Buyer is responsible for reviewing and complying with applicable rules.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for disputes about estate rules, body corporate rules, conduct rules, pet rules, parking rules, access rules, fines or restrictions.

21. Agents and Property Practitioners

Agents, property practitioners and property managers using Keytcha are responsible for:

holding required registrations;

acting under a valid mandate;

complying with applicable property law;

complying with trust account requirements;

disclosing fees;

protecting client funds;

providing accurate information;

complying with FICA and other verification duties;

handling offers, leases and sale agreements correctly.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for Agent misconduct, negligence, misrepresentation, unauthorised mandates, trust account issues or commission disputes.

22. Documents and Agreements

The Platform may allow users to upload, view or share documents, including:

lease agreements;

sale agreements;

identity documents;

proof of income;

bank statements;

proof of residence;

property mandates;

inspection reports;

deposit receipts;

municipal accounts;

title deeds;

rules or policies.

Users must only upload lawful, accurate and necessary documents.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee the legal validity, fairness, completeness or enforceability of any lease agreement, sale agreement, mandate or other document exchanged through the Platform.

Users should obtain legal advice before signing property agreements where necessary.

23. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

user registration;

property enquiries;

rental applications;

sale enquiries;

identity verification;

fraud prevention;

payment processing;

document uploads;

communication;

support;

dispute handling;

compliance.

Personal information may be shared with Landlords, Agents, Sellers, Buyers, Tenants, Service Providers, Payment Partners, verification providers, attorneys, conveyancers, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information obtained through Keytcha Properties.

24. Fraud Prevention

Users may not use Keytcha Properties for:

fake listings;

fake deposits;

rental scams;

false documents;

identity theft;

stolen property documents;

unauthorised listings;

deposit fraud;

phishing;

money laundering;

fake payment confirmations;

unlawful occupation;

illegal subletting.

Inkolo Connect may monitor, block, remove, suspend, report or restrict users, listings or payments where fraud or suspicious activity is suspected.

25. Ratings and Reviews

The Platform may allow users to rate or review properties, Landlords, Tenants, Agents, Sellers, Buyers or Service Providers.

Users must provide honest, fair and lawful reviews.

Users may not post false, defamatory, abusive, discriminatory or misleading reviews.

Inkolo Connect may remove or moderate reviews at its discretion.

A rating or review does not mean Inkolo Connect endorses or guarantees that user, property or Service Provider.

26. Communication and Chat

The Platform may allow users to communicate through chat, call, notification or message tools.

Users must not:

harass others;

threaten others;

send abusive content;

send discriminatory content;

send sexual or inappropriate content;

send spam;

send fake offers;

send fake payment proof;

request illegal payments;

misuse personal information.

Inkolo Connect may monitor, store, review or disclose communications where necessary for safety, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, legal compliance or enforcement of these Terms.

27. Disputes Between Users

Users must first attempt to resolve disputes directly and in good faith.

Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing:

listing information;

payment records;

chat records;

uploaded documents;

transaction references;

user complaints.

Inkolo Connect is not a court, arbitrator, mediator, attorney, conveyancer, estate agent, property practitioner, debt collector, landlord, tenant representative, buyer representative or seller representative.

Any dispute relating to rent, deposits, eviction, occupation, property defects, sale agreements, lease agreements, transfer, commissions or property ownership must be handled between the relevant parties and through the appropriate legal process.

28. Platform Moderation Rights

Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove listings;

suspend users;

restrict payments;

request verification;

request proof of ownership or authority;

block suspicious listings;

preserve records;

report fraud;

restrict chat;

hide disputed properties;

suspend Keytcha access.

These rights may be exercised where Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, security, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss of rental income, sale opportunity, commission, reputation or business caused by moderation actions taken in good faith.

29. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that Keytcha Properties will always be available, uninterrupted, accurate, secure or error-free.

The service may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

network failures;

payment partner failures;

cyber incidents;

power failure;

third-party provider failure;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed property opportunities, failed enquiries, delayed messages, lost applications or transaction delays caused by downtime or technical issues.

30. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, cost or liability arising from:

false Property Listings;

fake listings;

unauthorised listings;

rental scams;

deposit scams;

property defects;

hidden defects;

unsafe properties;

failed rentals;

failed sales;

failed transfers;

failed bond approvals;

unpaid rent;

unpaid deposits;

deposit disputes;

eviction disputes;

ownership disputes;

viewing incidents;

user misconduct;

Agent misconduct;

landlord or tenant disputes;

buyer or seller disputes;

incorrect property information;

property damage;

personal injury at a property;

municipal, levy or utility disputes;

delays caused by attorneys, agents, banks or conveyancers;

platform downtime;

third-party provider failures.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of profit, loss of commission, loss of opportunity, reputational harm or emotional distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific Keytcha transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

31. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of Keytcha Properties;

the user’s Property Listing;

false or misleading property information;

unauthorised property listings;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraudulent property activity;

deposit disputes;

rental disputes;

sale disputes;

lease disputes;

ownership disputes;

eviction disputes;

personal injury or property damage;

misuse of another person’s personal information;

disputes with Landlords, Tenants, Buyers, Sellers, Agents, property managers, attorneys, conveyancers or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

32. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to Keytcha Properties where the user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

provides false information;

uploads fake listings;

lists property without authority;

commits fraud;

misuses deposits or payments;

harasses other users;

uploads unlawful content;

creates legal, financial, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect may also suspend or discontinue Keytcha Properties, or any part of it, at any time.

33. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these Keytcha Properties Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of Keytcha Properties after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

34. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

35. Acceptance

By using Keytcha Properties, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator only;

accept that Inkolo Connect is not the landlord, seller, estate agent, attorney, conveyancer, property manager or guarantor;

accept responsibility for checking property details, documents, ownership, authority, agreements, payments and risks before proceeding;

accept that property transactions are concluded directly between the relevant users or third-party providers;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from property listings, disputes, payments, deposits, defects, viewings, rentals, sales or user conduct.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user lists, applies for, rents or buys through Keytcha Properties:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Keytcha Properties Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect only provides the digital property platform and is not the landlord, seller, estate agent, attorney, conveyancer, property manager or guarantor. I accept responsibility for checking all property details, documents, ownership, authority, payments and agreements before proceeding.

Suggested Listing Confirmation Text

Before a user lists a property:

I confirm that I am the owner or have written authority to list this property. I confirm that all information, photos, pricing, availability and conditions are true and accurate. I accept full responsibility for this listing and any rental, sale or dispute arising from it.

Suggested Rental Payment / Deposit Warning

Before a user pays a deposit or rental amount:

Please confirm that the landlord, agent, property, amount and payment details are correct. Inkolo Connect facilitates the payment only and is not responsible for lease disputes, deposit refunds, property defects, cancelled viewings, occupation disputes or rental disagreements.

Suggested Viewing Safety Notice

Before a viewing is confirmed:

Property viewings are arranged directly between users. Please take reasonable safety precautions and confirm the identity of the person you are meeting. Inkolo Connect is not responsible for accidents, theft, harassment, injury, cancelled viewings or unsafe viewing arrangements.
