WALLET SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Wallet Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, activate, top up, receive, transfer, send, spend, cash out or otherwise use the Wallet service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Wallet service, the user agrees to be bound by these Wallet Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Community Rules, Service Terms, Payment Terms, and any additional rules, limits, fees, notices or instructions published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet systems, payment tools, communication tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community, church and member-management platform operated on or through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital service, dashboard, app, website, wallet, payment channel, communication channel or service module.

“Wallet” means the digital wallet facility made available to registered users through the platform.

“Wallet Balance” means the value reflected in a user’s Wallet, subject to successful funding, transaction processing, fraud checks, reversals, fees, refunds, holds, limits and compliance reviews.

“User” means any person who registers for, accesses or uses the Wallet.

“Member-to-Member Transfer” means a transfer of available Wallet funds from one registered member’s Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

“Top-Up” means adding money to a Wallet using an approved payment method, including bank card payment where available.

“Cash-Out” means a request by a user to withdraw available Wallet funds from the Wallet to a bank account registered or nominated by that user.

“Payment Partner” means any bank, card acquirer, payment service provider, payment gateway, wallet provider, settlement provider, banking partner, compliance provider or other third-party payment processor used by Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

“Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or official South African public holiday.

2. Nature of the Wallet Service

The Wallet is a digital payment and value-transfer feature made available through the platform.

The Wallet may allow users to:

receive funds into their Wallet;

send funds from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

pay for approved goods or services on the platform;

receive payment from other members;

top up their Wallet using a bank card or other approved payment method;

request a cash-out to their bank account;

view Wallet transaction history;

use Wallet funds for approved platform services.

The Wallet is provided as a platform payment tool and not as a bank account, savings account, deposit account, credit facility, investment product or interest-bearing account.

No interest will be paid to users on Wallet balances unless expressly stated in writing.

3. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the Wallet as part of the platform’s digital service environment.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may use third-party Payment Partners to process card payments, Wallet transactions, settlements, reversals, cash-outs, refunds, fraud screening and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for delays, failures, reversals, declines, chargebacks, bank errors, card network issues, payment gateway failures or third-party processing problems outside their reasonable control.

Where required, Wallet services may be provided through, supported by or settled through approved Payment Partners.

4. Wallet Activation

To activate or use the Wallet, a user may be required to:

register on the platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide personal information;

accept these Wallet Terms;

pass fraud and compliance checks;

provide bank account details for cash-outs;

provide any additional documents requested by Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse, delay, suspend or deactivate Wallet access where verification is incomplete, failed, suspicious or required by law, risk policy or a Payment Partner.

5. Member-to-Member Wallet Transfers

The Wallet may allow a user to send money from their Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

The sender is responsible for ensuring that:

the recipient is the correct person;

the amount is correct;

the reason for the transfer is lawful;

the recipient details are correct;

they have sufficient available Wallet balance;

the transaction is not fraudulent, unlawful or prohibited.

Once a Member-to-Member Transfer has been submitted, it may not be reversible unless Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner determines that a reversal is permitted, required or necessary due to fraud, error, dispute, chargeback, system failure, compliance review or applicable law.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee recovery of funds sent to the wrong recipient.

Users must not use Member-to-Member Transfers for illegal activity, fraud, scams, gambling, money laundering, prohibited goods, unauthorised business activity or any other unlawful purpose.

6. Top-Up Using Bank Card

Users may be able to top up their Wallet using a bank card, subject to availability and approval by the Payment Partner.

A Top-Up may be declined, delayed, reversed or blocked where:

the card is declined by the issuing bank;

the card details are incorrect;

the card is expired;

there are insufficient funds;

3D Secure or OTP verification fails;

the transaction is flagged as suspicious;

the transaction exceeds platform limits;

the transaction breaches Payment Partner rules;

the cardholder disputes the transaction;

the transaction is subject to chargeback;

Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner suspects fraud or unauthorised use.

A Wallet balance may only be credited once the Top-Up has been successfully authorised and processed.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may reverse or debit a Wallet balance if a card Top-Up is later reversed, charged back, rejected, refunded, identified as fraudulent or not finally settled.

Card payments may be subject to chargeback rules imposed by card schemes, banks and card issuers. A chargeback may result in a reversal or deduction from the user’s Wallet balance, even after the Wallet was initially credited.

7. Cash-Out to Bank Account

Users may request to cash out available Wallet funds to their own bank account, subject to platform rules, verification, limits, fraud checks, compliance checks and Payment Partner processing.

A Cash-Out may only be made to a bank account accepted by the platform and/or Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require the bank account to be in the same name as the registered Wallet user.

The user is responsible for ensuring that the bank account details provided are complete, correct and active.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for loss, delay or failed payment caused by incorrect bank account details supplied by the user.

8. Cash-Out Waiting Period

Cash-Out requests are subject to a waiting period of up to 5 business days from the date the Cash-Out request is approved for processing.

The 5-business-day period is a standard waiting period and may be used for:

fraud checks;

card chargeback risk checks;

payment settlement confirmation;

bank processing;

Payment Partner processing;

compliance checks;

identity verification;

anti-money-laundering controls;

dispute checks;

system and reconciliation checks.

The 5-business-day period does not include Saturdays, Sundays or South African public holidays.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may extend the Cash-Out period where:

fraud is suspected;

the user fails verification;

bank details are incorrect;

the Payment Partner requires additional checks;

a dispute has been raised;

a chargeback risk exists;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the user’s Wallet is restricted;

the user’s account is under review;

delays occur outside the control of Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that every Cash-Out will be completed within 5 business days where additional verification, fraud checks, compliance checks or third-party delays apply.

9. Available Balance

A user may only spend, transfer or cash out the available Wallet balance.

The available Wallet balance may be different from the displayed balance where funds are:

pending;

under review;

subject to settlement;

subject to chargeback risk;

frozen;

reversed;

refunded;

disputed;

subject to fees;

subject to compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may correct Wallet balances where an error, duplicate credit, failed payment, reversal, chargeback, refund or system issue occurred.

10. Fees

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may charge fees for Wallet services.

Fees may apply to:

Wallet Top-Ups;

Member-to-Member Transfers;

Cash-Outs;

failed Cash-Outs;

reversed transactions;

card processing;

refunds;

chargebacks;

account verification;

platform service use;

any other Wallet-related service.

Applicable fees may be displayed in the app, dashboard, pricing schedule or transaction screen.

By confirming a transaction, the user agrees to pay the applicable fee.

Fees may be deducted from the Wallet balance or transaction amount before settlement.

11. Transaction Limits

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may apply limits to Wallet use.

Limits may include:

minimum Top-Up amount;

maximum Top-Up amount;

daily transaction limit;

monthly transaction limit;

maximum Wallet balance;

maximum Cash-Out amount;

minimum Cash-Out amount;

Member-to-Member Transfer limits;

card payment limits;

risk-based limits.

Limits may differ depending on verification level, user type, risk profile, community, service, Payment Partner requirements or regulatory requirements.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may change Wallet limits at any time.

12. No Credit, Loans or Overdraft

The Wallet does not provide credit, loans, overdraft or advance facilities unless a separate approved service is expressly provided under separate terms.

Users may not spend more than their available Wallet balance.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse transactions where there are insufficient available funds.

13. Payment Reversals and Chargebacks

A transaction may be reversed, deducted, frozen or adjusted where:

a cardholder disputes a Top-Up;

a chargeback is received;

fraud is suspected;

payment was made in error;

duplicate credit occurred;

the Payment Partner reverses the transaction;

a refund is required;

a transaction is unlawful;

a transaction breaches these Terms;

Duranki or Inkolo Connect is required to do so by law, court order, bank, regulator or Payment Partner.

If a user has already spent or withdrawn funds that are later reversed, the user remains liable to repay the amount to Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may recover the amount from future Wallet balances, withhold future payments, suspend the Wallet or pursue recovery by lawful means.

14. Incorrect Transfers

Users are responsible for checking recipient details before sending money.

If a user sends money to the wrong member, Duranki and Inkolo Connect will not be liable for the loss.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may attempt to assist, but do not guarantee recovery.

A recipient who receives money in error must return it immediately when requested.

Failure to return money received in error may result in Wallet suspension, account restriction and legal action.

15. Prohibited Wallet Use

Users may not use the Wallet for:

fraud;

scams;

money laundering;

terrorist financing;

illegal gambling;

unlawful loans;

prohibited goods or services;

stolen goods;

fake payment confirmations;

identity theft;

unauthorised card use;

chargeback abuse;

illegal investment schemes;

pyramid schemes;

bribery or corruption;

evading platform rules;

any unlawful or suspicious activity.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may report suspicious transactions to banks, Payment Partners, regulators or law enforcement where required or permitted.

16. Account Security

Users are responsible for keeping their account, PIN, password, OTP, device, SIM card and login details secure.

Users must not share:

passwords;

PINs;

OTPs;

card details;

login details;

device access;

wallet access;

verification codes.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by the user sharing security details, losing their device, allowing unauthorised access, falling for scams or failing to secure their account.

Users must immediately report suspected unauthorised access or fraud.

17. Wallet Freezing, Holds and Restrictions

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, hold, delay, reverse, restrict or suspend Wallet funds or Wallet access where:

fraud is suspected;

suspicious activity is detected;

a chargeback is received;

a user fails verification;

a dispute is raised;

a law, regulator, bank or Payment Partner requires it;

the user breaches these Terms;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the Wallet is used for prohibited activity;

there is a technical or reconciliation issue;

the user provides false or incomplete information;

risk controls require review.

A Wallet freeze, hold or restriction does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect accepts liability to the user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep the Wallet restricted until the issue has been investigated and resolved to their satisfaction.

18. Refunds

Refunds may be processed where allowed by the relevant service terms, merchant rules, Payment Partner rules or applicable law.

Refunds are not automatic and may be subject to:

service-specific refund rules;

merchant approval;

fraud checks;

payment reversal rules;

chargeback rules;

transaction status;

platform fees;

settlement status;

Payment Partner approval.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for refunding users from their own funds where the dispute is between users, merchants, service providers or third parties.

19. Wallet Use for Platform Services

The Wallet may be used to pay for approved services on the platform, including services offered by Duranki, Inkolo Connect, communities, merchants, vendors, service providers or other members.

Each service may have its own separate terms and conditions.

Using the Wallet to pay for a service does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees that service, provider, member, merchant, product, delivery, quality or outcome.

20. Bank Account Details

For Cash-Outs, the user must provide accurate bank account details.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may verify bank details before processing Cash-Outs.

Cash-Outs may be declined or delayed where:

the bank account is invalid;

the account is closed;

the account holder name does not match;

verification fails;

the bank rejects the payment;

the account is flagged;

the Payment Partner requires more information.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for delays or losses caused by incorrect or rejected bank details.

21. Third-Party Payment Partners

Wallet transactions may depend on banks, card schemes, payment gateways, settlement providers, mobile networks, fraud-screening providers and other third parties.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for:

bank downtime;

failed card processing;

delayed settlement;

rejected payments;

reversed payments;

card network failures;

Payment Partner errors;

chargeback rules;

mobile network failures;

third-party system downtime.

Users may be required to accept additional terms from Payment Partners.

22. Tax and Legal Responsibility

Users are responsible for any tax, declaration, reporting or legal obligations arising from Wallet transactions.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide tax, legal, accounting or financial advice.

Users must not use the Wallet to hide income, evade tax, avoid legal obligations or conduct unlawful business.

23. Personal Information

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may process personal information for Wallet registration, identity verification, payment processing, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, transaction monitoring, cash-outs, compliance, support and reporting.

Personal information may be shared with Payment Partners, banks, regulators, law enforcement, verification providers and service providers where necessary or legally permitted.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information obtained through Wallet transactions.

24. Transaction Records

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep records of Wallet transactions, including:

sender details;

recipient details;

transaction amounts;

dates and times;

payment method;

transaction references;

device information;

location information, where applicable;

status history;

dispute history;

fraud checks;

cash-out details.

These records may be used for support, reporting, disputes, compliance, audits, reconciliation, fraud prevention and legal purposes.

25. Service Availability

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that the Wallet will always be available, uninterrupted, error-free or secure.

Wallet services may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

bank downtime;

Payment Partner downtime;

mobile network failure;

cyber incidents;

power failure;

regulatory issues;

events beyond reasonable control.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for loss caused by Wallet downtime, delayed payments, failed transactions or service interruptions.

26. User Disputes

Users must first attempt to resolve payment disputes directly with the relevant user, merchant, service provider or third party.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing Wallet records, payment records, chat records or transaction data.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a court, arbitrator, debt collector, insurer or legal representative.

Any decision to reverse, hold, release, freeze or block funds is a platform risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

27. Death, Incapacity or Account Closure

If a user dies, becomes incapacitated or requests account closure, Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require legal documents before releasing or transferring Wallet funds.

Required documents may include:

death certificate;

letters of executorship;

proof of authority;

identity documents;

bank confirmation;

court orders;

any other document reasonably required.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may withhold Wallet funds until lawful authority is confirmed.

28. Closing or Suspending the Wallet

A user may request Wallet closure, subject to settlement of outstanding transactions, disputes, fees, reversals, chargebacks and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may close, suspend or restrict a Wallet where:

the user breaches these Terms;

the user fails verification;

fraud is suspected;

the Wallet is inactive;

required by law;

required by a Payment Partner;

the user misuses the platform;

the platform stops offering the Wallet service.

Any remaining funds may be paid out to the user’s verified bank account, subject to fees, deductions, chargebacks, legal holds and compliance requirements.

29. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

failed Wallet transactions;

delayed Top-Ups;

delayed Cash-Outs;

incorrect recipient details;

incorrect bank details;

unauthorised account access;

fraud or scams;

chargebacks;

reversals;

Payment Partner failures;

bank failures;

mobile network failures;

wallet freezes or holds;

user error;

system downtime;

disputed transactions;

loss of income;

loss of opportunity;

emotional distress;

third-party conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the fees actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific Wallet transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

30. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s Wallet use;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraudulent activity;

unauthorised card use;

chargebacks;

incorrect transfers;

incorrect bank details;

money laundering or suspicious activity;

misuse of another user’s information;

disputes with another user;

claims by banks, cardholders, Payment Partners, regulators or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Wallet.

31. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Wallet Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Wallet after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

32. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled in accordance with South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

33. Acceptance

By activating or using the Wallet, the user confirms that they:

have read these Wallet Terms;

understand these Wallet Terms;

agree to be bound by these Wallet Terms;

understand that the Wallet is not a bank account;

understand that funds can be sent from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

understand that Top-Ups may be made using a bank card where available;

understand that Cash-Outs may take up to 5 business days;

accept responsibility for checking recipient details, bank details, transaction amounts and account security;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, delay, reverse or restrict Wallet transactions where required for fraud, compliance, legal, payment or risk reasons.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before activating the Wallet:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Wallet Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that I can send money from my Wallet to another member’s Wallet, top up using my bank card where available, and request a cash-out to my bank account. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed for fraud, verification, compliance, bank or payment partner checks.

Suggested Transfer Confirmation Text

Before sending money to another member:

Please confirm that the recipient and amount are correct. Wallet transfers may not be reversible once submitted. Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible if you send money to the wrong member.

Suggested Cash-Out Confirmation Text

Before cashing out:

I confirm that my bank details are correct. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed, declined or held if verification, fraud, compliance, bank or payment partner checks are required.

EDUU SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These EduU Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, register for, participate in, pay for, complete, receive, upload content to, communicate about or otherwise use educational programmes, courses, training, workshops, learning material, assessments, certificates or skills-development services made available through the EduU service on the Inkolo Connect platform.

By using EduU, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Acceptable Use Policy, Community Terms and any additional service-specific terms, training provider terms, course rules, programme rules, assessment rules or certification rules applicable to the selected educational programme.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools and service modules.

“EduU” means the education and training service module on the Platform that allows users to access, register for, participate in or complete educational programmes, training, courses, workshops, lessons, assessments, skills programmes or learning-related services.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses EduU, including learners, students, parents, guardians, facilitators, tutors, trainers, instructors, service providers and administrators.

“Learner” means a user who enrols in, participates in or completes an educational programme through EduU.

“Training Provider” means any third-party school, college, academy, institution, tutor, instructor, facilitator, content provider, assessment body, skills provider, employer partner, NGO, church, community organisation or other provider responsible for supplying educational content, training, instruction, assessment or certification.

“Course” means any educational programme, lesson, course, workshop, class, training session, learning path, module, webinar, assessment or skills programme made available through EduU.

“Course Material” means any videos, documents, notes, slides, worksheets, assessments, exams, quizzes, assignments, templates, resources, recordings or other learning content made available through EduU.

“Certificate” means any completion certificate, attendance certificate, skills certificate, statement of results, badge, record of participation or other proof of completion issued by Inkolo Connect, a Training Provider or a third-party certification body.

2. Purpose of EduU

EduU is intended to provide access to educational programmes, skills-development opportunities, online courses, community training, workshops and learning support.

EduU may include:

online courses;

in-person training;

hybrid learning programmes;

skills-development programmes;

church or community training;

youth development programmes;

entrepreneurship training;

digital skills training;

financial literacy training;

employment-readiness training;

second-chance education programmes;

vocational training;

exam preparation;

mentorship programmes;

workshops and seminars;

any other approved educational programme made available on the Platform.

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform and may facilitate access, registration, communication, payment, learning records and document delivery.

Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect is not the school, university, college, accredited training provider, examination body, employer, tutor, instructor or certification authority.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect acts as the Platform provider and education access facilitator.

Inkolo Connect may assist by:

displaying Courses;

allowing users to register for Courses;

collecting or facilitating payment;

providing access to Course Material;

enabling communication between Learners and Training Providers;

tracking enrolment and participation;

displaying certificates or completion records where available;

providing dashboards and reporting tools;

sending reminders and notifications;

supporting document uploads;

routing support queries.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee:

that a Course will meet the Learner’s expectations;

that a Learner will pass;

that a Learner will complete the Course;

that a Certificate will be accepted by an employer or institution;

that a Course is accredited unless expressly stated;

that a Course will lead to employment;

that a Course will lead to income;

that a Training Provider will always be available;

that every learning outcome will be achieved.

4. Third-Party Training Providers

Some Courses may be provided by third-party Training Providers.

Where a Course is provided by a Training Provider, the relationship for the educational content, instruction, assessments, course delivery and certification is between the Learner and the Training Provider, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

Training Provider conduct;

course quality;

instructor availability;

assessment standards;

course delays;

course cancellations;

certification delays;

accreditation claims made by the Training Provider;

employment promises made by the Training Provider;

disputes between Learners and Training Providers.

Training Providers remain responsible for their own content, instructors, course delivery, assessment processes, certifications, accreditation, compliance and learner support.

5. No Accreditation Guarantee

Not all Courses on EduU are accredited.

A Course is only accredited if this is expressly stated in the Course description and supported by the relevant Training Provider or accrediting body.

Unless clearly stated in writing, a Course should be treated as non-accredited training, personal development, skills development, community learning or informal education.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Course, Certificate, badge or completion record will be recognised by:

employers;

universities;

colleges;

schools;

government departments;

professional bodies;

SETAs;

regulators;

scholarship providers;

any other institution.

Users must check accreditation status before registering or paying.

6. No Guarantee of Results, Employment or Income

EduU is intended to support learning and skills development, but results depend on the Learner’s own effort, attendance, participation, ability, resources, device access, internet access and completion of required activities.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee:

academic success;

exam success;

assessment success;

employment;

promotion;

income;

business success;

acceptance into another institution;

qualification recognition;

scholarship approval;

funding approval;

job placement.

Any employment, income, business or career outcome remains the responsibility of the Learner and any relevant employer, institution or Training Provider.

7. User Eligibility

To use EduU, a user may be required to:

register on the Platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide accurate personal information;

accept these Terms;

accept the applicable Course rules;

pay applicable fees;

meet Course entry requirements;

provide required documents;

have access to a suitable device and internet connection where required.

Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider may refuse, delay, suspend or cancel access where the user does not meet the relevant requirements.

8. Minors

Users under the age of 18 may require the consent and involvement of a parent or legal guardian to register for or participate in EduU.

Parents or guardians are responsible for:

approving the minor’s participation;

reviewing Course suitability;

paying applicable fees;

supervising online access where necessary;

ensuring the minor behaves appropriately;

understanding any risks related to online learning, communication and data use.

Inkolo Connect may restrict access to certain Courses for minors where required by law, policy, safety rules or Course requirements.

9. Course Registration

Registration for a Course may be subject to availability, eligibility, payment, verification and acceptance by Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider.

A Course registration is not confirmed until the platform or Training Provider confirms enrolment.

Inkolo Connect may cancel or reject registration where:

information is incomplete;

payment fails;

the Course is full;

the Course is cancelled;

the user is not eligible;

fraud is suspected;

the user breaches these Terms;

the Training Provider rejects the registration.

10. Course Fees and Payments

EduU Courses may be free, sponsored, subsidised or paid.

Where Course fees apply, the user is responsible for paying the fees according to the payment terms shown on the Platform or provided by the Training Provider.

Payments may be made through:

Wallet;

card payment;

bank payment;

voucher;

sponsor funding;

grant funding;

employer payment;

any other approved payment method.

Inkolo Connect may act only as a payment facilitator.

Payment through the Platform does not make Inkolo Connect responsible for Course delivery where the Course is supplied by a Training Provider.

11. Refunds and Cancellations

Refunds and cancellations are governed by the relevant Course rules, Training Provider terms, sponsor rules, payment terms and applicable law.

Refunds may not be available where:

the user has accessed Course Material;

the Course has started;

digital content has been delivered;

the user failed to attend;

the user did not complete the Course;

the user changed their mind;

the cancellation period has expired;

the Training Provider’s rules do not allow a refund.

Inkolo Connect is not required to refund users from its own funds where the Course is provided by a Training Provider.

Where a refund is approved, it may be processed to the Wallet, original payment method or other approved payment channel, subject to payment partner rules and processing timelines.

12. Course Changes, Delays and Cancellations

Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider may change, postpone, replace or cancel a Course where necessary.

This may happen because of:

low enrolment;

instructor unavailability;

technical issues;

venue issues;

funding changes;

sponsor changes;

Course content updates;

safety concerns;

regulatory issues;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss of income, travel costs, opportunity loss, disappointment or inconvenience caused by Course changes, delays or cancellations.

13. Learner Responsibilities

Learners must:

provide accurate information;

attend required sessions;

complete assignments honestly;

respect instructors and other Learners;

follow Course rules;

use Course Material lawfully;

not cheat or plagiarise;

not submit another person’s work;

not disrupt classes;

not harass or abuse others;

not share login details;

not record sessions without permission;

not redistribute Course Material without permission.

A Learner who breaches Course rules may be suspended, removed, failed, denied a Certificate or banned from EduU.

14. Training Provider Responsibilities

Training Providers are responsible for:

Course content;

Course accuracy;

instructor conduct;

learning outcomes;

Course delivery;

assessments;

Course schedules;

certificates issued by them;

accreditation claims;

learner support;

compliance with applicable laws;

suitability of Course Material;

safety of in-person training venues.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for failures, negligence, misrepresentations or disputes caused by Training Providers.

15. Assessments and Examinations

Some Courses may include assessments, tests, exams, assignments, practical tasks or participation requirements.

Assessment rules will be set by Inkolo Connect, the Training Provider or the relevant assessment body.

The user must comply with all assessment rules.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that a Learner will pass or that assessment results will be accepted by any third party.

Assessment results may be withheld where:

fees are unpaid;

fraud is suspected;

cheating is suspected;

identity verification fails;

required work was not completed;

the Training Provider has not released results;

administrative checks are incomplete.

16. Certificates and Completion Records

A Certificate may be issued where a Learner meets the completion requirements of a Course.

A Certificate may be an attendance certificate, completion certificate, informal certificate, platform certificate, provider certificate or accredited certificate, depending on the Course.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Certificate will be accepted by employers, institutions, regulators or other third parties unless expressly stated and supported by the relevant accrediting body.

Certificates may be withheld, cancelled or corrected where fraud, non-payment, cheating, incorrect information or administrative error is identified.

17. Course Material and Intellectual Property

All Course Material is protected by intellectual property rights.

Users may access Course Material only for their own personal learning or authorised educational use.

Users may not, without permission:

copy Course Material;

sell Course Material;

upload Course Material elsewhere;

share Course videos or documents;

reproduce assessments;

record live sessions;

distribute login access;

remove copyright notices;

use Course Material for commercial training.

Course Material remains the property of Inkolo Connect, the Training Provider, content owner or licensor.

Breach of this clause may result in suspension, legal action and loss of access.

18. User-Generated Content

Users may be allowed to upload assignments, comments, questions, posts, documents, videos, photos or other content.

The user is responsible for all content they upload.

Users must not upload content that is:

unlawful;

false;

defamatory;

abusive;

discriminatory;

sexually explicit;

violent;

plagiarised;

infected with malware;

infringing intellectual property rights;

harmful to children;

in breach of these Terms.

Inkolo Connect may remove or restrict content that breaches these Terms or creates legal, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

19. Online Learning and Technical Requirements

Users are responsible for ensuring that they have the required device, internet connection, data, software, email access, phone number and technical ability to participate in online Courses.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for a user’s inability to access a Course due to:

poor internet connection;

lack of data;

device failure;

outdated software;

incorrect login details;

email failure;

mobile network issues;

user technical problems.

Where a Course requires online participation, failure to connect or attend may affect completion.

20. In-Person Training

Where EduU includes in-person training, workshops, practical sessions or events, the Training Provider or event organiser is responsible for venue safety, attendance control, instructor availability and training delivery.

Users attend in-person training at their own risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

accidents;

injuries;

illness;

theft;

lost property;

transport issues;

venue problems;

food safety;

cancelled sessions;

personal disputes at the venue.

21. Code of Conduct

Users must behave respectfully in all EduU spaces, including online classes, chats, forums, workshops and in-person sessions.

Users may not:

harass others;

threaten others;

use hate speech;

bully others;

disrupt learning;

share offensive content;

send spam;

impersonate instructors or learners;

solicit money unlawfully;

misuse the learning environment.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or remove users who breach the code of conduct.

22. Communication

EduU may use platform notifications, SMS, WhatsApp, email, in-app messages, calls or other channels to communicate Course updates, reminders, schedules, results, payment notices and support messages.

Users are responsible for keeping their contact details updated.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed messages caused by incorrect contact details, phone issues, network problems or user failure to check messages.

23. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

registration;

identity verification;

Course enrolment;

attendance tracking;

assessments;

certificate issuing;

payment processing;

learner support;

sponsor reporting;

programme reporting;

compliance;

fraud prevention;

communication;

dispute handling.

Personal information may be shared with Training Providers, sponsors, employers, community partners, government funders, assessment bodies, certification bodies, payment partners, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse another Learner’s, instructor’s or Training Provider’s personal information.

24. Sponsored, Funded or Grant-Based Programmes

Some EduU programmes may be funded, sponsored, subsidised or supported by third parties.

Participation in such programmes may be subject to additional rules, including:

eligibility requirements;

attendance requirements;

reporting requirements;

identity verification;

proof of completion;

demographic reporting;

sponsor approval;

funding availability;

programme audits.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that a user will be accepted into a funded programme or that funding will continue.

Where a sponsor, funder or grant provider changes or withdraws funding, the programme may be changed, suspended or cancelled.

25. No Medical, Legal, Financial or Professional Advice

Course content may include general educational information.

Unless expressly stated and provided by a properly authorised professional, EduU content does not constitute medical advice, legal advice, financial advice, investment advice, tax advice, counselling advice or professional advice.

Users should seek qualified professional advice before relying on Course content for important decisions.

26. Fraud, Cheating and Misrepresentation

Users may not:

submit false documents;

use another person’s identity;

cheat in assessments;

plagiarise work;

forge certificates;

manipulate attendance records;

falsely claim completion;

sell Course access;

hack or misuse systems;

create fake learner accounts.

Inkolo Connect may suspend users, cancel enrolments, withhold certificates, reverse rewards, notify Training Providers and take legal action where fraud or cheating is suspected.

27. Disputes

Users must first attempt to resolve Course-related disputes through the relevant support process.

Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing:

registration records;

payment records;

attendance records;

course access logs;

communication records;

assessment submissions;

Training Provider feedback.

Inkolo Connect is not a court, arbitrator, school authority, university, regulator or accreditation body.

Disputes about Course delivery, instructor conduct, assessment results or certificates issued by a Training Provider must be handled with the relevant Training Provider.

28. Platform Moderation Rights

Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove Course content;

suspend Course access;

suspend users;

restrict communication tools;

remove abusive content;

request verification;

withhold certificates;

block payments;

report fraud;

terminate access to EduU.

These rights may be exercised where Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, security, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

29. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that EduU will always be available, uninterrupted, secure or error-free.

EduU may be unavailable because of:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

internet failures;

mobile network failures;

power failures;

payment partner failures;

Training Provider system failures;

cyber incidents;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed classes, delayed access, lost progress, delayed certificates or failed submissions caused by downtime, unless caused by proven gross negligence.

30. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Training Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

Course dissatisfaction;

Course cancellation;

Course delay;

Training Provider failure;

instructor conduct;

failed assessments;

non-completion;

certificate rejection;

lack of accreditation;

employment not being obtained;

income not being earned;

technical access problems;

device or internet failure;

missed classes;

incorrect user information;

plagiarism or cheating by users;

in-person training incidents;

learner disputes;

sponsor or funder withdrawal;

third-party decisions;

platform downtime.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of opportunity, loss of employment, loss of profit, reputational harm or emotional distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific EduU transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

31. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Training Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of EduU;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

false information supplied by the user;

plagiarism, cheating or fraud;

misuse of Course Material;

unauthorised sharing of content;

harassment or misconduct;

misuse of personal information;

disputes with Training Providers, instructors, Learners, sponsors or third parties;

any claim arising from the user’s participation in a Course, assessment, workshop or training event.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

32. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to EduU where the user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

fails to pay fees;

abuses Course Material;

shares login details;

harasses others;

cheats or plagiarises;

submits false documents;

disrupts learning;

creates legal, financial, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect may also suspend or discontinue EduU, any Course, or any part of the service at any time.

33. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these EduU Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of EduU after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

34. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

35. Acceptance

By using EduU, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is the Platform provider and education access facilitator;

accept that Courses may be provided by third-party Training Providers;

accept that Inkolo Connect does not guarantee accreditation, results, certificates, employment, income or acceptance by third parties unless expressly stated;

accept responsibility for reading Course details before registering or paying;

accept responsibility for their own participation, attendance, conduct, assignments and device access;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from Course participation, user conduct, provider disputes, assessments, certificates or educational outcomes.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user registers for a Course:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the EduU Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect provides the platform and may facilitate access to educational programmes, but does not guarantee accreditation, results, certificates, employment, income or acceptance by any employer, institution or third party unless expressly stated in writing.

Suggested Course Registration Warning

Before payment or enrolment:

Please read the Course details carefully before registering. Confirm the provider, cost, duration, accreditation status, refund rules, certificate type and requirements. Inkolo Connect is not responsible if you register for the wrong Course or if the Course does not meet your expectations.

Suggested Certificate Disclaimer

On certificates or completion pages:

This certificate or completion record confirms participation or completion only as described in the Course details. It does not guarantee employment, income, admission to another institution or professional recognition unless expressly stated by the relevant accredited provider.

Suggested Parent / Guardian Checkbox for Minors

Where the Learner is under 18:

I confirm that I am the parent or legal guardian of the minor Learner and I consent to their participation in this EduU programme. I accept responsibility for reviewing the Course suitability, supervising access where necessary and ensuring that the minor complies with the EduU Service Terms and Conditions.

BUY & SELL SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Buy & Sell Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, browse, list, advertise, buy, sell, communicate, negotiate, pay, receive payment, arrange delivery, collect goods, or otherwise use the Buy & Sell service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Buy & Sell service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Community Rules, and any additional rules, policies or instructions published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its website, mobile application, backend systems, dashboards, wallet systems, payment tools, communication tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community, church and member-management platform operated on or through Duranki, including services offered to churches, communities, members and approved service providers.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital service, dashboard, app, website, wallet, communication channel or service module.

“Buy & Sell Service” means the marketplace function where users may list, advertise, browse, buy, sell, negotiate, communicate about and transact in respect of goods and/or permitted services.

“User” means any person who registers, logs in, lists goods, buys goods, sells goods, communicates, pays, receives payment or otherwise uses the Buy & Sell service.

“Seller” means a user who lists, advertises or offers goods or services for sale.

“Buyer” means a user who browses, negotiates, purchases or attempts to purchase goods or services.

“Listing” means any advertisement, product post, service post, description, image, price, condition statement, offer or other information uploaded by a Seller.

“Transaction” means any sale, attempted sale, payment, delivery, collection, exchange or agreement between a Buyer and Seller.

“Prohibited Items” means goods or services that may not be listed, advertised, sold or purchased through the Buy & Sell service.

2. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide a digital marketplace facility that allows users to connect with each other for the purpose of buying and selling permitted goods and services.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not the seller, buyer, manufacturer, supplier, importer, distributor, owner, employer, agent, broker, auctioneer or guarantor of any goods or services listed by users, unless this is expressly stated in writing.

The contract of sale is concluded directly between the Buyer and the Seller. Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not parties to that sale unless they expressly state otherwise in writing.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee:

that any item exists;

that any item is genuine;

that any item is legal to sell;

that the Seller owns the item;

that the Buyer will pay;

that the Seller will deliver;

that the item is safe, working, complete or fit for purpose;

that the item matches the listing description;

that a user is trustworthy;

that a transaction will be successful.

Users use the Buy & Sell service at their own risk.

3. User Eligibility

To use the Buy & Sell service, a user must:

be registered on the Duranki platform;

provide accurate personal and contact information;

comply with all verification requirements requested by Duranki or Inkolo Connect;

be legally capable of entering into a transaction;

comply with all applicable laws;

comply with these Terms and all platform rules.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse, suspend, limit or terminate access to the Buy & Sell service at any time where a user fails verification, provides false information, abuses the service, creates risk, breaches these Terms or is suspected of unlawful activity.

4. Minors

Users under the age of 18 may not buy, sell, list items, make payment requests, receive wallet payments or conclude transactions through the Buy & Sell service without the involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove listings, reverse access, suspend accounts or block transactions where a minor appears to be using the service in a way that creates legal, financial, safety or compliance risk.

5. User Responsibility

Each user is fully responsible for their own conduct, listings, communications, offers, purchases, sales, deliveries, collections, negotiations and transactions.

Users must ensure that:

all information they provide is true, accurate and not misleading;

they have the legal right to sell any item listed;

the item is not stolen, counterfeit, illegal, dangerous or prohibited;

the item is accurately described;

the item condition is honestly disclosed;

the price is clearly stated;

any defects, damage or missing parts are disclosed;

they comply with all laws, permits and tax obligations;

they do not defraud, mislead, threaten, harass or abuse another user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for user behaviour, user promises, user dishonesty, user mistakes or user misconduct.

6. Listings

A Seller may only create listings that are accurate, lawful and honest.

Each listing must include sufficient information to allow a Buyer to make an informed decision, including where applicable:

item name;

description;

condition;

price;

location;

photos;

defects;

missing parts;

delivery or collection options;

warranty status, if any;

whether the item is new, used, refurbished or damaged.

The Seller is solely responsible for the content of the listing.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may, without notice:

remove a listing;

edit or hide a listing;

suspend a Seller;

request proof of ownership;

request identity verification;

block a category of goods;

report suspicious activity to authorities.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not required to pre-screen every listing and are not liable for listings uploaded by users.

7. Prohibited Items and Services

Users may not list, advertise, buy, sell, exchange or arrange any transaction involving prohibited, illegal, unsafe, restricted or inappropriate goods or services.

Prohibited items include, but are not limited to:

stolen goods;

counterfeit goods;

illegal drugs or controlled substances;

prescription medication;

weapons, firearms, ammunition or explosives;

dangerous chemicals;

pornography or sexually explicit material;

human body parts or remains;

live animals, unless expressly permitted by the platform and allowed by law;

alcohol, tobacco or vaping products where restricted by law;

fake identity documents, passports, licences or certificates;

SIM cards, bank cards, account credentials or access codes;

hacked accounts, stolen software or illegal digital goods;

pirated media or software;

goods subject to recall or safety prohibition;

goods requiring a licence, permit or regulatory approval unless the Seller has provided proof acceptable to Duranki;

any item that promotes hate, discrimination, violence or unlawful conduct;

any item Duranki or Inkolo Connect considers unsafe, unlawful, reputationally harmful or unsuitable for the platform.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update the prohibited-items list at any time.

A user who lists prohibited items may be suspended, permanently banned and reported to law enforcement.

8. No Guarantee of Product Quality or Safety

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not inspect, test, certify, verify or approve user-listed goods unless expressly stated in writing.

All goods are sold by the Seller, not by Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Buyers must satisfy themselves before purchasing that the item is:

real;

correctly described;

legally owned by the Seller;

safe;

complete;

working;

suitable for the Buyer’s intended use;

priced fairly;

available for collection or delivery.

Buyers should inspect goods before confirming acceptance wherever possible.

9. Used Goods

Most goods listed through the Buy & Sell service may be second-hand or pre-owned.

The Buyer accepts that used goods may show wear, age, damage, defects, missing accessories, reduced performance or limited remaining useful life.

The Seller must disclose known defects, but Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not accept responsibility for defects, disputes or dissatisfaction relating to used goods.

10. Pricing

The Seller is responsible for setting the price.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that prices are fair, market-related, reasonable or correct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove or flag listings that appear misleading, abusive, fraudulent or suspicious.

The platform may charge service fees, commission, transaction fees, wallet fees, payment-processing fees, listing fees or promotional fees where applicable. Any such fees may be deducted before settlement to the Seller.

11. Payments

Where the platform provides wallet, payment request, QR, scan-to-pay, card, bank or other payment functionality, users agree to comply with the applicable Wallet Terms and Payment Terms.

Payment through the platform does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees the transaction, the item, the Seller, the Buyer, delivery, quality or refund.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may hold, delay, block, reverse, review or suspend a payment where:

fraud is suspected;

a dispute is raised;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the user fails verification;

chargeback risk exists;

incorrect details were provided;

a regulator, bank or payment partner requires it;

Duranki determines that the transaction creates risk.

Users must not make payments outside the platform if the platform requires in-app payment for that transaction.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses arising from users making side payments, cash payments, EFTs, deposits or arrangements outside the platform.

12. No Escrow Unless Expressly Stated

Unless Duranki expressly states in writing that an escrow service applies to a specific transaction, the platform does not provide escrow protection.

Any reference to payment processing, wallet transfer or payment request does not automatically create escrow, buyer protection, seller protection or delivery guarantee.

13. Delivery and Collection

Delivery, courier, handover and collection arrangements are the responsibility of the Buyer and Seller, unless Duranki expressly offers a specific delivery service for that transaction.

Users must agree on:

collection location;

delivery method;

delivery cost;

risk of loss;

timing;

proof of delivery;

inspection on delivery;

who pays for failed delivery.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for:

late delivery;

failed delivery;

damaged goods during delivery;

lost goods;

stolen goods;

wrong addresses;

courier disputes;

unsafe collection arrangements.

Users are encouraged to meet in safe public locations and avoid sharing unnecessary personal information.

14. Risk and Ownership

Risk in the goods passes between Buyer and Seller according to the arrangement they make between themselves.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not determine when ownership passes unless this is expressly regulated by a separate written platform rule for a specific transaction type.

The Seller remains responsible for ensuring they are legally entitled to sell the goods.

The Buyer remains responsible for ensuring they are satisfied with the goods before accepting them.

15. Returns, Refunds and Cancellations

Returns, refunds and cancellations are primarily between the Buyer and Seller.

The Seller must comply with applicable South African consumer-protection laws where those laws apply to the Seller and transaction. South African e-commerce transactions may trigger consumer protection obligations under the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act and Consumer Protection Act, depending on the nature of the seller, buyer and transaction.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not automatically provide refunds and are not responsible for refunding a Buyer from their own funds unless Duranki or Inkolo Connect was the actual seller or has expressly agreed in writing.

Duranki may assist with dispute communication, but this does not make Duranki or Inkolo Connect liable for the transaction.

Where payment was made through the platform, Duranki may withhold or reverse funds if required by law, payment partner rules, fraud controls, chargeback process, internal risk assessment or dispute procedures.

16. Disputes Between Buyers and Sellers

Users must first attempt to resolve disputes directly with each other in good faith.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may, but are not obliged to, assist by:

reviewing platform messages;

reviewing payment records;

requesting proof of delivery;

requesting photos or evidence;

temporarily holding wallet settlement;

suspending listings;

warning users;

limiting accounts;

referring users to police, regulators or other authorities.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not act as a court, arbitrator, mediator, debt collector, insurer or legal representative.

Any decision by Duranki or Inkolo Connect to suspend, release, reverse or hold a payment is a platform risk-control decision and does not prevent any user from pursuing legal remedies independently.

17. Fraud Prevention

Users may not use the Buy & Sell service for fraud, scams, theft, money laundering, identity theft, false listings, phishing, fake payment confirmations, stolen goods, counterfeit goods or any unlawful activity.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may use fraud-detection measures, including:

identity verification;

device information;

wallet activity monitoring;

transaction monitoring;

user reports;

suspicious pattern detection;

payment-partner alerts;

account restrictions;

manual review.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may share information with banks, payment providers, law enforcement, regulators, community administrators or affected parties where legally permitted or required.

18. User Communication and Chat

The platform may allow Buyers and Sellers to communicate through chat, messaging, notifications or calls.

Users must not:

harass, threaten or abuse others;

use hate speech;

send sexual, violent or offensive content;

send spam;

send fake payment proof;

request illegal goods;

move transactions off-platform to avoid fees or safety controls;

share another person’s private information;

impersonate another person or organisation.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may monitor, store, review or disclose communications where necessary for platform safety, dispute resolution, legal compliance, fraud prevention or enforcement of these Terms.

19. Ratings and Reviews

The platform may allow users to rate or review each other.

Users must provide honest, fair and lawful reviews.

Users may not post false, defamatory, abusive, threatening, discriminatory or misleading reviews.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove or moderate reviews at their discretion.

A rating or review does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect endorses or guarantees that user.

20. Platform Moderation Rights

Duranki and Inkolo Connect have the right, at any time and without prior notice, to:

remove listings;

remove images;

block keywords;

suspend accounts;

restrict wallet access;

delay payments;

request verification;

require proof of ownership;

block high-risk categories;

limit transaction values;

ban users;

report unlawful conduct;

refuse service.

These rights may be exercised where Duranki or Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, reputational, safety, community or compliance risk.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for loss of sales, income, profit, opportunity or reputation caused by moderation actions taken in good faith.

21. No Endorsement

The appearance of a listing on the platform does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect has approved, verified, endorsed, inspected, certified or recommended the item, Seller or Buyer.

Users must conduct their own checks before transacting.

22. Community and Church Environment

Where the Buy & Sell service is used within a church, community or Inkolo Connect environment, users must act respectfully and in line with the values, rules and standards of that community.

Community access does not mean that the church, KZNCC, Inkolo Connect, Duranki or any community leader guarantees any user, listing or transaction.

Churches, pastors, bishops, community leaders and administrators are not liable for user transactions unless they personally act as the Seller or Buyer.

23. Taxes and Compliance

Users are responsible for their own taxes, declarations, licences, permits and legal obligations arising from their buying or selling activity.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide tax, accounting, legal or business advice.

A user who sells regularly or commercially may be required to comply with additional consumer, tax, business registration, product safety and regulatory obligations.

24. Product Safety

Sellers must not sell unsafe, recalled, defective or dangerous goods.

Sellers must disclose safety risks, defects, damage or missing parts.

Buyers must use caution when purchasing items such as electrical goods, appliances, tools, vehicle parts, baby goods, children’s products, cosmetics, food-related items or any item that may create safety risk.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for injury, death, damage or loss caused by goods purchased from another user.

25. Intellectual Property

Users may only upload content they own or are legally allowed to use.

Users may not upload copyrighted images, brand logos, product photos, trademarks, manuals or other content without permission.

By uploading listing content, the user grants Duranki and Inkolo Connect a non-exclusive, royalty-free right to use, display, store, reproduce, moderate and promote that content for purposes of operating the platform.

26. Personal Information

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may process user personal information for registration, verification, listing management, payments, communication, fraud prevention, dispute handling, legal compliance, reporting and platform operation.

Personal information must be processed in line with the platform Privacy Policy and applicable data-protection laws, including POPIA. POPIA regulates the processing of personal information by public and private bodies in South Africa and introduces conditions for lawful processing.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information obtained through the platform.

27. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, community administrators and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, injury, death, expense or liability arising from:

user listings;

inaccurate descriptions;

defective goods;

counterfeit goods;

stolen goods;

failed payments;

chargebacks;

delivery problems;

fraud;

scams;

disputes between users;

unsafe meetings or collections;

user communications;

account suspension;

listing removal;

system downtime;

data loss;

third-party service failures;

payment-provider delays;

courier failures;

user breach of law.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of profit, loss of income, loss of business, loss of opportunity, reputational harm or emotional distress.

Where liability cannot legally be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid by the affected user for the specific transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

28. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, community administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s listing;

the user’s transaction;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

the user’s sale of illegal, unsafe, stolen or counterfeit goods;

the user’s fraud, negligence or misconduct;

the user’s misuse of another person’s information;

disputes with another user;

claims by buyers, sellers, regulators, banks, payment partners, couriers or third parties.

This indemnity continues even after the user stops using the platform.

29. Suspension and Termination

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access immediately if the user:

breaches these Terms;

provides false information;

fails verification;

lists prohibited goods;

is suspected of fraud;

abuses another user;

damages the platform reputation;

creates legal or financial risk;

circumvents platform payments;

repeatedly receives complaints;

violates community rules.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may also suspend or terminate the Buy & Sell service, or any part of it, at any time.

30. Reporting Problems

Users must report suspicious listings, fraud, prohibited goods, abusive behaviour, fake payment proof or safety concerns through the available platform reporting tools or support channels.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may investigate, but are not obliged to resolve every report in favour of the reporting user.

Users should report suspected crimes directly to the South African Police Service or the relevant authority.

31. Availability of the Service

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that the Buy & Sell service will always be available, uninterrupted, error-free or secure.

The service may be unavailable due to maintenance, upgrades, technical failure, network problems, cyber incidents, third-party provider failure or reasons beyond Duranki’s control.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by downtime or service interruption.

32. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Buy & Sell service after the updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

33. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled in accordance with South African law, subject to any applicable consumer-protection rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

34. Acceptance

By accessing or using the Buy & Sell service, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

agree to be bound by these Terms;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect are platform facilitators and not parties to user transactions;

accept that they use the Buy & Sell service at their own risk.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user lists or buys an item, you can show this checkbox:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Buy & Sell Terms and Conditions. I understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform and are not responsible for the item, the seller, the buyer, delivery, quality, defects, payment disputes or losses arising from my transaction.

Suggested Short Warning on Buy & Sell Page

Buy and Sell safely. Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the platform only. All listings, prices, descriptions, payments, delivery and collections are the responsibility of the buyer and seller. Do not buy prohibited goods, do not make payments outside the platform, and report suspicious activity immediately.
