legalAcceptances.documentSnapshot:
TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF USE

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, registration number 2025/674136/07, is a private company with limited liability incorporated in the Republic of South Africa.

In these Terms, references to “Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” mean Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, applications, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners, where applicable.

These Terms and Conditions of Use govern the access to and use of the Inkolo Connect platform, including all services, member dashboards, community dashboards, church dashboards, wallet tools, communication tools, marketplace tools, job tools, ride tools, funeral cover access tools, value-added services and any other services made available through the platform from time to time.

Important Disclaimer

Inkolo Connect is a digital platform, onboarding, administration, communication and facilitation service.

Unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement, Inkolo Connect is not:

a licensed financial institution;

a bank;

an insurer;

an underwriter;

a financial services provider;

a funeral cover provider;

an employer;

a recruiter;

a labour broker;

a transport operator;

a taxi or e-hailing operator;

a seller of user-listed goods;

a guarantor of any third-party service.

Inkolo Connect may assist members with onboarding, administrative processes, platform access, communication, payment facilitation, digital dashboards, document uploads, member records, service activation and support processes.

Inkolo Connect does not provide financial advice, insurance advice, legal advice, tax advice, investment advice, labour advice, transport advice or medical advice unless expressly authorised and confirmed in writing.

Where third-party services, financial products, funeral cover, banking products, insurance products, payment products, employment opportunities, transport arrangements, marketplace listings or other services are made available through the platform, the contract is between the user and the relevant third-party service provider, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

1. Definitions and Interpretation

In these Terms, unless the context indicates otherwise:

1.1 “Acceptable Use Policy”

means Inkolo Connect’s rules for acceptable use of the platform, as published on the platform or website and updated from time to time.

1.2 “Affinity Treasury Account”

means a dedicated account, where applicable, funded or used by approved third-party service providers, financial service providers or platform partners to cover service fees, platform access costs, member benefits, community benefits or related agreed amounts.

1.3 “Agreement”

means these Terms and Conditions of Use, together with any applicable service-specific terms, policies, mandates, annexures, application forms, consent forms, product terms, payment terms and privacy policies.

1.4 “Basic Service Package”

means the minimum combination or level of platform services, community services, financial products, funeral products, wallet services, value-added services or other products selected or activated by a user, where applicable.

1.5 “Commencement Date”

means the date on which a user is granted access to the platform or a specific service.

1.6 “Confidential Information”

means all information, in whatever format, disclosed by one party to another, including business information, platform information, technical information, financial information, pricing, member data, product information, community information, software, trade secrets, processes, documents, reports, agreements, strategies, user information and any information marked or reasonably understood to be confidential.

1.7 “FICA”

means the Financial Intelligence Centre Act 38 of 2001, as amended.

1.8 “FSP”

means a Financial Services Provider licensed under the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, where applicable.

1.9 “Main Service Fee”

means the consolidated fixed fee, subscription fee, platform fee, service fee, product fee, access fee, administration fee or other amount payable for a service, product or package made available through the platform.

1.10 “Personal Information”

has the meaning given to it under POPIA and includes any information relating to an identifiable natural or juristic person.

1.11 “Platform”

means the Inkolo Connect online portal, application, website, mobile platform, dashboard, member profile, wallet system, service modules, communication tools, payment tools, marketplace tools and related digital services.

1.12 “Platform Costs”

means the portion of any Main Service Fee, subscription fee, service fee, transaction fee, commission, platform access fee or administration fee allocated to platform access, operation, hosting, maintenance, support, reporting, dashboards, technology, compliance and administration.

1.13 “POPIA”

means the Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013, as amended.

1.14 “Service Provider”

means any approved third-party provider, financial institution, bank, insurer, underwriter, FSP, payment provider, wallet provider, funeral cover provider, merchant, vendor, employer, job poster, driver, supplier, training provider, connectivity provider, value-added service provider or other party whose service is made available through the platform.

1.15 “Service-Specific Terms”

means additional terms and conditions that apply to specific platform services, including but not limited to Wallet, Buy & Sell, Job Search, Catch a Ride, Funeral Cover, Airtime, Data, Electricity, Vouchers, Loans, Banking Services, Merchant Services, QR Payments, Education Services and any other services introduced from time to time.

1.16 “User”

means any person who accesses or uses the platform, including a member, church member, community member, administrator, pastor, bishop, service provider, merchant, seller, buyer, job poster, worker, driver, passenger or any other authorised user.

1.17 “User Account”

means a registered user profile on the Inkolo Connect platform.

1.18 “Inkolo Connect Services”

means the administrative, onboarding, facilitation, platform access, dashboard, communication, payment facilitation, service activation, document management, reporting and support services provided by Inkolo Connect.

1.19 Interpretation

Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

Words in the singular include the plural, and words in the plural include the singular.

Any reference to law includes that law as amended or replaced from time to time.

2. Acceptance of Terms

2.1

By accessing, registering for, logging into, browsing, using or transacting through the platform, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms.

2.2

If the user does not agree to these Terms, the user must not access or use the platform.

2.3

The user confirms that they have legal capacity to enter into these Terms.

2.4

If the user acts on behalf of a church, organisation, community, business, service provider or other entity, the user confirms that they are authorised to bind that entity.

2.5

The user also agrees to all applicable Service-Specific Terms when using a specific service.

3. Risk Disclosure

3.1

The platform may provide access to third-party services, financial products, funeral cover, payment services, wallet services, marketplace services, job opportunities, ride arrangements and other products or services.

3.2

These services may carry risks, including financial risk, fraud risk, payment risk, operational risk, service provider risk, insurance risk, employment risk, transport risk, safety risk, legal risk and data risk.

3.3

The user must read all product documents, service provider terms, policy documents, fee schedules, disclosures, waiting periods, exclusions, payment terms and Service-Specific Terms before using or relying on any service.

3.4

Past performance, previous service availability, previous payments, previous claims, previous earnings or previous transaction success are not guarantees of future results.

4. Platform Access

4.1

Access to the platform is granted subject to:

registration of a valid User Account;

completion of any required verification;

enrolment in any required Basic Service Package, where applicable;

acceptance of these Terms;

acceptance of applicable Service-Specific Terms;

compliance with platform rules;

payment of applicable fees, where applicable.

4.2

Platform access may automatically renew unless cancelled by the user or terminated by Inkolo Connect.

4.3

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate platform access where the user breaches these Terms, fails verification, fails to pay fees, misuses the platform, creates risk or acts unlawfully.

4.4

Inkolo Connect may change, suspend, remove or replace any platform service at any time for operational, legal, commercial, compliance, technical or security reasons.

5. User Obligations

5.1

Users must:

provide accurate, current and complete information;

keep their information updated;

maintain confidentiality of login credentials;

keep OTPs, PINs, passwords and devices secure;

use the platform lawfully;

comply with all applicable laws and regulations;

comply with these Terms and Service-Specific Terms;

comply with the Acceptable Use Policy;

treat other users respectfully;

not misuse the platform;

not provide false documents or false information;

not impersonate another person;

not interfere with platform security or operation.

5.2 Prohibited Use

Users may not:

allow unauthorised persons to access their account;

sell, transfer or share their account;

use the platform for fraud, scams or unlawful activity;

use the platform for money laundering or terrorist financing;

upload viruses, malware or harmful code;

attempt to access another user’s account;

reverse engineer or copy the platform;

scrape, harvest or misuse data;

harass, threaten or abuse others;

post unlawful, misleading or harmful content;

bypass fees, payment rules or platform controls;

damage the reputation of Inkolo Connect, Duranki, churches, communities or approved partners.

6. Third-Party Services

6.1

The platform may provide access to third-party services, including financial services, payment services, funeral cover, insurance products, bank products, wallet tools, airtime, data, electricity, merchant services, transport arrangements, job listings, marketplace listings, education services, connectivity services and other products or services.

6.2

Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect does not:

own the third-party product;

provide the third-party service;

underwrite insurance or funeral cover;

guarantee claims;

guarantee payment;

guarantee quality;

guarantee delivery;

guarantee employment;

guarantee transport safety;

guarantee any outcome;

endorse or recommend any specific provider as suitable for the user;

provide financial advice, insurance advice or legal advice.

6.3

Users agree to cooperate with Service Providers for onboarding, verification, FICA, KYC, POPIA consent, payment compliance, application processing, product activation, claims, support and any other required process.

6.4

The user’s contract for a third-party product or service is with the relevant Service Provider and not with Inkolo Connect, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

7. Financial Services, Funeral Cover and Insurance Disclaimer

7.1

Inkolo Connect is not a licensed bank, insurer, underwriter or FSP unless expressly confirmed in writing.

7.2

Where financial products, funeral cover, insurance products, bank accounts, loans, savings products, payment products, rewards, vouchers or other financial services are displayed on the platform, those products are provided by the relevant Service Provider.

7.3

Inkolo Connect may assist with administrative onboarding, document capture, payment facilitation, member dashboards, policy document display, support routing and communication.

7.4

Inkolo Connect does not decide whether:

a financial product is suitable for the user;

funeral cover should be approved;

a claim should be paid;

a claim should be rejected;

a loan should be granted;

a bank account should be opened;

a payment should be reversed;

an insurer or FSP should accept the user.

7.5

The user must read and accept the applicable Service Provider terms before using or relying on any third-party financial, funeral, insurance or banking product.

8. Cooling-Off Period

8.1

Where a cooling-off period applies under the Consumer Protection Act, Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, Service Provider terms or any other applicable law, the user may exercise that right according to the applicable process and time period.

8.2

Cooling-off rights may not apply to all services, digital services, used services, urgent services, completed services, certain financial products or services already activated with user consent.

8.3

Where a third-party Service Provider product is involved, cancellation and cooling-off rights are governed by that Service Provider’s terms and applicable law.

9. Subscription, Fees and Payments

9.1

Platform Costs may be included within a fixed Main Service Fee, service fee, subscription fee, commission, revenue share, product fee, administration fee or transaction fee.

9.2

Main Service Fees become payable according to the invoice, billing notice, product terms, service-specific terms, mandate, annexure or payment schedule applicable to the service.

9.3

Unless otherwise stated, fees become payable within 15 days of billing.

9.4

Users remain solely responsible for all fees, charges, taxes, penalties, reversals, chargebacks, bank fees, payment fees and service fees arising from their use of the platform.

9.5

Any variation to Platform Costs or service fees may be communicated in advance by email, platform notification, updated pricing schedule or service notice.

9.6

Where applicable, FSPs, Service Providers, communities, churches or approved partners may remit platform access fees, commissions, service fees or revenue-share amounts as agreed in separate written agreements.

9.7 Suspension for Non-Payment

Failure to pay any amount due may result in suspension, restriction or termination of platform access, service access, dashboard access, wallet functions or account functions.

9.8

Inkolo Connect may deduct applicable fees from payments, wallet balances, service provider remittances, commission amounts, revenue-share amounts or other amounts payable through the platform where legally and contractually permitted.

10. Wallet and Payment Facilitation

10.1

The platform may include wallet, payment request, card top-up, member-to-member transfer, QR payment, scan-to-pay, cash-out, settlement or other payment functionality.

10.2

Wallet and payment services are subject to separate Wallet Terms and Payment Terms.

10.3

Inkolo Connect may use banks, payment gateways, card acquirers, payment service providers, wallet providers or other Payment Partners.

10.4

Inkolo Connect is not liable for bank delays, failed payments, chargebacks, reversed card transactions, incorrect recipient details, incorrect bank details, payment partner failures or third-party processing delays outside its reasonable control.

10.5

Inkolo Connect may freeze, hold, reverse, delay or restrict payments where fraud, disputes, failed verification, chargebacks, compliance issues or suspicious activity are detected.

11. Service Levels

11.1

Inkolo Connect targets reasonable platform availability and may target 98% platform uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance, emergency maintenance, upgrades, network failures, third-party failures, cyber incidents, power failures, force majeure events and circumstances outside Inkolo Connect’s reasonable control.

11.2

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee uninterrupted, error-free or continuous access.

11.3

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss caused by downtime, delayed access, failed messages, delayed transactions, missed opportunities or service interruptions.

12. Intellectual Property

12.1

12.2

12.5

User content uploaded to the platform remains the responsibility of the user. By uploading content, the user grants Inkolo Connect a non-exclusive right to use, store, display, process, transmit and reproduce that content for platform operation, service delivery, moderation, reporting and support.

13. Data Protection

13.1

13.2

Inkolo Connect may process Personal Information for:

registration;

identity verification;

service activation;

member management;

community and church dashboards;

communication;

payments;

wallet transactions;

service provider onboarding;

funeral cover administration;

marketplace transactions;

fraud prevention;

compliance;

reporting;

support;

13.3

13.4

Users must not misuse, disclose, sell or unlawfully process another user’s Personal Information obtained through the platform.

14. Confidentiality

14.1

Each party must keep Confidential Information confidential.

14.2

Each party must:

disclose Confidential Information only to persons who need to know it;

ensure that any person receiving Confidential Information is bound by similar confidentiality obligations;

protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

14.3

These obligations do not apply to information that:

is in or comes into the public domain through no fault of the receiving party;

was independently developed or obtained without use of Confidential Information;

is disclosed with prior written approval;

is required to be disclosed by law, regulator, court order or lawful authority.

14.4

This confidentiality section survives termination of the Agreement.

15. Platform Content and User Content

15.1

Users are responsible for all content they upload, list, publish, send or share through the platform.

15.2

User content may include listings, job posts, messages, documents, images, profiles, reviews, ratings, comments, payment references and service information.

15.3

Users must ensure that their content is lawful, accurate, not misleading and does not infringe third-party rights.

15.4

Inkolo Connect may remove, hide, edit, restrict or report content that is unlawful, misleading, abusive, fraudulent, unsafe, offensive, discriminatory, reputationally harmful or in breach of these Terms.

16. Limitation of Liability

16.1

Inkolo Connect is not a licensed financial services provider, bank, insurer, underwriter, employer, transport operator or seller of user-listed goods unless expressly stated in writing.

16.2

Use of the platform does not create any fiduciary relationship, advisory relationship, employment relationship, agency relationship, partnership or joint venture between Inkolo Connect and any user.

16.3

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, its directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates are not liable for:

direct, indirect, special or consequential losses;

loss of profits;

loss of income;

loss of data;

loss of opportunity;

reputational harm;

emotional distress;

platform interruptions;

delayed access;

errors in platform access;

third-party service failures;

payment failures;

fraud by users;

disputes between users;

rejected claims;

failed jobs;

failed rides;

marketplace disputes;

Service Provider decisions;

any loss caused by user error, incorrect information or unlawful use.

16.4

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific service or transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

16.5

Inkolo Connect may receive commissions, platform fees, administration fees, referral fees, revenue share or other fees from certain third parties, Service Providers, churches, communities or platform partners. Such fees may be disclosed to users where required by law or policy.

17. Indemnity

17.1

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of the platform;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of any Service-Specific Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

false or incorrect information supplied by the user;

fraud or unlawful conduct;

misuse of another person’s information;

user content uploaded to the platform;

disputes with other users;

disputes with Service Providers;

claims by regulators, banks, insurers, employers, workers, buyers, sellers, drivers, passengers, beneficiaries, dependants or third parties.

17.2

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

18. Termination

18.1

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate access where a user:

breaches these Terms;

breaches Service-Specific Terms;

fails verification;

fails to pay fees;

misuses the platform;

provides false information;

commits fraud;

18.2

18.3

If the user cancels or ceases their Basic Service Package:

service access may be terminated;

outstanding fees remain due;

Wallet obligations may continue until settlement;

18.4

Termination of platform access does not automatically cancel third-party products, insurance policies, funeral cover, bank products, loans, debit orders, subscriptions or contracts with Service Providers. The user must cancel those directly according to the applicable Service Provider terms.

19. Dispute Resolution

19.1

Any dispute relating to these Terms must first be attempted to be resolved through good faith discussions between the parties.

19.2

If the dispute remains unresolved, the parties may refer the dispute to mediation or arbitration in South Africa under applicable arbitration rules.

19.3

Nothing in this clause prevents Inkolo Connect from approaching a court for urgent relief, debt recovery, interdictory relief, intellectual property protection, confidentiality breaches or unlawful platform use.

19.4

20. Privacy and Acceptable Use Policies

20.1

By accessing or using the platform, the user agrees to Inkolo Connect’s Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy and any other applicable platform policies.

20.2

The processing of Personal Information is subject to POPIA and the Privacy Policy.

20.3

Use of the platform must comply with all Inkolo Connect policies.

20.4

Inkolo Connect reserves the right to modify its policies and notify users by email, platform notification, website notice or other reasonable means.

20.5

Any violation of the Acceptable Use Policy may result in suspension or termination.

20.6

In the event of conflict between these Terms and policy documents, these Terms will prevail, unless a Service-Specific Term or legally required product document expressly overrides them for that specific service.

20.7

By registering a User Account, the user acknowledges and consents to Inkolo Connect’s applicable policies.

21. Service-Specific Terms

21.1

Certain services on the platform are subject to additional Service-Specific Terms.

These may include:

Wallet Service Terms;

Buy & Sell Service Terms;

Job Search Service Terms;

Catch a Ride Service Terms;

Funeral Cover Service Terms;

Airtime, Data and Electricity Terms;

Merchant Services Terms;

QR Payment Terms;

Voucher and Rewards Terms;

Education and Training Terms;

Loan or Banking Product Terms;

Community Dashboard Terms;

Service Provider Terms.

21.2

By using a specific service, the user agrees to the Service-Specific Terms for that service.

21.3

If there is a conflict between these general Terms and the Service-Specific Terms, the Service-Specific Terms will apply to that specific service.

22. Amendments to Terms

22.1

Inkolo Connect reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time.

22.2

Notice may be provided by email, platform notification, website notice, SMS, WhatsApp notification or other reasonable means.

22.3

Continued use of the platform after updated Terms are published or notified means that the user accepts the updated Terms.

22.4

If the user does not agree to the updated Terms, the user must stop using the platform and may request account closure, subject to outstanding obligations.

23. General Provisions

23.1 Entire Agreement

These Terms, together with applicable policies and Service-Specific Terms, constitute the entire agreement between Inkolo Connect and the user regarding platform use.

23.2 No Waiver

No waiver of any provision will be binding unless recorded in writing by Inkolo Connect.

23.3 Severability

If any provision is found to be invalid, unlawful or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain valid and enforceable.

23.4 Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

23.5 Force Majeure

Inkolo Connect is not liable for delays, failures or non-performance caused by events beyond its reasonable control, including power failures, network failures, internet outages, payment partner failures, bank failures, cyber incidents, natural disasters, strikes, riots, civil unrest, acts of government, regulatory action, pandemics, war, terrorism or other force majeure events.

23.6 Assignment

Inkolo Connect may assign, transfer or subcontract its rights and obligations under these Terms to an affiliate, successor, service provider, platform partner or purchaser of its business, provided this does not unlawfully prejudice the user.

23.7 Notices

Notices may be sent by email, SMS, WhatsApp, platform notification, website notice or any contact method provided by the user.

23.8 Survival

Clauses relating to confidentiality, intellectual property, limitation of liability, indemnity, payment obligations, data retention, dispute resolution and governing law survive termination of these Terms.

24. User Acceptance

By registering, accessing or using the Inkolo Connect platform, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

agree to be bound by these Terms;

understand that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator and not the provider of every service displayed on the platform;

understand that third-party products and services are governed by the relevant Service Provider terms;

accept responsibility for their own information, account security, service choices, payments and conduct;

MY COMMUNITY / CHURCH TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These My Community / Church Terms and Conditions apply to all users who join, access, participate in, communicate through, donate to, receive messages from, upload content to, or otherwise use a church, branch, ministry, community, group or faith-based community space made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By joining or using a church/community space on the platform, the user agrees to these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, Wallet Terms, Communication Terms, Service-Specific Terms and any additional rules published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, communication tools, wallet tools, payment tools and related services.

“My Community” means the platform feature that allows a user to select, join, view or participate in a church, branch, ministry, community, organisation, group or faith-based structure.

“Church” means any church, branch, ministry, denomination, congregation, fellowship, faith organisation, religious body or approved faith-based community made available on the platform.

“Community” means any group, organisation, church, branch, member body, association, programme or approved group structure hosted or represented on the platform.

“Church Leader” means a pastor, bishop, reverend, priest, minister, elder, deacon, administrator, church official, community leader or any person appointed or recognised by a church to perform leadership, administrative or spiritual functions.

“Member” means a user who joins, belongs to, follows, participates in or is linked to a Church or Community on the platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform, including a Member, Church Leader, administrator, visitor, donor, volunteer, service provider or community participant.

“Content” means any message, notice, announcement, document, image, video, post, comment, prayer request, donation reference, event notice, profile information or other material uploaded, shared or sent through the Platform.

2. Purpose of My Community

The My Community feature allows users to digitally connect with their selected Church or Community.

The feature may allow users to:

select their Church or branch;

view Church announcements;

receive messages from Church Leaders or administrators;

communicate with approved Church or Community groups;

access events;

access community services;

make payments, donations, tithes or offerings where available;

view documents or notices;

participate in community activities;

access approved services linked to the Church or Community.

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform and administrative tools only.

Inkolo Connect does not manage, control, govern, lead or represent the Church unless expressly agreed in writing.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator.

Inkolo Connect provides technology, dashboards, communication tools, member management tools, payment facilitation tools and service access tools.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for the internal affairs of any Church or Community.

Inkolo Connect does not:

appoint Church Leaders;

remove Church Leaders;

decide Church membership;

decide doctrine;

decide religious rules;

decide internal disputes;

manage Church governance;

control Church finances;

approve Church budgets;

audit Church funds;

supervise pastors, bishops or leaders;

decide disciplinary matters;

resolve family or member disputes;

guarantee the conduct of Church Leaders or Members;

guarantee that a Church is properly governed;

guarantee that donations, tithes or offerings are used correctly by the Church.

The relationship between a Member and their Church remains between the Member and that Church.

4. Church and Member Disputes

The user understands and agrees that Inkolo Connect is not responsible for disputes between:

Church Leaders;

pastors and members;

bishops and pastors;

members and members;

branches and head offices;

church committees and members;

churches and denominations;

donors and churches;

family members;

volunteers and church leadership;

service providers and churches;

different church factions or groups.

Internal church disputes must be resolved by the Church according to its own constitution, rules, disciplinary procedures, leadership structures, denomination rules, mediation processes or applicable law.

Inkolo Connect may assist with platform records where legally and practically possible, but Inkolo Connect is not a court, arbitrator, mediator, church tribunal, disciplinary body, spiritual authority or governance body.

5. No Responsibility for Church Governance

Each Church is responsible for its own governance, leadership, constitution, rules, policies, bank accounts, finances, member records, disciplinary processes, meetings, votes, mandates and decision-making.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

leadership elections;

church board disputes;

removals of leaders;

church splits;

church mergers;

disputes over church property;

disputes over church money;

disputes over church assets;

disputes over mandates;

disputes over membership rights;

disputes over branch control;

disagreements over doctrine or religious interpretation;

disciplinary action taken by the Church.

Where there is a leadership dispute, Inkolo Connect may restrict dashboard changes, payment changes, administrator changes or communication rights until proper written authority or legal clarity is provided.

6. Church Administrators and Platform Access

Church administrators may be given access to manage certain Church or Community functions on the platform.

A Church is responsible for ensuring that its appointed administrators are authorised, trustworthy and properly mandated.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for any loss, dispute, incorrect message, incorrect record, unauthorised instruction, deleted content, incorrect member update, financial dispute or internal conflict caused by a Church administrator.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or restrict administrator access if there is suspected fraud, conflict, unauthorised use, leadership dispute, legal risk, security risk or breach of these Terms.

7. Member Selection of Church and Branch

Users may be required to select their Church, branch, congregation or community from a dropdown list or other platform process.

The user is responsible for selecting the correct Church or branch.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for incorrect communication, incorrect membership allocation, missed announcements, wrong community access, wrong payment allocation or disputes caused by the user selecting the wrong Church or branch.

Inkolo Connect may correct or update a user’s Church or branch where instructed by an authorised Church administrator or where required for platform accuracy.

8. Communications and Announcements

The platform may allow Church Leaders and administrators to send messages, announcements, event notices, reminders, documents or other communications to Members.

Churches and Church administrators are responsible for the content they send.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that all messages are accurate, lawful, appropriate, approved by the Church, or received by all Members.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

incorrect announcements;

offensive messages;

misleading messages;

unauthorised messages;

late messages;

failed delivery;

missed events;

communication disputes;

internal church politics communicated through the platform.

Inkolo Connect may remove or restrict communications that are unlawful, abusive, fraudulent, defamatory, discriminatory, threatening, unsafe, reputationally harmful or in breach of these Terms.

9. User Content and Conduct

Users are responsible for all Content they post, upload, send or share through My Community.

Users must not post or send Content that is:

unlawful;

false;

defamatory;

abusive;

threatening;

discriminatory;

hateful;

sexually explicit;

violent;

fraudulent;

misleading;

invasive of privacy;

damaging to another person’s reputation;

harmful to children;

designed to create conflict or unrest;

in breach of Church or Community rules;

in breach of Inkolo Connect policies.

Inkolo Connect may remove Content, restrict communication tools or suspend users where Content creates legal, safety, reputational, operational or community risk.

10. Respectful Community Participation

Users must participate respectfully and responsibly.

Users may not use My Community to:

harass Church Leaders or Members;

threaten or intimidate others;

spread false allegations;

incite conflict;

publish private disputes;

promote hate speech;

attack another Church or denomination;

misuse religious content to exploit or manipulate others;

solicit money fraudulently;

impersonate a Church Leader;

interfere with Church operations;

damage the reputation of Inkolo Connect, a Church, Community or another Member.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or remove users who misuse the platform.

11. Donations, Tithes, Offerings and Payments

The platform may allow Members to make donations, tithes, offerings, pledges, event payments, community contributions or other payments to a Church or Community.

Unless expressly stated otherwise, such payments are made to the relevant Church or Community and not to Inkolo Connect.

Inkolo Connect may act only as a payment facilitator, wallet provider, payment channel or technology provider.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for how a Church uses donations, tithes, offerings or contributions once paid to the Church or allocated to the Church wallet/account.

The Church is responsible for:

its own financial governance;

donation records;

financial reporting;

tax treatment;

receipts;

accounting;

use of funds;

member queries;

refund decisions;

compliance with its own constitution and applicable law.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes about Church funds, missing donations, alleged misuse of funds, leadership disputes over money, donor dissatisfaction or how the Church spends money.

12. Payment Errors and Refunds

Users are responsible for checking the amount, recipient, Church, branch and payment reason before making a payment.

Inkolo Connect is not liable where a user pays the wrong Church, wrong branch, wrong amount or wrong reference because of user error.

Inkolo Connect may assist with payment records or payment reversal requests where possible, but refunds are not guaranteed.

Refunds of donations, tithes, offerings or Church payments are subject to the Church’s own rules, applicable law, payment partner rules and platform payment terms.

13. No Guarantee of Church Services

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Church will provide any particular service, support, counselling, pastoral care, event, funeral support, welfare support, prayer support, membership benefit or community benefit.

Any spiritual, pastoral, welfare, counselling or community support provided by a Church is the responsibility of that Church and not Inkolo Connect.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disappointment, disagreement, emotional distress, religious disputes or pastoral disputes arising from Church services or Church conduct.

14. Events and Activities

The platform may display Church or Community events.

The Church or Community is responsible for organising, managing, controlling and supervising its own events.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

cancelled events;

event injuries;

event accidents;

transport to or from events;

food safety;

crowd control;

security;

venue safety;

child supervision;

lost property;

event disputes;

incorrect event information.

Users attend Church or Community events at their own risk, unless otherwise stated in writing by the event organiser.

15. Volunteers and Community Work

The platform may allow Churches or Communities to coordinate volunteers, projects, outreach work or community support.

Any volunteer arrangement is between the Church or Community and the volunteer.

Inkolo Connect is not the employer, supervisor, labour broker, recruiter or safety officer for volunteers or community workers.

The Church or Community is responsible for safety, instructions, supervision, permissions, tools, protective equipment, transport and any legal obligations relating to volunteers or community work.

16. Children and Vulnerable Persons

Churches and Communities are responsible for their own safeguarding policies, child-protection processes, consent requirements and supervision of minors or vulnerable persons.

Users must not upload, share or misuse images, personal information or private details of children or vulnerable persons without proper consent.

Inkolo Connect may remove content or suspend users where child safety or vulnerable-person protection concerns arise.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for the Church’s failure to supervise, protect or safeguard minors or vulnerable persons.

17. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

user registration;

Church and branch allocation;

member records;

communication;

event participation;

payment processing;

wallet services;

service activation;

community reporting;

support;

security;

fraud prevention;

legal compliance.

Personal information may be shared with the selected Church, authorised Church administrators, service providers, payment partners, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse personal information of Church Leaders, Members, donors, children, volunteers or community participants obtained through the platform.

18. Privacy Between Members

Members must respect the privacy of other Members.

Users may not:

copy member lists without authority;

use member information for personal business without consent;

send spam;

share private phone numbers unlawfully;

publish private disputes;

expose personal information;

use Church data for political, commercial or unauthorised purposes;

contact Members outside the purpose for which information was provided.

Breach of privacy may result in suspension, removal and legal action.

19. Church Data and Records

The platform may assist Churches with member records, attendance records, payment records, communication records, event records or service records.

The Church remains responsible for ensuring that its own records are correct and legally maintained.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for internal Church disputes based on member lists, historical records, leadership lists, branch lists, attendance records or governance records unless the error was directly caused by Inkolo Connect’s proven platform fault.

Inkolo Connect may archive, correct, restrict or preserve data where there is a legal, security, compliance or dispute-related reason to do so.

20. Moderation Rights

Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove Content;

restrict user access;

suspend communication tools;

suspend Church administrator rights;

restrict payment functions;

hide disputed Church information;

request proof of authority;

block abusive users;

preserve records;

report unlawful conduct;

suspend a Church or Community space.

These actions may be taken where Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, safety, security, reputational, governance, community or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss, embarrassment, income loss, donation loss, reputation loss or opportunity loss caused by moderation actions taken in good faith.

21. Leadership Disputes

Where two or more people claim authority over a Church or branch, Inkolo Connect may require written proof of authority before making platform changes.

Proof may include:

Church constitution;

board resolution;

letter from recognised denominational authority;

mandate;

court order;

signed instruction from authorised office bearers;

minutes of meeting;

legal opinion;

any other document reasonably required.

Until Inkolo Connect is satisfied, it may freeze administrator changes, payment changes, dashboard changes, communication rights or Church profile edits.

Inkolo Connect does not decide who the rightful Church Leader is. Inkolo Connect only applies reasonable platform risk controls.

22. No Religious or Doctrinal Responsibility

Inkolo Connect does not control or determine religious doctrine, sermons, beliefs, teachings, rituals, prayers, spiritual practices, counselling, pastoral guidance or theological interpretation.

Any religious, spiritual, pastoral or doctrinal content is the responsibility of the relevant Church or user who provides it.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for religious disagreement, spiritual harm claims, doctrinal disputes, offence, disappointment or disagreement arising from Church content or conduct.

23. No Endorsement

The presence of a Church or Community on the platform does not mean Inkolo Connect guarantees, endorses or certifies that Church, its leadership, doctrine, governance, finances, services, safety, statements or conduct.

Users must make their own decisions about joining, supporting, donating to or participating in a Church or Community.

24. Complaints

Complaints about Church leadership, Church conduct, doctrine, membership, discipline, donations, events, pastoral care, governance or Church disputes must be directed to the relevant Church or denominational authority.

Complaints about technical platform issues may be submitted to Inkolo Connect support.

Inkolo Connect may review complaints that relate to platform misuse, fraud, abuse, unlawful content, privacy breaches or safety concerns.

Inkolo Connect is not obliged to resolve internal Church disputes.

25. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, injury, expense or liability arising from:

internal Church disputes;

leadership fights;

member disagreements;

branch disputes;

denominational disputes;

governance disputes;

Church disciplinary actions;

Church membership decisions;

removal from a Church or group;

doctrine or religious disputes;

pastoral advice or counselling;

donations, tithes or offerings;

alleged misuse of Church funds;

Church administrator actions;

unauthorised Church messages;

user-generated content;

defamatory statements by users;

harassment between Members;

event incidents;

volunteer incidents;

privacy breaches by users;

wrong Church or branch selection by a user;

disputes between Church Leaders and Members;

reputational harm caused by Church or Member conduct;

platform restrictions imposed in good faith.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of donations, loss of opportunity, reputational harm, emotional distress or spiritual distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific platform service giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

26. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s participation in My Community;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

the user’s content, messages or posts;

the user’s conduct towards Church Leaders or Members;

defamatory, abusive or unlawful statements made by the user;

misuse of personal information;

incorrect Church or branch selection;

disputes with Church Leaders;

disputes with other Members;

disputes about donations, tithes, offerings or payments;

disputes about Church membership or discipline;

claims arising from Church events, volunteer work or community activities;

any internal Church or Community dispute involving the user.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

27. Suspension and Removal

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or remove a user from My Community where the user:

breaches these Terms;

abuses another user;

posts harmful or unlawful content;

misuses personal information;

commits fraud;

creates community conflict through platform misuse;

impersonates a Church Leader;

interferes with Church administration;

creates legal, financial, safety, reputational or compliance risk;

is removed by an authorised Church administrator, subject to platform rules.

Suspension from the platform does not necessarily mean the user has been removed from Church membership. Church membership remains a matter between the user and the Church.

28. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these My Community / Church Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of My Community after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

29. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

30. Acceptance

By joining or using My Community, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator only;

accept that Inkolo Connect is not responsible for internal Church disputes;

accept that disputes between Church Leaders and Members must be resolved by the Church or relevant authority;

accept that donations, tithes and offerings are made to the relevant Church or Community, not to Inkolo Connect unless expressly stated;

accept responsibility for their own conduct, content, payments and interactions;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from Church and Member disputes.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user joins or selects a Church/Community:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the My Community / Church Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect only provides the digital platform and is not responsible for internal church disputes, leadership disagreements, member fights, church governance, doctrine, discipline, donations, tithes, offerings, events, pastoral matters or disputes between church leaders and members.

Suggested Short Warning on My Community Page

Inkolo Connect provides the digital community platform only. Church governance, leadership, doctrine, donations, events, discipline and member disputes remain the responsibility of the relevant Church or Community. Please act respectfully and report platform misuse.

legalAcceptances.documentSnapshot:
INKOLO REFERRAL SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who participate in, access, share, use, submit, receive or claim rewards, benefits, incentives, commissions, vouchers, wallet credits or any other value through the Inkolo Referral Service made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By participating in the Inkolo Referral Service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Community Terms, Acceptable Use Policy and any additional service-specific rules published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet tools, payment tools, referral tools, member profiles and service modules.

“Referral Service” means the feature that allows eligible users to refer new members, churches, communities, service users, merchants, vendors, customers or other participants to Inkolo Connect or approved platform services.

“Referrer” means a user who shares a referral link, referral code, invitation or approved referral process.

“Referred User” means a person, member, church, organisation, merchant, vendor or customer who joins, registers, activates or purchases a service through a referral.

“Referral Reward” means any reward, incentive, commission, voucher, wallet credit, discount, benefit, points, coins, cash-equivalent value or other reward that may be offered for a successful referral.

“Qualifying Referral” means a referral that meets all requirements set by Inkolo Connect for that specific referral campaign, service, product or reward.

“Referral Campaign” means a specific referral programme, promotion, offer, service referral or reward structure made available by Inkolo Connect from time to time.

“Service Provider” means any third-party provider, insurer, bank, payment provider, merchant, vendor, funeral cover provider, VAS provider, training provider, connectivity provider, property provider, employer, transport provider or other approved partner whose service may be referred through the platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform or participates in the Referral Service.

2. Purpose of the Referral Service

The Referral Service allows eligible users to invite others to join Inkolo Connect, activate services, join communities, use approved products or participate in platform programmes.

The Referral Service may apply to:

member registrations;

church registrations;

branch registrations;

community registrations;

funeral cover activations;

wallet activations;

VAS purchases;

merchant registrations;

vendor registrations;

job listings;

property listings;

connectivity services;

education services;

approved third-party services;

any other referral campaign approved by Inkolo Connect.

Inkolo Connect may change, suspend, limit, replace or terminate any referral campaign at any time.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect provides the digital referral tool and may record, track, verify, approve, reject or reward referrals.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that:

every referral will be accepted;

every referral will qualify for a reward;

a referred person will register;

a referred person will activate a service;

a referred person will remain active;

a third-party Service Provider will approve the referred user;

a referred service will be successful;

a reward will be payable before verification is complete;

a referral reward will remain available permanently.

Inkolo Connect may rely on platform records, service provider confirmations, payment records, activation records and fraud checks when deciding whether a referral qualifies.

4. Eligibility

To participate in the Referral Service, a user may be required to:

be registered on the Platform;

have an active Inkolo Connect account;

have verified their mobile number;

have completed identity verification where required;

have accepted these Terms;

have complied with all platform rules;

have an active Wallet where rewards are paid to Wallet;

not be suspended, banned or under investigation;

comply with referral campaign rules.

Inkolo Connect may refuse participation where a user fails verification, provides false information, abuses the referral system or creates risk.

5. Qualifying Referrals

A referral will only qualify for a reward if it meets all requirements of the relevant referral campaign.

Requirements may include:

the Referred User must be new to the Platform;

the Referred User must register using the correct referral link, code or process;

the Referred User must complete verification;

the Referred User must activate the relevant service;

the Referred User must make a qualifying payment, where applicable;

the Referred User must remain active for a minimum period;

the Referred User must not cancel, reverse or refund the service;

the Referred User must not be fraudulent, duplicated or self-referred;

the referral must be properly tracked by the Platform;

Inkolo Connect must approve the referral as valid.

Inkolo Connect’s platform records will be the primary record for referral tracking.

6. Non-Qualifying Referrals

A referral will not qualify for a reward where:

the Referred User was already registered;

the Referred User used another referral code;

the referral was not tracked correctly due to user error;

the Referrer referred themselves;

the Referrer created fake accounts;

the Referred User failed verification;

the Referred User cancelled the service;

the payment was reversed or refunded;

fraud or abuse is suspected;

the referral was generated through spam;

the referral breached these Terms;

the referral campaign had ended;

the maximum reward limit was reached;

the Service Provider declined or cancelled the service;

Inkolo Connect reasonably determines that the referral is invalid.

Inkolo Connect is not obliged to pay a reward for non-qualifying referrals.

7. Referral Rewards

Referral Rewards may be paid or issued in the form of:

Wallet credit;

cash-equivalent platform credit;

voucher;

discount;

reward points;

Inkolo coins or community coins;

airtime, data or VAS benefits;

service discount;

commission;

once-off incentive;

recurring incentive;

any other approved reward.

The type, value, timing and conditions of the reward will be determined by the applicable referral campaign.

Inkolo Connect may change reward values, reward types and campaign rules at any time.

8. No Automatic Right to a Reward

A referral does not automatically create a right to a reward.

A reward becomes payable only when Inkolo Connect confirms that the referral is a Qualifying Referral and that all campaign conditions have been met.

Inkolo Connect may delay, hold, reverse, cancel or reject a reward where verification, fraud checks, chargeback checks, service activation checks, payment checks or Service Provider confirmations are required.

9. Reward Payment Timing

Referral Rewards may be delayed to allow for:

verification of the Referrer;

verification of the Referred User;

service activation confirmation;

payment confirmation;

fraud checks;

duplicate-account checks;

chargeback checks;

refund checks;

cancellation checks;

Service Provider confirmation;

Wallet settlement;

compliance checks.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee instant referral reward payments.

Where a referral relates to a third-party service, reward payment may depend on confirmation and settlement from the relevant Service Provider.

10. Wallet Rewards

Where Referral Rewards are paid into the user’s Wallet, the Wallet Terms apply.

Wallet rewards may be subject to:

Wallet verification;

transaction limits;

withdrawal limits;

cash-out rules;

cash-out waiting periods;

fees;

fraud checks;

reversal rights;

compliance checks;

account restrictions.

Inkolo Connect may reverse a Wallet reward if the referral later becomes invalid, fraudulent, reversed, refunded, cancelled or disputed.

11. Referral Codes and Links

Inkolo Connect may provide users with referral codes, links, QR codes, invite messages or other referral tools.

Users are responsible for using the correct referral method.

Inkolo Connect is not liable where a referral is not tracked because:

the wrong code was used;

the link was broken;

the Referred User did not use the referral process;

the Referred User registered independently;

the Referred User used another Referrer’s code;

the user shared an incorrect link;

technical settings prevented tracking;

the campaign had expired.

Inkolo Connect may refuse manual referral claims where the referral was not tracked by the Platform.

12. Prohibited Referral Conduct

Users may not:

create fake accounts;

refer themselves;

refer the same person multiple times;

use stolen identity information;

use bots, scripts or automated referral tools;

spam referral links;

mislead people about Inkolo Connect;

promise rewards not approved by Inkolo Connect;

make false claims about services;

impersonate Inkolo Connect;

impersonate a church or community leader;

pressure or harass people to register;

use unlawful marketing methods;

advertise referral links on prohibited platforms;

use referral links for scams;

abuse vouchers, rewards or Wallet credits;

manipulate the system;

use referrals for money laundering or fraud.

Breach of this clause may result in reward cancellation, account suspension, Wallet restriction, permanent ban and legal action.

13. No Misrepresentation

Referrers must be honest when promoting Inkolo Connect or any service on the Platform.

Referrers may not claim that:

Inkolo Connect guarantees income;

all users will receive money;

services are free when they are not;

funeral claims are guaranteed;

jobs are guaranteed;

loans are guaranteed;

bank accounts are guaranteed;

property rentals or sales are guaranteed;

transport is guaranteed;

any third-party service is approved for all users;

rewards are guaranteed before qualification.

Any information shared by a Referrer must be accurate, lawful and not misleading.

14. Third-Party Service Referrals

Some referrals may relate to services provided by third-party Service Providers.

These may include:

funeral cover;

banking products;

wallet services;

payment services;

VAS services;

connectivity services;

education services;

property services;

merchant services;

insurance products;

employment-related services;

any other approved third-party service.

The contract for the referred product or service is between the Referred User and the relevant Service Provider.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for the Service Provider’s approval, rejection, pricing, service delivery, claims, refunds, cancellations or product performance.

Referral Rewards linked to third-party services may depend on Service Provider confirmation and payment.

15. Churches, Communities and Leaders

Where referrals are made within a church, branch, community or faith-based environment, users must act respectfully and transparently.

Church Leaders, pastors, bishops, administrators or community leaders may not misuse their position to pressure members into registering or buying services.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes between churches, leaders, members or communities arising from referral activity.

Referral disputes inside a church or community must be resolved by the relevant church or community, unless the dispute concerns platform misuse, fraud or a technical issue.

16. Referral Disputes

Users must report referral disputes through the Platform support process.

Referral disputes may include:

missing referral credit;

wrong Referrer;

duplicate referral claims;

delayed rewards;

rejected referrals;

cancelled rewards;

reward amount disputes.

Inkolo Connect may review platform records, registration timestamps, referral codes, activation records, payment records, device records, Service Provider confirmations and fraud checks.

Inkolo Connect’s decision on referral qualification and reward allocation will be final, unless a clear platform error is proven.

17. Fraud Prevention

Inkolo Connect may monitor referral activity for fraud, abuse, suspicious behaviour, duplicate accounts, fake registrations, identity misuse, payment abuse, chargebacks and system manipulation.

Inkolo Connect may:

delay rewards;

hold rewards;

reverse rewards;

cancel rewards;

request verification;

suspend accounts;

restrict Wallets;

block referral codes;

terminate referral access;

report unlawful activity.

Users who abuse the Referral Service may lose all pending and future rewards.

18. Tax Responsibility

Referral Rewards may have tax consequences.

Users are responsible for any tax, declaration, reporting or legal obligations arising from Referral Rewards, commissions, incentives, vouchers, credits or benefits received through the Referral Service.

Inkolo Connect does not provide tax advice and is not responsible for a user’s tax obligations.

19. No Employment, Agency or Franchise Relationship

Participation in the Referral Service does not make the user an employee, agent, representative, broker, franchisee, partner, contractor or authorised salesperson of Inkolo Connect unless a separate written agreement is signed.

Users may not bind Inkolo Connect, sign agreements on behalf of Inkolo Connect, make promises on behalf of Inkolo Connect or represent themselves as authorised employees or agents unless expressly authorised in writing.

20. Marketing and Communication Rules

Users must comply with all applicable laws and platform rules when sharing referral links or messages.

Users may not send referral messages in a way that is unlawful, abusive, misleading, unsolicited, excessive or harmful to Inkolo Connect’s reputation.

Users must stop contacting any person who asks not to be contacted.

Inkolo Connect may restrict or suspend referral access where a user’s marketing creates complaints, reputational risk, legal risk or community conflict.

21. Campaign Changes and Termination

Inkolo Connect may at any time:

change a referral campaign;

change reward amounts;

change reward types;

introduce new conditions;

limit the number of rewards;

end a campaign;

suspend a campaign;

exclude certain users;

exclude certain services;

cancel referral rewards for abuse or risk reasons.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss of expected rewards, income, opportunity or benefit caused by campaign changes or termination.

22. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

referral tracking;

user registration;

identity verification;

reward allocation;

Wallet payments;

fraud prevention;

duplicate-account detection;

campaign reporting;

Service Provider confirmation;

support;

compliance;

dispute resolution.

Users may not misuse another person’s personal information for referral purposes.

A Referrer must not register another person without consent or provide another person’s information without permission.

23. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that the Referral Service will always be available, uninterrupted, accurate, secure or error-free.

The service may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

network failures;

payment partner failures;

Service Provider delays;

cyber incidents;

power failures;

fraud controls;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed referrals, delayed rewards, failed tracking or lost opportunities caused by downtime or technical issues, unless caused by proven gross negligence.

24. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

rejected referrals;

delayed rewards;

cancelled rewards;

incorrect referral codes;

untracked referrals;

duplicate referral claims;

reward reversals;

fraudulent referrals;

Service Provider rejection;

Service Provider delays;

campaign changes;

campaign termination;

user misrepresentation;

tax obligations;

disputes between Referrers and Referred Users;

disputes inside churches or communities;

technical failures;

platform downtime;

lost income;

lost opportunity;

reputational harm.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific referral transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

25. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s participation in the Referral Service;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

fraudulent referrals;

fake accounts;

unauthorised marketing;

misleading statements;

spam or unlawful communication;

misuse of personal information;

tax disputes;

disputes with Referred Users;

disputes with churches, communities or Service Providers;

claims caused by promises or statements made by the user;

any unlawful or abusive use of the Referral Service.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

26. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to the Referral Service where the user:

breaches these Terms;

commits fraud;

creates fake accounts;

misuses referral codes;

misleads others;

spams referral links;

abuses rewards;

fails verification;

creates legal, financial, operational, reputational or compliance risk;

is suspended from the Platform.

Inkolo Connect may also cancel pending rewards, reverse paid rewards or block future referral participation where abuse or invalid referral activity is detected.

27. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these Referral Service Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Referral Service after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

28. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

29. Acceptance

By using the Inkolo Referral Service, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that referral rewards are not automatic;

accept that referrals must qualify before rewards are paid;

accept that Inkolo Connect may reject, delay, hold, reverse or cancel rewards where required;

accept responsibility for truthful and lawful referral conduct;

accept responsibility for any tax or legal obligations arising from rewards;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from referral misuse, misrepresentation, fraud, disputes or unlawful conduct.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user activates or uses the Inkolo Referral Service:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that referral rewards are not automatic and will only be paid if the referral qualifies under Inkolo Connect’s campaign rules. I agree not to create fake accounts, mislead people, spam referral links or misuse the referral system.

Suggested Referral Share Warning

Before a user shares a referral link:

Please only share your referral link honestly and respectfully. Do not promise guaranteed income, guaranteed rewards, guaranteed services, guaranteed claims, guaranteed jobs or guaranteed approval. Referral rewards are subject to verification and Inkolo Connect approval.

Suggested Reward Pending Notice

When a reward is pending:

Your referral reward is pending verification. Rewards may be delayed, rejected, reversed or cancelled if the referred user does not qualify, cancels, fails verification, reverses payment or if suspicious activity is detected.

Suggested Reward Reversal Notice

If a reward is reversed:

This referral reward has been reversed because the referral no longer qualifies under the Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions. This may happen where a service is cancelled, payment is reversed, verification fails, duplicate accounts are detected or suspicious activity is identified.
