WALLET SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Wallet Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, activate, top up, receive, transfer, send, spend, cash out or otherwise use the Wallet service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Wallet service, the user agrees to be bound by these Wallet Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Community Rules, Service Terms, Payment Terms, and any additional rules, limits, fees, notices or instructions published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet systems, payment tools, communication tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community, church and member-management platform operated on or through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital service, dashboard, app, website, wallet, payment channel, communication channel or service module.

“Wallet” means the digital wallet facility made available to registered users through the platform.

“Wallet Balance” means the value reflected in a user’s Wallet, subject to successful funding, transaction processing, fraud checks, reversals, fees, refunds, holds, limits and compliance reviews.

“User” means any person who registers for, accesses or uses the Wallet.

“Member-to-Member Transfer” means a transfer of available Wallet funds from one registered member’s Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

“Top-Up” means adding money to a Wallet using an approved payment method, including bank card payment where available.

“Cash-Out” means a request by a user to withdraw available Wallet funds from the Wallet to a bank account registered or nominated by that user.

“Payment Partner” means any bank, card acquirer, payment service provider, payment gateway, wallet provider, settlement provider, banking partner, compliance provider or other third-party payment processor used by Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

“Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or official South African public holiday.

2. Nature of the Wallet Service

The Wallet is a digital payment and value-transfer feature made available through the platform.

The Wallet may allow users to:

receive funds into their Wallet;

send funds from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

pay for approved goods or services on the platform;

receive payment from other members;

top up their Wallet using a bank card or other approved payment method;

request a cash-out to their bank account;

view Wallet transaction history;

use Wallet funds for approved platform services.

The Wallet is provided as a platform payment tool and not as a bank account, savings account, deposit account, credit facility, investment product or interest-bearing account.

No interest will be paid to users on Wallet balances unless expressly stated in writing.

3. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the Wallet as part of the platform’s digital service environment.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may use third-party Payment Partners to process card payments, Wallet transactions, settlements, reversals, cash-outs, refunds, fraud screening and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for delays, failures, reversals, declines, chargebacks, bank errors, card network issues, payment gateway failures or third-party processing problems outside their reasonable control.

Where required, Wallet services may be provided through, supported by or settled through approved Payment Partners.

4. Wallet Activation

To activate or use the Wallet, a user may be required to:

register on the platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide personal information;

accept these Wallet Terms;

pass fraud and compliance checks;

provide bank account details for cash-outs;

provide any additional documents requested by Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse, delay, suspend or deactivate Wallet access where verification is incomplete, failed, suspicious or required by law, risk policy or a Payment Partner.

5. Member-to-Member Wallet Transfers

The Wallet may allow a user to send money from their Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

The sender is responsible for ensuring that:

the recipient is the correct person;

the amount is correct;

the reason for the transfer is lawful;

the recipient details are correct;

they have sufficient available Wallet balance;

the transaction is not fraudulent, unlawful or prohibited.

Once a Member-to-Member Transfer has been submitted, it may not be reversible unless Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner determines that a reversal is permitted, required or necessary due to fraud, error, dispute, chargeback, system failure, compliance review or applicable law.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee recovery of funds sent to the wrong recipient.

Users must not use Member-to-Member Transfers for illegal activity, fraud, scams, gambling, money laundering, prohibited goods, unauthorised business activity or any other unlawful purpose.

6. Top-Up Using Bank Card

Users may be able to top up their Wallet using a bank card, subject to availability and approval by the Payment Partner.

A Top-Up may be declined, delayed, reversed or blocked where:

the card is declined by the issuing bank;

the card details are incorrect;

the card is expired;

there are insufficient funds;

3D Secure or OTP verification fails;

the transaction is flagged as suspicious;

the transaction exceeds platform limits;

the transaction breaches Payment Partner rules;

the cardholder disputes the transaction;

the transaction is subject to chargeback;

Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner suspects fraud or unauthorised use.

A Wallet balance may only be credited once the Top-Up has been successfully authorised and processed.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may reverse or debit a Wallet balance if a card Top-Up is later reversed, charged back, rejected, refunded, identified as fraudulent or not finally settled.

Card payments may be subject to chargeback rules imposed by card schemes, banks and card issuers. A chargeback may result in a reversal or deduction from the user’s Wallet balance, even after the Wallet was initially credited.

7. Cash-Out to Bank Account

Users may request to cash out available Wallet funds to their own bank account, subject to platform rules, verification, limits, fraud checks, compliance checks and Payment Partner processing.

A Cash-Out may only be made to a bank account accepted by the platform and/or Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require the bank account to be in the same name as the registered Wallet user.

The user is responsible for ensuring that the bank account details provided are complete, correct and active.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for loss, delay or failed payment caused by incorrect bank account details supplied by the user.

8. Cash-Out Waiting Period

Cash-Out requests are subject to a waiting period of up to 5 business days from the date the Cash-Out request is approved for processing.

The 5-business-day period is a standard waiting period and may be used for:

fraud checks;

card chargeback risk checks;

payment settlement confirmation;

bank processing;

Payment Partner processing;

compliance checks;

identity verification;

anti-money-laundering controls;

dispute checks;

system and reconciliation checks.

The 5-business-day period does not include Saturdays, Sundays or South African public holidays.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may extend the Cash-Out period where:

fraud is suspected;

the user fails verification;

bank details are incorrect;

the Payment Partner requires additional checks;

a dispute has been raised;

a chargeback risk exists;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the user’s Wallet is restricted;

the user’s account is under review;

delays occur outside the control of Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that every Cash-Out will be completed within 5 business days where additional verification, fraud checks, compliance checks or third-party delays apply.

9. Available Balance

A user may only spend, transfer or cash out the available Wallet balance.

The available Wallet balance may be different from the displayed balance where funds are:

pending;

under review;

subject to settlement;

subject to chargeback risk;

frozen;

reversed;

refunded;

disputed;

subject to fees;

subject to compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may correct Wallet balances where an error, duplicate credit, failed payment, reversal, chargeback, refund or system issue occurred.

10. Fees

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may charge fees for Wallet services.

Fees may apply to:

Wallet Top-Ups;

Member-to-Member Transfers;

Cash-Outs;

failed Cash-Outs;

reversed transactions;

card processing;

refunds;

chargebacks;

account verification;

platform service use;

any other Wallet-related service.

Applicable fees may be displayed in the app, dashboard, pricing schedule or transaction screen.

By confirming a transaction, the user agrees to pay the applicable fee.

Fees may be deducted from the Wallet balance or transaction amount before settlement.

11. Transaction Limits

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may apply limits to Wallet use.

Limits may include:

minimum Top-Up amount;

maximum Top-Up amount;

daily transaction limit;

monthly transaction limit;

maximum Wallet balance;

maximum Cash-Out amount;

minimum Cash-Out amount;

Member-to-Member Transfer limits;

card payment limits;

risk-based limits.

Limits may differ depending on verification level, user type, risk profile, community, service, Payment Partner requirements or regulatory requirements.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may change Wallet limits at any time.

12. No Credit, Loans or Overdraft

The Wallet does not provide credit, loans, overdraft or advance facilities unless a separate approved service is expressly provided under separate terms.

Users may not spend more than their available Wallet balance.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse transactions where there are insufficient available funds.

13. Payment Reversals and Chargebacks

A transaction may be reversed, deducted, frozen or adjusted where:

a cardholder disputes a Top-Up;

a chargeback is received;

fraud is suspected;

payment was made in error;

duplicate credit occurred;

the Payment Partner reverses the transaction;

a refund is required;

a transaction is unlawful;

a transaction breaches these Terms;

Duranki or Inkolo Connect is required to do so by law, court order, bank, regulator or Payment Partner.

If a user has already spent or withdrawn funds that are later reversed, the user remains liable to repay the amount to Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may recover the amount from future Wallet balances, withhold future payments, suspend the Wallet or pursue recovery by lawful means.

14. Incorrect Transfers

Users are responsible for checking recipient details before sending money.

If a user sends money to the wrong member, Duranki and Inkolo Connect will not be liable for the loss.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may attempt to assist, but do not guarantee recovery.

A recipient who receives money in error must return it immediately when requested.

Failure to return money received in error may result in Wallet suspension, account restriction and legal action.

15. Prohibited Wallet Use

Users may not use the Wallet for:

fraud;

scams;

money laundering;

terrorist financing;

illegal gambling;

unlawful loans;

prohibited goods or services;

stolen goods;

fake payment confirmations;

identity theft;

unauthorised card use;

chargeback abuse;

illegal investment schemes;

pyramid schemes;

bribery or corruption;

evading platform rules;

any unlawful or suspicious activity.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may report suspicious transactions to banks, Payment Partners, regulators or law enforcement where required or permitted.

16. Account Security

Users are responsible for keeping their account, PIN, password, OTP, device, SIM card and login details secure.

Users must not share:

passwords;

PINs;

OTPs;

card details;

login details;

device access;

wallet access;

verification codes.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by the user sharing security details, losing their device, allowing unauthorised access, falling for scams or failing to secure their account.

Users must immediately report suspected unauthorised access or fraud.

17. Wallet Freezing, Holds and Restrictions

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, hold, delay, reverse, restrict or suspend Wallet funds or Wallet access where:

fraud is suspected;

suspicious activity is detected;

a chargeback is received;

a user fails verification;

a dispute is raised;

a law, regulator, bank or Payment Partner requires it;

the user breaches these Terms;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the Wallet is used for prohibited activity;

there is a technical or reconciliation issue;

the user provides false or incomplete information;

risk controls require review.

A Wallet freeze, hold or restriction does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect accepts liability to the user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep the Wallet restricted until the issue has been investigated and resolved to their satisfaction.

18. Refunds

Refunds may be processed where allowed by the relevant service terms, merchant rules, Payment Partner rules or applicable law.

Refunds are not automatic and may be subject to:

service-specific refund rules;

merchant approval;

fraud checks;

payment reversal rules;

chargeback rules;

transaction status;

platform fees;

settlement status;

Payment Partner approval.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for refunding users from their own funds where the dispute is between users, merchants, service providers or third parties.

19. Wallet Use for Platform Services

The Wallet may be used to pay for approved services on the platform, including services offered by Duranki, Inkolo Connect, communities, merchants, vendors, service providers or other members.

Each service may have its own separate terms and conditions.

Using the Wallet to pay for a service does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees that service, provider, member, merchant, product, delivery, quality or outcome.

20. Bank Account Details

For Cash-Outs, the user must provide accurate bank account details.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may verify bank details before processing Cash-Outs.

Cash-Outs may be declined or delayed where:

the bank account is invalid;

the account is closed;

the account holder name does not match;

verification fails;

the bank rejects the payment;

the account is flagged;

the Payment Partner requires more information.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for delays or losses caused by incorrect or rejected bank details.

21. Third-Party Payment Partners

Wallet transactions may depend on banks, card schemes, payment gateways, settlement providers, mobile networks, fraud-screening providers and other third parties.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for:

bank downtime;

failed card processing;

delayed settlement;

rejected payments;

reversed payments;

card network failures;

Payment Partner errors;

chargeback rules;

mobile network failures;

third-party system downtime.

Users may be required to accept additional terms from Payment Partners.

22. Tax and Legal Responsibility

Users are responsible for any tax, declaration, reporting or legal obligations arising from Wallet transactions.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide tax, legal, accounting or financial advice.

Users must not use the Wallet to hide income, evade tax, avoid legal obligations or conduct unlawful business.

23. Personal Information

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may process personal information for Wallet registration, identity verification, payment processing, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, transaction monitoring, cash-outs, compliance, support and reporting.

Personal information may be shared with Payment Partners, banks, regulators, law enforcement, verification providers and service providers where necessary or legally permitted.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information obtained through Wallet transactions.

24. Transaction Records

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep records of Wallet transactions, including:

sender details;

recipient details;

transaction amounts;

dates and times;

payment method;

transaction references;

device information;

location information, where applicable;

status history;

dispute history;

fraud checks;

cash-out details.

These records may be used for support, reporting, disputes, compliance, audits, reconciliation, fraud prevention and legal purposes.

25. Service Availability

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that the Wallet will always be available, uninterrupted, error-free or secure.

Wallet services may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

bank downtime;

Payment Partner downtime;

mobile network failure;

cyber incidents;

power failure;

regulatory issues;

events beyond reasonable control.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for loss caused by Wallet downtime, delayed payments, failed transactions or service interruptions.

26. User Disputes

Users must first attempt to resolve payment disputes directly with the relevant user, merchant, service provider or third party.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing Wallet records, payment records, chat records or transaction data.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a court, arbitrator, debt collector, insurer or legal representative.

Any decision to reverse, hold, release, freeze or block funds is a platform risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

27. Death, Incapacity or Account Closure

If a user dies, becomes incapacitated or requests account closure, Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require legal documents before releasing or transferring Wallet funds.

Required documents may include:

death certificate;

letters of executorship;

proof of authority;

identity documents;

bank confirmation;

court orders;

any other document reasonably required.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may withhold Wallet funds until lawful authority is confirmed.

28. Closing or Suspending the Wallet

A user may request Wallet closure, subject to settlement of outstanding transactions, disputes, fees, reversals, chargebacks and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may close, suspend or restrict a Wallet where:

the user breaches these Terms;

the user fails verification;

fraud is suspected;

the Wallet is inactive;

required by law;

required by a Payment Partner;

the user misuses the platform;

the platform stops offering the Wallet service.

Any remaining funds may be paid out to the user’s verified bank account, subject to fees, deductions, chargebacks, legal holds and compliance requirements.

29. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

failed Wallet transactions;

delayed Top-Ups;

delayed Cash-Outs;

incorrect recipient details;

incorrect bank details;

unauthorised account access;

fraud or scams;

chargebacks;

reversals;

Payment Partner failures;

bank failures;

mobile network failures;

wallet freezes or holds;

user error;

system downtime;

disputed transactions;

loss of income;

loss of opportunity;

emotional distress;

third-party conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the fees actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific Wallet transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

30. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s Wallet use;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraudulent activity;

unauthorised card use;

chargebacks;

incorrect transfers;

incorrect bank details;

money laundering or suspicious activity;

misuse of another user’s information;

disputes with another user;

claims by banks, cardholders, Payment Partners, regulators or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Wallet.

31. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Wallet Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Wallet after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

32. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled in accordance with South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

33. Acceptance

By activating or using the Wallet, the user confirms that they:

have read these Wallet Terms;

understand these Wallet Terms;

agree to be bound by these Wallet Terms;

understand that the Wallet is not a bank account;

understand that funds can be sent from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

understand that Top-Ups may be made using a bank card where available;

understand that Cash-Outs may take up to 5 business days;

accept responsibility for checking recipient details, bank details, transaction amounts and account security;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, delay, reverse or restrict Wallet transactions where required for fraud, compliance, legal, payment or risk reasons.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before activating the Wallet:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Wallet Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that I can send money from my Wallet to another member’s Wallet, top up using my bank card where available, and request a cash-out to my bank account. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed for fraud, verification, compliance, bank or payment partner checks.

Suggested Transfer Confirmation Text

Before sending money to another member:

Please confirm that the recipient and amount are correct. Wallet transfers may not be reversible once submitted. Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible if you send money to the wrong member.

Suggested Cash-Out Confirmation Text

Before cashing out:

I confirm that my bank details are correct. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed, declined or held if verification, fraud, compliance, bank or payment partner checks are required.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF USE

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, registration number 2025/674136/07, is a private company with limited liability incorporated in the Republic of South Africa.

In these Terms, references to “Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” mean Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, applications, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners, where applicable.

These Terms and Conditions of Use govern the access to and use of the Inkolo Connect platform, including all services, member dashboards, community dashboards, church dashboards, wallet tools, communication tools, marketplace tools, job tools, ride tools, funeral cover access tools, value-added services and any other services made available through the platform from time to time.

Important Disclaimer

Inkolo Connect is a digital platform, onboarding, administration, communication and facilitation service.

Unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement, Inkolo Connect is not:

a licensed financial institution;

a bank;

an insurer;

an underwriter;

a financial services provider;

a funeral cover provider;

an employer;

a recruiter;

a labour broker;

a transport operator;

a taxi or e-hailing operator;

a seller of user-listed goods;

a guarantor of any third-party service.

Inkolo Connect may assist members with onboarding, administrative processes, platform access, communication, payment facilitation, digital dashboards, document uploads, member records, service activation and support processes.

Inkolo Connect does not provide financial advice, insurance advice, legal advice, tax advice, investment advice, labour advice, transport advice or medical advice unless expressly authorised and confirmed in writing.

Where third-party services, financial products, funeral cover, banking products, insurance products, payment products, employment opportunities, transport arrangements, marketplace listings or other services are made available through the platform, the contract is between the user and the relevant third-party service provider, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

1. Definitions and Interpretation

In these Terms, unless the context indicates otherwise:

1.1 “Acceptable Use Policy”

means Inkolo Connect’s rules for acceptable use of the platform, as published on the platform or website and updated from time to time.

1.2 “Affinity Treasury Account”

means a dedicated account, where applicable, funded or used by approved third-party service providers, financial service providers or platform partners to cover service fees, platform access costs, member benefits, community benefits or related agreed amounts.

1.3 “Agreement”

means these Terms and Conditions of Use, together with any applicable service-specific terms, policies, mandates, annexures, application forms, consent forms, product terms, payment terms and privacy policies.

1.4 “Basic Service Package”

means the minimum combination or level of platform services, community services, financial products, funeral products, wallet services, value-added services or other products selected or activated by a user, where applicable.

1.5 “Commencement Date”

means the date on which a user is granted access to the platform or a specific service.

1.6 “Confidential Information”

means all information, in whatever format, disclosed by one party to another, including business information, platform information, technical information, financial information, pricing, member data, product information, community information, software, trade secrets, processes, documents, reports, agreements, strategies, user information and any information marked or reasonably understood to be confidential.

1.7 “FICA”

means the Financial Intelligence Centre Act 38 of 2001, as amended.

1.8 “FSP”

means a Financial Services Provider licensed under the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, where applicable.

1.9 “Main Service Fee”

means the consolidated fixed fee, subscription fee, platform fee, service fee, product fee, access fee, administration fee or other amount payable for a service, product or package made available through the platform.

1.10 “Personal Information”

has the meaning given to it under POPIA and includes any information relating to an identifiable natural or juristic person.

1.11 “Platform”

means the Inkolo Connect online portal, application, website, mobile platform, dashboard, member profile, wallet system, service modules, communication tools, payment tools, marketplace tools and related digital services.

1.12 “Platform Costs”

means the portion of any Main Service Fee, subscription fee, service fee, transaction fee, commission, platform access fee or administration fee allocated to platform access, operation, hosting, maintenance, support, reporting, dashboards, technology, compliance and administration.

1.13 “POPIA”

means the Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013, as amended.

1.14 “Service Provider”

means any approved third-party provider, financial institution, bank, insurer, underwriter, FSP, payment provider, wallet provider, funeral cover provider, merchant, vendor, employer, job poster, driver, supplier, training provider, connectivity provider, value-added service provider or other party whose service is made available through the platform.

1.15 “Service-Specific Terms”

means additional terms and conditions that apply to specific platform services, including but not limited to Wallet, Buy & Sell, Job Search, Catch a Ride, Funeral Cover, Airtime, Data, Electricity, Vouchers, Loans, Banking Services, Merchant Services, QR Payments, Education Services and any other services introduced from time to time.

1.16 “User”

means any person who accesses or uses the platform, including a member, church member, community member, administrator, pastor, bishop, service provider, merchant, seller, buyer, job poster, worker, driver, passenger or any other authorised user.

1.17 “User Account”

means a registered user profile on the Inkolo Connect platform.

1.18 “Inkolo Connect Services”

means the administrative, onboarding, facilitation, platform access, dashboard, communication, payment facilitation, service activation, document management, reporting and support services provided by Inkolo Connect.

1.19 Interpretation

Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

Words in the singular include the plural, and words in the plural include the singular.

Any reference to law includes that law as amended or replaced from time to time.

2. Acceptance of Terms

2.1

By accessing, registering for, logging into, browsing, using or transacting through the platform, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms.

2.2

If the user does not agree to these Terms, the user must not access or use the platform.

2.3

The user confirms that they have legal capacity to enter into these Terms.

2.4

If the user acts on behalf of a church, organisation, community, business, service provider or other entity, the user confirms that they are authorised to bind that entity.

2.5

The user also agrees to all applicable Service-Specific Terms when using a specific service.

3. Risk Disclosure

3.1

The platform may provide access to third-party services, financial products, funeral cover, payment services, wallet services, marketplace services, job opportunities, ride arrangements and other products or services.

3.2

These services may carry risks, including financial risk, fraud risk, payment risk, operational risk, service provider risk, insurance risk, employment risk, transport risk, safety risk, legal risk and data risk.

3.3

The user must read all product documents, service provider terms, policy documents, fee schedules, disclosures, waiting periods, exclusions, payment terms and Service-Specific Terms before using or relying on any service.

3.4

Past performance, previous service availability, previous payments, previous claims, previous earnings or previous transaction success are not guarantees of future results.

4. Platform Access

4.1

Access to the platform is granted subject to:

registration of a valid User Account;

completion of any required verification;

enrolment in any required Basic Service Package, where applicable;

acceptance of these Terms;

acceptance of applicable Service-Specific Terms;

compliance with platform rules;

payment of applicable fees, where applicable.

4.2

Platform access may automatically renew unless cancelled by the user or terminated by Inkolo Connect.

4.3

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate platform access where the user breaches these Terms, fails verification, fails to pay fees, misuses the platform, creates risk or acts unlawfully.

4.4

Inkolo Connect may change, suspend, remove or replace any platform service at any time for operational, legal, commercial, compliance, technical or security reasons.

5. User Obligations

5.1

Users must:

provide accurate, current and complete information;

keep their information updated;

maintain confidentiality of login credentials;

keep OTPs, PINs, passwords and devices secure;

use the platform lawfully;

comply with all applicable laws and regulations;

comply with these Terms and Service-Specific Terms;

comply with the Acceptable Use Policy;

treat other users respectfully;

not misuse the platform;

not provide false documents or false information;

not impersonate another person;

not interfere with platform security or operation.

5.2 Prohibited Use

Users may not:

allow unauthorised persons to access their account;

sell, transfer or share their account;

use the platform for fraud, scams or unlawful activity;

use the platform for money laundering or terrorist financing;

upload viruses, malware or harmful code;

attempt to access another user’s account;

reverse engineer or copy the platform;

scrape, harvest or misuse data;

harass, threaten or abuse others;

post unlawful, misleading or harmful content;

bypass fees, payment rules or platform controls;

damage the reputation of Inkolo Connect, Duranki, churches, communities or approved partners.

6. Third-Party Services

6.1

The platform may provide access to third-party services, including financial services, payment services, funeral cover, insurance products, bank products, wallet tools, airtime, data, electricity, merchant services, transport arrangements, job listings, marketplace listings, education services, connectivity services and other products or services.

6.2

Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect does not:

own the third-party product;

provide the third-party service;

underwrite insurance or funeral cover;

guarantee claims;

guarantee payment;

guarantee quality;

guarantee delivery;

guarantee employment;

guarantee transport safety;

guarantee any outcome;

endorse or recommend any specific provider as suitable for the user;

provide financial advice, insurance advice or legal advice.

6.3

Users agree to cooperate with Service Providers for onboarding, verification, FICA, KYC, POPIA consent, payment compliance, application processing, product activation, claims, support and any other required process.

6.4

The user’s contract for a third-party product or service is with the relevant Service Provider and not with Inkolo Connect, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

7. Financial Services, Funeral Cover and Insurance Disclaimer

7.1

Inkolo Connect is not a licensed bank, insurer, underwriter or FSP unless expressly confirmed in writing.

7.2

Where financial products, funeral cover, insurance products, bank accounts, loans, savings products, payment products, rewards, vouchers or other financial services are displayed on the platform, those products are provided by the relevant Service Provider.

7.3

Inkolo Connect may assist with administrative onboarding, document capture, payment facilitation, member dashboards, policy document display, support routing and communication.

7.4

Inkolo Connect does not decide whether:

a financial product is suitable for the user;

funeral cover should be approved;

a claim should be paid;

a claim should be rejected;

a loan should be granted;

a bank account should be opened;

a payment should be reversed;

an insurer or FSP should accept the user.

7.5

The user must read and accept the applicable Service Provider terms before using or relying on any third-party financial, funeral, insurance or banking product.

8. Cooling-Off Period

8.1

Where a cooling-off period applies under the Consumer Protection Act, Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, Service Provider terms or any other applicable law, the user may exercise that right according to the applicable process and time period.

8.2

Cooling-off rights may not apply to all services, digital services, used services, urgent services, completed services, certain financial products or services already activated with user consent.

8.3

Where a third-party Service Provider product is involved, cancellation and cooling-off rights are governed by that Service Provider’s terms and applicable law.

9. Subscription, Fees and Payments

9.1

Platform Costs may be included within a fixed Main Service Fee, service fee, subscription fee, commission, revenue share, product fee, administration fee or transaction fee.

9.2

Main Service Fees become payable according to the invoice, billing notice, product terms, service-specific terms, mandate, annexure or payment schedule applicable to the service.

9.3

Unless otherwise stated, fees become payable within 15 days of billing.

9.4

Users remain solely responsible for all fees, charges, taxes, penalties, reversals, chargebacks, bank fees, payment fees and service fees arising from their use of the platform.

9.5

Any variation to Platform Costs or service fees may be communicated in advance by email, platform notification, updated pricing schedule or service notice.

9.6

Where applicable, FSPs, Service Providers, communities, churches or approved partners may remit platform access fees, commissions, service fees or revenue-share amounts as agreed in separate written agreements.

9.7 Suspension for Non-Payment

Failure to pay any amount due may result in suspension, restriction or termination of platform access, service access, dashboard access, wallet functions or account functions.

9.8

Inkolo Connect may deduct applicable fees from payments, wallet balances, service provider remittances, commission amounts, revenue-share amounts or other amounts payable through the platform where legally and contractually permitted.

10. Wallet and Payment Facilitation

10.1

The platform may include wallet, payment request, card top-up, member-to-member transfer, QR payment, scan-to-pay, cash-out, settlement or other payment functionality.

10.2

Wallet and payment services are subject to separate Wallet Terms and Payment Terms.

10.3

Inkolo Connect may use banks, payment gateways, card acquirers, payment service providers, wallet providers or other Payment Partners.

10.4

Inkolo Connect is not liable for bank delays, failed payments, chargebacks, reversed card transactions, incorrect recipient details, incorrect bank details, payment partner failures or third-party processing delays outside its reasonable control.

10.5

Inkolo Connect may freeze, hold, reverse, delay or restrict payments where fraud, disputes, failed verification, chargebacks, compliance issues or suspicious activity are detected.

11. Service Levels

11.1

Inkolo Connect targets reasonable platform availability and may target 98% platform uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance, emergency maintenance, upgrades, network failures, third-party failures, cyber incidents, power failures, force majeure events and circumstances outside Inkolo Connect’s reasonable control.

11.2

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee uninterrupted, error-free or continuous access.

11.3

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss caused by downtime, delayed access, failed messages, delayed transactions, missed opportunities or service interruptions.

12. Intellectual Property

12.1

All intellectual property rights in the platform remain the property of Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their licensors, software providers or approved partners, as applicable.

12.2

This Agreement does not transfer any intellectual property rights to users.

12.3

Users are granted a limited, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable right to use the platform for lawful personal, community, church, business or service-related purposes as authorised by Inkolo Connect.

12.4

Users may not copy, reproduce, modify, reverse engineer, resell, sublicense, distribute or commercially exploit the platform without written permission.

12.5

User content uploaded to the platform remains the responsibility of the user. By uploading content, the user grants Inkolo Connect a non-exclusive right to use, store, display, process, transmit and reproduce that content for platform operation, service delivery, moderation, reporting and support.

13. Data Protection

13.1

Inkolo Connect will implement appropriate technical and organisational safeguards to protect Personal Information as required under POPIA.

13.2

Inkolo Connect may process Personal Information for:

registration;

identity verification;

service activation;

member management;

community and church dashboards;

communication;

payments;

wallet transactions;

service provider onboarding;

funeral cover administration;

job applications;

marketplace transactions;

ride arrangements;

fraud prevention;

compliance;

reporting;

support;

legal obligations.

13.3

Personal Information may be shared with Service Providers, Payment Partners, banks, insurers, underwriters, FSPs, verification providers, churches, communities, administrators, regulators, law enforcement bodies or other authorised parties where necessary, lawful or consented to.

13.4

Users must not misuse, disclose, sell or unlawfully process another user’s Personal Information obtained through the platform.

14. Confidentiality

14.1

Each party must keep Confidential Information confidential.

14.2

Each party must:

use Confidential Information only for purposes of this Agreement;

disclose Confidential Information only to persons who need to know it;

ensure that any person receiving Confidential Information is bound by similar confidentiality obligations;

protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

14.3

These obligations do not apply to information that:

is in or comes into the public domain through no fault of the receiving party;

was independently developed or obtained without use of Confidential Information;

is disclosed with prior written approval;

is required to be disclosed by law, regulator, court order or lawful authority.

14.4

This confidentiality section survives termination of the Agreement.

15. Platform Content and User Content

15.1

Users are responsible for all content they upload, list, publish, send or share through the platform.

15.2

User content may include listings, job posts, messages, documents, images, profiles, reviews, ratings, comments, payment references and service information.

15.3

Users must ensure that their content is lawful, accurate, not misleading and does not infringe third-party rights.

15.4

Inkolo Connect may remove, hide, edit, restrict or report content that is unlawful, misleading, abusive, fraudulent, unsafe, offensive, discriminatory, reputationally harmful or in breach of these Terms.

16. Limitation of Liability

16.1

Inkolo Connect is not a licensed financial services provider, bank, insurer, underwriter, employer, transport operator or seller of user-listed goods unless expressly stated in writing.

16.2

Use of the platform does not create any fiduciary relationship, advisory relationship, employment relationship, agency relationship, partnership or joint venture between Inkolo Connect and any user.

16.3

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, its directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates are not liable for:

direct, indirect, special or consequential losses;

loss of profits;

loss of income;

loss of data;

loss of opportunity;

reputational harm;

emotional distress;

platform interruptions;

delayed access;

errors in platform access;

third-party service failures;

payment failures;

fraud by users;

disputes between users;

rejected claims;

failed jobs;

failed rides;

marketplace disputes;

Service Provider decisions;

any loss caused by user error, incorrect information or unlawful use.

16.4

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific service or transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

16.5

Inkolo Connect may receive commissions, platform fees, administration fees, referral fees, revenue share or other fees from certain third parties, Service Providers, churches, communities or platform partners. Such fees may be disclosed to users where required by law or policy.

17. Indemnity

17.1

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of the platform;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of any Service-Specific Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

false or incorrect information supplied by the user;

fraud or unlawful conduct;

misuse of another person’s information;

user content uploaded to the platform;

disputes with other users;

disputes with Service Providers;

claims by regulators, banks, insurers, employers, workers, buyers, sellers, drivers, passengers, beneficiaries, dependants or third parties.

17.2

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

18. Termination

18.1

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate access where a user:

breaches these Terms;

breaches Service-Specific Terms;

fails verification;

fails to pay fees;

misuses the platform;

provides false information;

commits fraud;

creates legal, financial, operational, safety, security, reputational or compliance risk;

violates the Acceptable Use Policy;

is required to be suspended by a Service Provider, Payment Partner, regulator, bank, insurer or lawful authority.

18.2

Users may terminate their User Account by written notice or through the platform, subject to outstanding obligations.

18.3

If the user cancels or ceases their Basic Service Package:

platform access may be revoked;

service access may be terminated;

outstanding fees remain due;

Wallet obligations may continue until settlement;

Service Provider obligations may continue under the relevant Service Provider terms;

data may be archived, retained or deleted in accordance with policy and applicable law.

18.4

Termination of platform access does not automatically cancel third-party products, insurance policies, funeral cover, bank products, loans, debit orders, subscriptions or contracts with Service Providers. The user must cancel those directly according to the applicable Service Provider terms.

19. Dispute Resolution

19.1

Any dispute relating to these Terms must first be attempted to be resolved through good faith discussions between the parties.

19.2

If the dispute remains unresolved, the parties may refer the dispute to mediation or arbitration in South Africa under applicable arbitration rules.

19.3

Nothing in this clause prevents Inkolo Connect from approaching a court for urgent relief, debt recovery, interdictory relief, intellectual property protection, confidentiality breaches or unlawful platform use.

19.4

Disputes relating to third-party products or services must be raised with the relevant Service Provider according to that Service Provider’s complaints and dispute process.

20. Privacy and Acceptable Use Policies

20.1

By accessing or using the platform, the user agrees to Inkolo Connect’s Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy and any other applicable platform policies.

20.2

The processing of Personal Information is subject to POPIA and the Privacy Policy.

20.3

Use of the platform must comply with all Inkolo Connect policies.

20.4

Inkolo Connect reserves the right to modify its policies and notify users by email, platform notification, website notice or other reasonable means.

20.5

Any violation of the Acceptable Use Policy may result in suspension or termination.

20.6

In the event of conflict between these Terms and policy documents, these Terms will prevail, unless a Service-Specific Term or legally required product document expressly overrides them for that specific service.

20.7

By registering a User Account, the user acknowledges and consents to Inkolo Connect’s applicable policies.

21. Service-Specific Terms

21.1

Certain services on the platform are subject to additional Service-Specific Terms.

These may include:

Wallet Service Terms;

Buy & Sell Service Terms;

Job Search Service Terms;

Catch a Ride Service Terms;

Funeral Cover Service Terms;

Airtime, Data and Electricity Terms;

Merchant Services Terms;

QR Payment Terms;

Voucher and Rewards Terms;

Education and Training Terms;

Loan or Banking Product Terms;

Community Dashboard Terms;

Service Provider Terms.

21.2

By using a specific service, the user agrees to the Service-Specific Terms for that service.

21.3

If there is a conflict between these general Terms and the Service-Specific Terms, the Service-Specific Terms will apply to that specific service.

22. Amendments to Terms

22.1

Inkolo Connect reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time.

22.2

Notice may be provided by email, platform notification, website notice, SMS, WhatsApp notification or other reasonable means.

22.3

Continued use of the platform after updated Terms are published or notified means that the user accepts the updated Terms.

22.4

If the user does not agree to the updated Terms, the user must stop using the platform and may request account closure, subject to outstanding obligations.

23. General Provisions

23.1 Entire Agreement

These Terms, together with applicable policies and Service-Specific Terms, constitute the entire agreement between Inkolo Connect and the user regarding platform use.

23.2 No Waiver

No waiver of any provision will be binding unless recorded in writing by Inkolo Connect.

23.3 Severability

If any provision is found to be invalid, unlawful or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain valid and enforceable.

23.4 Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

23.5 Force Majeure

Inkolo Connect is not liable for delays, failures or non-performance caused by events beyond its reasonable control, including power failures, network failures, internet outages, payment partner failures, bank failures, cyber incidents, natural disasters, strikes, riots, civil unrest, acts of government, regulatory action, pandemics, war, terrorism or other force majeure events.

23.6 Assignment

Inkolo Connect may assign, transfer or subcontract its rights and obligations under these Terms to an affiliate, successor, service provider, platform partner or purchaser of its business, provided this does not unlawfully prejudice the user.

23.7 Notices

Notices may be sent by email, SMS, WhatsApp, platform notification, website notice or any contact method provided by the user.

23.8 Survival

Clauses relating to confidentiality, intellectual property, limitation of liability, indemnity, payment obligations, data retention, dispute resolution and governing law survive termination of these Terms.

24. User Acceptance

By registering, accessing or using the Inkolo Connect platform, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

agree to be bound by these Terms;

understand that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator and not the provider of every service displayed on the platform;

understand that third-party products and services are governed by the relevant Service Provider terms;

accept responsibility for their own information, account security, service choices, payments and conduct;

agree to comply with all applicable Service-Specific Terms.

Suggested Platform Checkbox

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy and all applicable Service-Specific Terms. I understand that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator and that third-party services are provided by the relevant service providers under their own terms.

Suggested Short Disclaimer for App Footer

Inkolo Connect is a digital platform and facilitation service. Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect is not a bank, insurer, FSP, employer, transport operator, seller or guarantor of third-party services. All third-party services remain subject to the terms of the relevant service provider.

WALLET SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Wallet Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, activate, top up, receive, transfer, send, spend, cash out or otherwise use the Wallet service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Wallet service, the user agrees to be bound by these Wallet Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Community Rules, Service Terms, Payment Terms, and any additional rules, limits, fees, notices or instructions published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet systems, payment tools, communication tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community, church and member-management platform operated on or through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital service, dashboard, app, website, wallet, payment channel, communication channel or service module.

“Wallet” means the digital wallet facility made available to registered users through the platform.

“Wallet Balance” means the value reflected in a user’s Wallet, subject to successful funding, transaction processing, fraud checks, reversals, fees, refunds, holds, limits and compliance reviews.

“User” means any person who registers for, accesses or uses the Wallet.

“Member-to-Member Transfer” means a transfer of available Wallet funds from one registered member’s Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

“Top-Up” means adding money to a Wallet using an approved payment method, including bank card payment where available.

“Cash-Out” means a request by a user to withdraw available Wallet funds from the Wallet to a bank account registered or nominated by that user.

“Payment Partner” means any bank, card acquirer, payment service provider, payment gateway, wallet provider, settlement provider, banking partner, compliance provider or other third-party payment processor used by Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

“Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or official South African public holiday.

2. Nature of the Wallet Service

The Wallet is a digital payment and value-transfer feature made available through the platform.

The Wallet may allow users to:

receive funds into their Wallet;

send funds from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

pay for approved goods or services on the platform;

receive payment from other members;

top up their Wallet using a bank card or other approved payment method;

request a cash-out to their bank account;

view Wallet transaction history;

use Wallet funds for approved platform services.

The Wallet is provided as a platform payment tool and not as a bank account, savings account, deposit account, credit facility, investment product or interest-bearing account.

No interest will be paid to users on Wallet balances unless expressly stated in writing.

3. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the Wallet as part of the platform’s digital service environment.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may use third-party Payment Partners to process card payments, Wallet transactions, settlements, reversals, cash-outs, refunds, fraud screening and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for delays, failures, reversals, declines, chargebacks, bank errors, card network issues, payment gateway failures or third-party processing problems outside their reasonable control.

Where required, Wallet services may be provided through, supported by or settled through approved Payment Partners.

4. Wallet Activation

To activate or use the Wallet, a user may be required to:

register on the platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide personal information;

accept these Wallet Terms;

pass fraud and compliance checks;

provide bank account details for cash-outs;

provide any additional documents requested by Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse, delay, suspend or deactivate Wallet access where verification is incomplete, failed, suspicious or required by law, risk policy or a Payment Partner.

5. Member-to-Member Wallet Transfers

The Wallet may allow a user to send money from their Wallet to another registered member’s Wallet.

The sender is responsible for ensuring that:

the recipient is the correct person;

the amount is correct;

the reason for the transfer is lawful;

the recipient details are correct;

they have sufficient available Wallet balance;

the transaction is not fraudulent, unlawful or prohibited.

Once a Member-to-Member Transfer has been submitted, it may not be reversible unless Duranki, Inkolo Connect or a Payment Partner determines that a reversal is permitted, required or necessary due to fraud, error, dispute, chargeback, system failure, compliance review or applicable law.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee recovery of funds sent to the wrong recipient.

Users must not use Member-to-Member Transfers for illegal activity, fraud, scams, gambling, money laundering, prohibited goods, unauthorised business activity or any other unlawful purpose.

6. Top-Up Using Bank Card

Users may be able to top up their Wallet using a bank card, subject to availability and approval by the Payment Partner.

A Top-Up may be declined, delayed, reversed or blocked where:

the card is declined by the issuing bank;

the card details are incorrect;

the card is expired;

there are insufficient funds;

3D Secure or OTP verification fails;

the transaction is flagged as suspicious;

the transaction exceeds platform limits;

the transaction breaches Payment Partner rules;

the cardholder disputes the transaction;

the transaction is subject to chargeback;

Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner suspects fraud or unauthorised use.

A Wallet balance may only be credited once the Top-Up has been successfully authorised and processed.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may reverse or debit a Wallet balance if a card Top-Up is later reversed, charged back, rejected, refunded, identified as fraudulent or not finally settled.

Card payments may be subject to chargeback rules imposed by card schemes, banks and card issuers. A chargeback may result in a reversal or deduction from the user’s Wallet balance, even after the Wallet was initially credited.

7. Cash-Out to Bank Account

Users may request to cash out available Wallet funds to their own bank account, subject to platform rules, verification, limits, fraud checks, compliance checks and Payment Partner processing.

A Cash-Out may only be made to a bank account accepted by the platform and/or Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require the bank account to be in the same name as the registered Wallet user.

The user is responsible for ensuring that the bank account details provided are complete, correct and active.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for loss, delay or failed payment caused by incorrect bank account details supplied by the user.

8. Cash-Out Waiting Period

Cash-Out requests are subject to a waiting period of up to 5 business days from the date the Cash-Out request is approved for processing.

The 5-business-day period is a standard waiting period and may be used for:

fraud checks;

card chargeback risk checks;

payment settlement confirmation;

bank processing;

Payment Partner processing;

compliance checks;

identity verification;

anti-money-laundering controls;

dispute checks;

system and reconciliation checks.

The 5-business-day period does not include Saturdays, Sundays or South African public holidays.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may extend the Cash-Out period where:

fraud is suspected;

the user fails verification;

bank details are incorrect;

the Payment Partner requires additional checks;

a dispute has been raised;

a chargeback risk exists;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the user’s Wallet is restricted;

the user’s account is under review;

delays occur outside the control of Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that every Cash-Out will be completed within 5 business days where additional verification, fraud checks, compliance checks or third-party delays apply.

9. Available Balance

A user may only spend, transfer or cash out the available Wallet balance.

The available Wallet balance may be different from the displayed balance where funds are:

pending;

under review;

subject to settlement;

subject to chargeback risk;

frozen;

reversed;

refunded;

disputed;

subject to fees;

subject to compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may correct Wallet balances where an error, duplicate credit, failed payment, reversal, chargeback, refund or system issue occurred.

10. Fees

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may charge fees for Wallet services.

Fees may apply to:

Wallet Top-Ups;

Member-to-Member Transfers;

Cash-Outs;

failed Cash-Outs;

reversed transactions;

card processing;

refunds;

chargebacks;

account verification;

platform service use;

any other Wallet-related service.

Applicable fees may be displayed in the app, dashboard, pricing schedule or transaction screen.

By confirming a transaction, the user agrees to pay the applicable fee.

Fees may be deducted from the Wallet balance or transaction amount before settlement.

11. Transaction Limits

Duranki, Inkolo Connect and/or their Payment Partners may apply limits to Wallet use.

Limits may include:

minimum Top-Up amount;

maximum Top-Up amount;

daily transaction limit;

monthly transaction limit;

maximum Wallet balance;

maximum Cash-Out amount;

minimum Cash-Out amount;

Member-to-Member Transfer limits;

card payment limits;

risk-based limits.

Limits may differ depending on verification level, user type, risk profile, community, service, Payment Partner requirements or regulatory requirements.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may change Wallet limits at any time.

12. No Credit, Loans or Overdraft

The Wallet does not provide credit, loans, overdraft or advance facilities unless a separate approved service is expressly provided under separate terms.

Users may not spend more than their available Wallet balance.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse transactions where there are insufficient available funds.

13. Payment Reversals and Chargebacks

A transaction may be reversed, deducted, frozen or adjusted where:

a cardholder disputes a Top-Up;

a chargeback is received;

fraud is suspected;

payment was made in error;

duplicate credit occurred;

the Payment Partner reverses the transaction;

a refund is required;

a transaction is unlawful;

a transaction breaches these Terms;

Duranki or Inkolo Connect is required to do so by law, court order, bank, regulator or Payment Partner.

If a user has already spent or withdrawn funds that are later reversed, the user remains liable to repay the amount to Duranki, Inkolo Connect or the Payment Partner.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may recover the amount from future Wallet balances, withhold future payments, suspend the Wallet or pursue recovery by lawful means.

14. Incorrect Transfers

Users are responsible for checking recipient details before sending money.

If a user sends money to the wrong member, Duranki and Inkolo Connect will not be liable for the loss.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may attempt to assist, but do not guarantee recovery.

A recipient who receives money in error must return it immediately when requested.

Failure to return money received in error may result in Wallet suspension, account restriction and legal action.

15. Prohibited Wallet Use

Users may not use the Wallet for:

fraud;

scams;

money laundering;

terrorist financing;

illegal gambling;

unlawful loans;

prohibited goods or services;

stolen goods;

fake payment confirmations;

identity theft;

unauthorised card use;

chargeback abuse;

illegal investment schemes;

pyramid schemes;

bribery or corruption;

evading platform rules;

any unlawful or suspicious activity.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may report suspicious transactions to banks, Payment Partners, regulators or law enforcement where required or permitted.

16. Account Security

Users are responsible for keeping their account, PIN, password, OTP, device, SIM card and login details secure.

Users must not share:

passwords;

PINs;

OTPs;

card details;

login details;

device access;

wallet access;

verification codes.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by the user sharing security details, losing their device, allowing unauthorised access, falling for scams or failing to secure their account.

Users must immediately report suspected unauthorised access or fraud.

17. Wallet Freezing, Holds and Restrictions

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, hold, delay, reverse, restrict or suspend Wallet funds or Wallet access where:

fraud is suspected;

suspicious activity is detected;

a chargeback is received;

a user fails verification;

a dispute is raised;

a law, regulator, bank or Payment Partner requires it;

the user breaches these Terms;

the transaction appears unlawful;

the Wallet is used for prohibited activity;

there is a technical or reconciliation issue;

the user provides false or incomplete information;

risk controls require review.

A Wallet freeze, hold or restriction does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect accepts liability to the user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep the Wallet restricted until the issue has been investigated and resolved to their satisfaction.

18. Refunds

Refunds may be processed where allowed by the relevant service terms, merchant rules, Payment Partner rules or applicable law.

Refunds are not automatic and may be subject to:

service-specific refund rules;

merchant approval;

fraud checks;

payment reversal rules;

chargeback rules;

transaction status;

platform fees;

settlement status;

Payment Partner approval.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for refunding users from their own funds where the dispute is between users, merchants, service providers or third parties.

19. Wallet Use for Platform Services

The Wallet may be used to pay for approved services on the platform, including services offered by Duranki, Inkolo Connect, communities, merchants, vendors, service providers or other members.

Each service may have its own separate terms and conditions.

Using the Wallet to pay for a service does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees that service, provider, member, merchant, product, delivery, quality or outcome.

20. Bank Account Details

For Cash-Outs, the user must provide accurate bank account details.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may verify bank details before processing Cash-Outs.

Cash-Outs may be declined or delayed where:

the bank account is invalid;

the account is closed;

the account holder name does not match;

verification fails;

the bank rejects the payment;

the account is flagged;

the Payment Partner requires more information.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for delays or losses caused by incorrect or rejected bank details.

21. Third-Party Payment Partners

Wallet transactions may depend on banks, card schemes, payment gateways, settlement providers, mobile networks, fraud-screening providers and other third parties.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for:

bank downtime;

failed card processing;

delayed settlement;

rejected payments;

reversed payments;

card network failures;

Payment Partner errors;

chargeback rules;

mobile network failures;

third-party system downtime.

Users may be required to accept additional terms from Payment Partners.

22. Tax and Legal Responsibility

Users are responsible for any tax, declaration, reporting or legal obligations arising from Wallet transactions.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide tax, legal, accounting or financial advice.

Users must not use the Wallet to hide income, evade tax, avoid legal obligations or conduct unlawful business.

23. Personal Information

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may process personal information for Wallet registration, identity verification, payment processing, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, transaction monitoring, cash-outs, compliance, support and reporting.

Personal information may be shared with Payment Partners, banks, regulators, law enforcement, verification providers and service providers where necessary or legally permitted.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information obtained through Wallet transactions.

24. Transaction Records

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may keep records of Wallet transactions, including:

sender details;

recipient details;

transaction amounts;

dates and times;

payment method;

transaction references;

device information;

location information, where applicable;

status history;

dispute history;

fraud checks;

cash-out details.

These records may be used for support, reporting, disputes, compliance, audits, reconciliation, fraud prevention and legal purposes.

25. Service Availability

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that the Wallet will always be available, uninterrupted, error-free or secure.

Wallet services may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

bank downtime;

Payment Partner downtime;

mobile network failure;

cyber incidents;

power failure;

regulatory issues;

events beyond reasonable control.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for loss caused by Wallet downtime, delayed payments, failed transactions or service interruptions.

26. User Disputes

Users must first attempt to resolve payment disputes directly with the relevant user, merchant, service provider or third party.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing Wallet records, payment records, chat records or transaction data.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a court, arbitrator, debt collector, insurer or legal representative.

Any decision to reverse, hold, release, freeze or block funds is a platform risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

27. Death, Incapacity or Account Closure

If a user dies, becomes incapacitated or requests account closure, Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require legal documents before releasing or transferring Wallet funds.

Required documents may include:

death certificate;

letters of executorship;

proof of authority;

identity documents;

bank confirmation;

court orders;

any other document reasonably required.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may withhold Wallet funds until lawful authority is confirmed.

28. Closing or Suspending the Wallet

A user may request Wallet closure, subject to settlement of outstanding transactions, disputes, fees, reversals, chargebacks and compliance checks.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may close, suspend or restrict a Wallet where:

the user breaches these Terms;

the user fails verification;

fraud is suspected;

the Wallet is inactive;

required by law;

required by a Payment Partner;

the user misuses the platform;

the platform stops offering the Wallet service.

Any remaining funds may be paid out to the user’s verified bank account, subject to fees, deductions, chargebacks, legal holds and compliance requirements.

29. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

failed Wallet transactions;

delayed Top-Ups;

delayed Cash-Outs;

incorrect recipient details;

incorrect bank details;

unauthorised account access;

fraud or scams;

chargebacks;

reversals;

Payment Partner failures;

bank failures;

mobile network failures;

wallet freezes or holds;

user error;

system downtime;

disputed transactions;

loss of income;

loss of opportunity;

emotional distress;

third-party conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the fees actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific Wallet transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

30. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, communities, churches, Payment Partners and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s Wallet use;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraudulent activity;

unauthorised card use;

chargebacks;

incorrect transfers;

incorrect bank details;

money laundering or suspicious activity;

misuse of another user’s information;

disputes with another user;

claims by banks, cardholders, Payment Partners, regulators or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Wallet.

31. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Wallet Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Wallet after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

32. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled in accordance with South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

33. Acceptance

By activating or using the Wallet, the user confirms that they:

have read these Wallet Terms;

understand these Wallet Terms;

agree to be bound by these Wallet Terms;

understand that the Wallet is not a bank account;

understand that funds can be sent from one member Wallet to another member Wallet;

understand that Top-Ups may be made using a bank card where available;

understand that Cash-Outs may take up to 5 business days;

accept responsibility for checking recipient details, bank details, transaction amounts and account security;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect may freeze, delay, reverse or restrict Wallet transactions where required for fraud, compliance, legal, payment or risk reasons.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before activating the Wallet:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Wallet Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that I can send money from my Wallet to another member’s Wallet, top up using my bank card where available, and request a cash-out to my bank account. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed for fraud, verification, compliance, bank or payment partner checks.

Suggested Transfer Confirmation Text

Before sending money to another member:

Please confirm that the recipient and amount are correct. Wallet transfers may not be reversible once submitted. Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible if you send money to the wrong member.

Suggested Cash-Out Confirmation Text

Before cashing out:

I confirm that my bank details are correct. I understand that cash-outs may take up to 5 business days and may be delayed, declined or held if verification, fraud, compliance, bank or payment partner checks are required.
