INKOLO REFERRAL SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who participate in, access, share, use, submit, receive or claim rewards, benefits, incentives, commissions, vouchers, wallet credits or any other value through the Inkolo Referral Service made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By participating in the Inkolo Referral Service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Community Terms, Acceptable Use Policy and any additional service-specific rules published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet tools, payment tools, referral tools, member profiles and service modules.

“Referral Service” means the feature that allows eligible users to refer new members, churches, communities, service users, merchants, vendors, customers or other participants to Inkolo Connect or approved platform services.

“Referrer” means a user who shares a referral link, referral code, invitation or approved referral process.

“Referred User” means a person, member, church, organisation, merchant, vendor or customer who joins, registers, activates or purchases a service through a referral.

“Referral Reward” means any reward, incentive, commission, voucher, wallet credit, discount, benefit, points, coins, cash-equivalent value or other reward that may be offered for a successful referral.

“Qualifying Referral” means a referral that meets all requirements set by Inkolo Connect for that specific referral campaign, service, product or reward.

“Referral Campaign” means a specific referral programme, promotion, offer, service referral or reward structure made available by Inkolo Connect from time to time.

“Service Provider” means any third-party provider, insurer, bank, payment provider, merchant, vendor, funeral cover provider, VAS provider, training provider, connectivity provider, property provider, employer, transport provider or other approved partner whose service may be referred through the platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform or participates in the Referral Service.

2. Purpose of the Referral Service

The Referral Service allows eligible users to invite others to join Inkolo Connect, activate services, join communities, use approved products or participate in platform programmes.

The Referral Service may apply to:

member registrations;

church registrations;

branch registrations;

community registrations;

funeral cover activations;

wallet activations;

VAS purchases;

merchant registrations;

vendor registrations;

job listings;

property listings;

connectivity services;

education services;

approved third-party services;

any other referral campaign approved by Inkolo Connect.

Inkolo Connect may change, suspend, limit, replace or terminate any referral campaign at any time.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect provides the digital referral tool and may record, track, verify, approve, reject or reward referrals.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that:

every referral will be accepted;

every referral will qualify for a reward;

a referred person will register;

a referred person will activate a service;

a referred person will remain active;

a third-party Service Provider will approve the referred user;

a referred service will be successful;

a reward will be payable before verification is complete;

a referral reward will remain available permanently.

Inkolo Connect may rely on platform records, service provider confirmations, payment records, activation records and fraud checks when deciding whether a referral qualifies.

4. Eligibility

To participate in the Referral Service, a user may be required to:

be registered on the Platform;

have an active Inkolo Connect account;

have verified their mobile number;

have completed identity verification where required;

have accepted these Terms;

have complied with all platform rules;

have an active Wallet where rewards are paid to Wallet;

not be suspended, banned or under investigation;

comply with referral campaign rules.

Inkolo Connect may refuse participation where a user fails verification, provides false information, abuses the referral system or creates risk.

5. Qualifying Referrals

A referral will only qualify for a reward if it meets all requirements of the relevant referral campaign.

Requirements may include:

the Referred User must be new to the Platform;

the Referred User must register using the correct referral link, code or process;

the Referred User must complete verification;

the Referred User must activate the relevant service;

the Referred User must make a qualifying payment, where applicable;

the Referred User must remain active for a minimum period;

the Referred User must not cancel, reverse or refund the service;

the Referred User must not be fraudulent, duplicated or self-referred;

the referral must be properly tracked by the Platform;

Inkolo Connect must approve the referral as valid.

Inkolo Connect’s platform records will be the primary record for referral tracking.

6. Non-Qualifying Referrals

A referral will not qualify for a reward where:

the Referred User was already registered;

the Referred User used another referral code;

the referral was not tracked correctly due to user error;

the Referrer referred themselves;

the Referrer created fake accounts;

the Referred User failed verification;

the Referred User cancelled the service;

the payment was reversed or refunded;

fraud or abuse is suspected;

the referral was generated through spam;

the referral breached these Terms;

the referral campaign had ended;

the maximum reward limit was reached;

the Service Provider declined or cancelled the service;

Inkolo Connect reasonably determines that the referral is invalid.

Inkolo Connect is not obliged to pay a reward for non-qualifying referrals.

7. Referral Rewards

Referral Rewards may be paid or issued in the form of:

Wallet credit;

cash-equivalent platform credit;

voucher;

discount;

reward points;

Inkolo coins or community coins;

airtime, data or VAS benefits;

service discount;

commission;

once-off incentive;

recurring incentive;

any other approved reward.

The type, value, timing and conditions of the reward will be determined by the applicable referral campaign.

Inkolo Connect may change reward values, reward types and campaign rules at any time.

8. No Automatic Right to a Reward

A referral does not automatically create a right to a reward.

A reward becomes payable only when Inkolo Connect confirms that the referral is a Qualifying Referral and that all campaign conditions have been met.

Inkolo Connect may delay, hold, reverse, cancel or reject a reward where verification, fraud checks, chargeback checks, service activation checks, payment checks or Service Provider confirmations are required.

9. Reward Payment Timing

Referral Rewards may be delayed to allow for:

verification of the Referrer;

verification of the Referred User;

service activation confirmation;

payment confirmation;

fraud checks;

duplicate-account checks;

chargeback checks;

refund checks;

cancellation checks;

Service Provider confirmation;

Wallet settlement;

compliance checks.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee instant referral reward payments.

Where a referral relates to a third-party service, reward payment may depend on confirmation and settlement from the relevant Service Provider.

10. Wallet Rewards

Where Referral Rewards are paid into the user’s Wallet, the Wallet Terms apply.

Wallet rewards may be subject to:

Wallet verification;

transaction limits;

withdrawal limits;

cash-out rules;

cash-out waiting periods;

fees;

fraud checks;

reversal rights;

compliance checks;

account restrictions.

Inkolo Connect may reverse a Wallet reward if the referral later becomes invalid, fraudulent, reversed, refunded, cancelled or disputed.

11. Referral Codes and Links

Inkolo Connect may provide users with referral codes, links, QR codes, invite messages or other referral tools.

Users are responsible for using the correct referral method.

Inkolo Connect is not liable where a referral is not tracked because:

the wrong code was used;

the link was broken;

the Referred User did not use the referral process;

the Referred User registered independently;

the Referred User used another Referrer’s code;

the user shared an incorrect link;

technical settings prevented tracking;

the campaign had expired.

Inkolo Connect may refuse manual referral claims where the referral was not tracked by the Platform.

12. Prohibited Referral Conduct

Users may not:

create fake accounts;

refer themselves;

refer the same person multiple times;

use stolen identity information;

use bots, scripts or automated referral tools;

spam referral links;

mislead people about Inkolo Connect;

promise rewards not approved by Inkolo Connect;

make false claims about services;

impersonate Inkolo Connect;

impersonate a church or community leader;

pressure or harass people to register;

use unlawful marketing methods;

advertise referral links on prohibited platforms;

use referral links for scams;

abuse vouchers, rewards or Wallet credits;

manipulate the system;

use referrals for money laundering or fraud.

Breach of this clause may result in reward cancellation, account suspension, Wallet restriction, permanent ban and legal action.

13. No Misrepresentation

Referrers must be honest when promoting Inkolo Connect or any service on the Platform.

Referrers may not claim that:

Inkolo Connect guarantees income;

all users will receive money;

services are free when they are not;

funeral claims are guaranteed;

jobs are guaranteed;

loans are guaranteed;

bank accounts are guaranteed;

property rentals or sales are guaranteed;

transport is guaranteed;

any third-party service is approved for all users;

rewards are guaranteed before qualification.

Any information shared by a Referrer must be accurate, lawful and not misleading.

14. Third-Party Service Referrals

Some referrals may relate to services provided by third-party Service Providers.

These may include:

funeral cover;

banking products;

wallet services;

payment services;

VAS services;

connectivity services;

education services;

property services;

merchant services;

insurance products;

employment-related services;

any other approved third-party service.

The contract for the referred product or service is between the Referred User and the relevant Service Provider.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for the Service Provider’s approval, rejection, pricing, service delivery, claims, refunds, cancellations or product performance.

Referral Rewards linked to third-party services may depend on Service Provider confirmation and payment.

15. Churches, Communities and Leaders

Where referrals are made within a church, branch, community or faith-based environment, users must act respectfully and transparently.

Church Leaders, pastors, bishops, administrators or community leaders may not misuse their position to pressure members into registering or buying services.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes between churches, leaders, members or communities arising from referral activity.

Referral disputes inside a church or community must be resolved by the relevant church or community, unless the dispute concerns platform misuse, fraud or a technical issue.

16. Referral Disputes

Users must report referral disputes through the Platform support process.

Referral disputes may include:

missing referral credit;

wrong Referrer;

duplicate referral claims;

delayed rewards;

rejected referrals;

cancelled rewards;

reward amount disputes.

Inkolo Connect may review platform records, registration timestamps, referral codes, activation records, payment records, device records, Service Provider confirmations and fraud checks.

Inkolo Connect’s decision on referral qualification and reward allocation will be final, unless a clear platform error is proven.

17. Fraud Prevention

Inkolo Connect may monitor referral activity for fraud, abuse, suspicious behaviour, duplicate accounts, fake registrations, identity misuse, payment abuse, chargebacks and system manipulation.

Inkolo Connect may:

delay rewards;

hold rewards;

reverse rewards;

cancel rewards;

request verification;

suspend accounts;

restrict Wallets;

block referral codes;

terminate referral access;

report unlawful activity.

Users who abuse the Referral Service may lose all pending and future rewards.

18. Tax Responsibility

Referral Rewards may have tax consequences.

Users are responsible for any tax, declaration, reporting or legal obligations arising from Referral Rewards, commissions, incentives, vouchers, credits or benefits received through the Referral Service.

Inkolo Connect does not provide tax advice and is not responsible for a user’s tax obligations.

19. No Employment, Agency or Franchise Relationship

Participation in the Referral Service does not make the user an employee, agent, representative, broker, franchisee, partner, contractor or authorised salesperson of Inkolo Connect unless a separate written agreement is signed.

Users may not bind Inkolo Connect, sign agreements on behalf of Inkolo Connect, make promises on behalf of Inkolo Connect or represent themselves as authorised employees or agents unless expressly authorised in writing.

20. Marketing and Communication Rules

Users must comply with all applicable laws and platform rules when sharing referral links or messages.

Users may not send referral messages in a way that is unlawful, abusive, misleading, unsolicited, excessive or harmful to Inkolo Connect’s reputation.

Users must stop contacting any person who asks not to be contacted.

Inkolo Connect may restrict or suspend referral access where a user’s marketing creates complaints, reputational risk, legal risk or community conflict.

21. Campaign Changes and Termination

Inkolo Connect may at any time:

change a referral campaign;

change reward amounts;

change reward types;

introduce new conditions;

limit the number of rewards;

end a campaign;

suspend a campaign;

exclude certain users;

exclude certain services;

cancel referral rewards for abuse or risk reasons.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss of expected rewards, income, opportunity or benefit caused by campaign changes or termination.

22. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

referral tracking;

user registration;

identity verification;

reward allocation;

Wallet payments;

fraud prevention;

duplicate-account detection;

campaign reporting;

Service Provider confirmation;

support;

compliance;

dispute resolution.

Users may not misuse another person’s personal information for referral purposes.

A Referrer must not register another person without consent or provide another person’s information without permission.

23. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that the Referral Service will always be available, uninterrupted, accurate, secure or error-free.

The service may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

network failures;

payment partner failures;

Service Provider delays;

cyber incidents;

power failures;

fraud controls;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed referrals, delayed rewards, failed tracking or lost opportunities caused by downtime or technical issues, unless caused by proven gross negligence.

24. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

rejected referrals;

delayed rewards;

cancelled rewards;

incorrect referral codes;

untracked referrals;

duplicate referral claims;

reward reversals;

fraudulent referrals;

Service Provider rejection;

Service Provider delays;

campaign changes;

campaign termination;

user misrepresentation;

tax obligations;

disputes between Referrers and Referred Users;

disputes inside churches or communities;

technical failures;

platform downtime;

lost income;

lost opportunity;

reputational harm.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific referral transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

25. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s participation in the Referral Service;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

fraudulent referrals;

fake accounts;

unauthorised marketing;

misleading statements;

spam or unlawful communication;

misuse of personal information;

tax disputes;

disputes with Referred Users;

disputes with churches, communities or Service Providers;

claims caused by promises or statements made by the user;

any unlawful or abusive use of the Referral Service.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

26. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to the Referral Service where the user:

breaches these Terms;

commits fraud;

creates fake accounts;

misuses referral codes;

misleads others;

spams referral links;

abuses rewards;

fails verification;

creates legal, financial, operational, reputational or compliance risk;

is suspended from the Platform.

Inkolo Connect may also cancel pending rewards, reverse paid rewards or block future referral participation where abuse or invalid referral activity is detected.

27. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these Referral Service Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Referral Service after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

28. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

29. Acceptance

By using the Inkolo Referral Service, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that referral rewards are not automatic;

accept that referrals must qualify before rewards are paid;

accept that Inkolo Connect may reject, delay, hold, reverse or cancel rewards where required;

accept responsibility for truthful and lawful referral conduct;

accept responsibility for any tax or legal obligations arising from rewards;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from referral misuse, misrepresentation, fraud, disputes or unlawful conduct.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user activates or uses the Inkolo Referral Service:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that referral rewards are not automatic and will only be paid if the referral qualifies under Inkolo Connect’s campaign rules. I agree not to create fake accounts, mislead people, spam referral links or misuse the referral system.

Suggested Referral Share Warning

Before a user shares a referral link:

Please only share your referral link honestly and respectfully. Do not promise guaranteed income, guaranteed rewards, guaranteed services, guaranteed claims, guaranteed jobs or guaranteed approval. Referral rewards are subject to verification and Inkolo Connect approval.

Suggested Reward Pending Notice

When a reward is pending:

Your referral reward is pending verification. Rewards may be delayed, rejected, reversed or cancelled if the referred user does not qualify, cancels, fails verification, reverses payment or if suspicious activity is detected.

Suggested Reward Reversal Notice

If a reward is reversed:

This referral reward has been reversed because the referral no longer qualifies under the Inkolo Referral Service Terms and Conditions. This may happen where a service is cancelled, payment is reversed, verification fails, duplicate accounts are detected or suspicious activity is identified.

JOB SEARCH SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Job Search Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, browse, list, advertise, apply for, accept, offer, perform, pay for, communicate about or otherwise use the Job Search service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Job Search service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Community Rules, Payment Terms, and any additional rules or policies published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, wallet systems, payment tools, communication tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community, church and member-management platform operated on or through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital service, dashboard, app, website, wallet, communication channel or service module.

“Job Search Service” means the platform function that allows users to search for, list, advertise, apply for, accept, offer, perform, communicate about and pay for jobs, tasks, work opportunities, services, projects or micro-jobs.

“Job Poster” means any user, employer, business, church, community, organisation or individual who lists or advertises a job, task, service request or work opportunity.

“Applicant” means any user who views, responds to, applies for or accepts a job, task, service request or work opportunity.

“Worker” means any user who performs work, services, tasks, labour or duties for a Job Poster.

“Job Listing” means any advertisement, post, description, opportunity, work request, micro-job, job offer, task, vacancy, service request or employment-related post uploaded to the platform.

“Micro-Job” means a short-term, task-based or once-off work opportunity listed through the platform.

“Employment Job” means a work opportunity that may create an employment relationship between a Job Poster and Worker.

“User” means any person who uses the Job Search Service as a Job Poster, Applicant, Worker, employer, viewer or participant.

2. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide a digital platform that allows users to connect with each other for job opportunities, task-based work, micro-jobs, service requests and employment-related communication.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not the employer, employee, recruiter, labour broker, temporary employment service, private employment agency, contractor, agent, guarantor, payroll provider, supervisor, manager or workplace controller of any user unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement.

The work agreement, employment agreement, service agreement or task agreement is concluded directly between the Job Poster and the Applicant or Worker.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee:

that any job exists;

that any Job Poster is legitimate;

that any Applicant is qualified;

that any Worker will perform properly;

that any Job Poster will pay;

that any workplace is safe;

that any job description is accurate;

that any job complies with labour law;

that a user will be hired;

that a user will earn income;

that a user is trustworthy;

that any work relationship will be successful.

Users use the Job Search Service at their own risk.

3. Important Legal Notice

South African labour law may apply to work arrangements made through the platform.

The Basic Conditions of Employment Act establishes minimum employment conditions in South Africa, and employment arrangements may create legal duties for employers.

The Employment Services Act provides for public employment services and regulates private employment agencies. It also refers to the registration and regulation of private employment agencies.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide legal, labour, tax, immigration, recruitment or employment advice.

Each Job Poster is responsible for ensuring that their listing, hiring process, work conditions, payment, contract, safety measures and employment practices comply with all applicable laws.

Each Applicant or Worker is responsible for understanding the nature of the job before accepting it.

4. Platform Facilitator Only

Duranki and Inkolo Connect act only as a technology platform and communication facilitator.

They do not:

select applicants for Job Posters;

interview applicants on behalf of Job Posters;

employ Workers;

supervise Workers;

control working hours;

set salaries or wages;

manage workplace discipline;

guarantee payment;

guarantee job performance;

guarantee job safety;

verify every job listing;

verify every user’s qualifications;

guarantee legal compliance by users.

If Duranki or Inkolo Connect provides dashboard tools, wallet tools, ratings, document-upload functions, communication tools or payment tools, this does not make Duranki or Inkolo Connect the employer, recruiter or labour broker.

5. User Eligibility

To use the Job Search Service, a user must:

be registered on the Duranki platform;

provide accurate personal and contact information;

comply with verification requirements;

comply with these Terms;

comply with community rules;

comply with applicable law;

not be suspended or banned from the platform.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse, suspend or terminate access where a user provides false information, fails verification, abuses the service, posts unlawful jobs or creates risk.

6. Minors

Users under the age of 18 may not apply for, accept, list, offer or perform work through the Job Search Service without the involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian.

Job Posters must comply with all laws relating to child labour, young workers, school-going children, workplace safety and parental consent.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove listings, block applications or suspend users where the platform suspects unlawful or unsafe work involving minors.

7. Job Poster Responsibilities

A Job Poster is fully responsible for every Job Listing they create.

The Job Poster must ensure that each listing is:

truthful;

accurate;

lawful;

not misleading;

clear about the nature of the work;

clear about payment or salary;

clear about working hours;

clear about location;

clear about whether the role is full-time, part-time, temporary, permanent, casual, contract or once-off;

clear about required skills or qualifications;

clear about risks, tools, equipment or transport requirements;

compliant with labour, tax, immigration, health and safety laws.

The Job Poster is responsible for screening, interviewing, selecting, appointing, managing, paying and supervising the Worker.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for any Job Poster’s listing, promises, conduct, workplace, payment failure or legal non-compliance.

8. Applicant and Worker Responsibilities

An Applicant or Worker must:

provide truthful information;

not exaggerate qualifications or experience;

not upload false documents;

not impersonate another person;

understand the job before accepting it;

ask questions about payment, hours, duties and safety;

arrive on time if work is accepted;

perform work honestly and competently;

comply with lawful workplace instructions;

not steal, damage property, threaten, harass or endanger anyone;

not accept work they are not qualified or legally permitted to perform;

not accept unsafe or unlawful work.

Applicants and Workers are responsible for their own decisions when accepting work.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that any Job Poster will pay, treat the Worker fairly or provide a safe workplace.

9. Job Listing Requirements

Each Job Listing should include, where applicable:

job title;

job category;

location;

remote/on-site requirement;

type of job;

expected working hours;

start date;

expected duration;

payment amount or salary range;

payment method;

payment timing;

required skills;

required experience;

required documents;

tools or equipment needed;

transport requirements;

safety risks;

contact process;

selection process;

cancellation rules.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove, hide or edit listings that are incomplete, suspicious, misleading, unlawful, discriminatory, unsafe or reputationally harmful.

10. Prohibited Job Listings

Users may not list, advertise, accept or arrange any job, task or service that is illegal, unsafe, exploitative, discriminatory or harmful.

Prohibited listings include, but are not limited to:

illegal work;

scams or fake jobs;

pyramid schemes;

unlawful commission-only schemes;

jobs requiring upfront payment from Applicants;

adult sexual services;

escort services;

drug-related work;

illegal gambling work;

unlawful debt collection;

weapons-related work;

human trafficking or forced labour;

work involving stolen goods;

work involving fake documents;

work requiring bribery or corruption;

work that discriminates unlawfully;

work that exposes users to unreasonable danger;

work involving minors unlawfully;

work that requires a licence where the Job Poster or Worker does not have one;

any job Duranki or Inkolo Connect considers unsafe, unlawful, abusive or unsuitable.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may immediately remove prohibited listings and report serious matters to authorities.

11. No Upfront Fees to Job Seekers

Job Posters may not charge Applicants or Workers an upfront fee to apply for a job, secure a job, attend an interview or be considered for work.

The Department of Employment and Labour has stated that private employment agencies are prohibited from charging work seekers fees for services rendered.

Any user who requests payment from a job seeker as a condition for employment, application, interview or placement may be suspended, banned and reported.

12. No Guarantee of Employment

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that any user will receive work, employment, income, interviews or job offers.

The Job Search Service is a listing and connection tool only.

Users should not rely on the platform as their only source of income or employment.

13. Employment Relationship

Where a Job Poster hires a Worker, the legal relationship exists between the Job Poster and the Worker.

Depending on the facts, the relationship may be:

permanent employment;

fixed-term employment;

part-time employment;

casual work;

independent contracting;

once-off service work;

micro-job work;

volunteer work.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not determine the legal classification of the relationship.

The Job Poster must ensure that the correct agreement, payment structure, legal status and compliance process is used.

If an employment relationship is created, the Job Poster is responsible for all employer obligations, including wages, leave, working hours, deductions, UIF, PAYE, workplace safety, disciplinary processes and termination procedures where applicable.

14. Independent Contractors and Micro-Jobs

Where a Worker performs a once-off task or micro-job, the parties must agree clearly on:

task description;

expected result;

price;

deadline;

location;

required materials;

who supplies tools;

payment timing;

cancellation;

completion standard.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not supervise or control micro-jobs.

The Job Poster and Worker are responsible for agreeing whether the task is completed satisfactorily.

15. Payment for Work

Where the platform allows wallet payments, payment requests, QR payments or other payment tools, users must comply with the Wallet Terms and Payment Terms.

Payment may be made:

after completion;

in milestones;

upfront, if agreed;

through a platform payment request;

through wallet transfer;

through any other approved payment method.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may hold, delay, block, reverse or review payments where:

fraud is suspected;

a dispute is raised;

the transaction appears unlawful;

a user fails verification;

a payment partner requires it;

chargeback risk exists;

the platform detects suspicious activity.

Payment through the platform does not mean Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees the quality of work, payment by the Job Poster, performance by the Worker or legal compliance of the job.

16. No Payroll Responsibility

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for payroll, payslips, tax deductions, UIF, PAYE, employment benefits, leave pay, overtime, minimum wage compliance or employment records unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement.

The Job Poster is responsible for all employer obligations where applicable.

The Worker is responsible for their own tax and legal obligations where applicable.

17. Interviews and Meetings

Users may arrange interviews, calls, chats or meetings through the platform.

Users are responsible for their own safety when attending interviews or meeting other users.

Applicants should avoid unsafe locations, suspicious requests, unnecessary sharing of private information and any job that requires payment to apply.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for theft, assault, harassment, loss, injury or harm arising from interviews, meetings or workplace visits.

18. Workplace Safety

Job Posters must provide a safe working environment where work is performed.

Job Posters must disclose any known safety risks, including:

working at heights;

electrical work;

construction work;

driving;

machinery;

chemicals;

heavy lifting;

night work;

isolated locations;

public-facing risk;

crime-prone areas;

tools or protective clothing required.

Workers must not accept work that is unsafe, unlawful or beyond their competence.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for workplace injuries, accidents, illness, death, unsafe conditions or damage arising from work arranged through the platform.

19. Qualifications, Licences and Background Checks

Some jobs may require qualifications, licences, permits, police clearance, professional registration, driving licences or specialised training.

The Job Poster is responsible for verifying these requirements before appointing a Worker.

The Worker is responsible for ensuring they are qualified and legally allowed to perform the work.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may offer document-upload or verification tools, but this does not guarantee that a user is qualified, honest, suitable or legally compliant.

20. Discrimination and Fair Treatment

Users may not post or respond to listings in a way that unlawfully discriminates on the basis of race, gender, sex, pregnancy, marital status, ethnic or social origin, colour, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, conscience, belief, culture, language, birth or any other prohibited ground.

Job Posters must treat Applicants and Workers fairly and respectfully.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove discriminatory listings and suspend users who abuse the service.

21. Documents and Personal Information

The Job Search Service may allow users to upload or share CVs, identity documents, certificates, licences, references, work history, banking information, location information or other personal information.

Users must only upload true and lawful documents.

Job Posters must only request information that is necessary for the job.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may process personal information for registration, verification, job matching, applications, communication, fraud prevention, dispute handling, safety, reporting and legal compliance.

POPIA regulates the processing of personal information by public and private bodies and creates conditions for lawful processing.

Users may not misuse, sell, share or disclose another user’s personal information obtained through the platform.

22. Communication and Chat

The platform may allow Job Posters, Applicants and Workers to communicate through chat, call, notifications or messages.

Users must not:

harass others;

threaten others;

send sexual content;

send abusive content;

send spam;

send fake job offers;

send fake payment confirmations;

request bribes;

request upfront fees;

request illegal work;

share another user’s private information unlawfully.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may monitor, store, review or disclose communications where necessary for safety, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, legal compliance or enforcement of these Terms.

23. Ratings and Reviews

The platform may allow users to rate Job Posters, Applicants or Workers.

Ratings are intended to support trust and accountability but do not guarantee that a user is honest, safe, qualified, reliable or legally compliant.

Users must provide truthful, fair and lawful reviews.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove false, abusive, defamatory or manipulated reviews.

Repeated poor ratings may result in restricted access or suspension.

24. Fraud, Scams and Abuse

Users may not use the Job Search Service for fraud, fake jobs, fake documents, fake applications, identity theft, phishing, money laundering, illegal work, bribery, harassment, exploitation or any unlawful conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may use fraud-prevention measures, including:

identity checks;

device checks;

wallet monitoring;

listing review;

user reports;

suspicious pattern detection;

payment controls;

manual moderation.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may share information with banks, payment providers, law enforcement, regulators, community administrators or affected users where legally permitted or required.

25. Platform Moderation Rights

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove job listings;

hide listings;

suspend users;

restrict wallet access;

request verification;

block job categories;

block payment requests;

review communications;

limit account functions;

ban users;

report unlawful conduct.

These rights may be exercised where Duranki or Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, reputational, safety, community or compliance risk.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for loss of income, job opportunities, applicants, reputation or business caused by moderation actions taken in good faith.

26. Community and Church Environment

Where the Job Search Service is used within a church, community or Inkolo Connect environment, users must act respectfully and in line with the rules and values of that community.

Community membership does not mean that Duranki, Inkolo Connect, KZNCC, a church, pastor, bishop or community leader guarantees any job, employer, worker, applicant or payment.

Churches, pastors, bishops, KZNCC, community leaders and administrators are not liable for job listings or work arrangements unless they personally act as the Job Poster or employer.

27. No Endorsement

The appearance of a Job Listing on the platform does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect has approved, verified, endorsed, inspected, certified or recommended the job, Job Poster, Applicant or Worker.

Users must conduct their own checks before applying, hiring, accepting work or making payment.

28. Disputes Between Users

Users must first attempt to resolve disputes directly and in good faith.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may, but are not obliged to, assist by reviewing:

job listing information;

chat messages;

payment records;

completion evidence;

ratings;

uploaded documents;

user complaints.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not act as a court, arbitrator, employer, union, labour inspector, debt collector or legal representative.

Any decision by Duranki or Inkolo Connect to hold, release, reverse or block payment is a platform risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

29. Labour Disputes

If a Worker and Job Poster create an employment relationship, any labour dispute must be handled directly between them according to applicable labour laws and dispute-resolution processes.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not responsible for unfair dismissal claims, wage disputes, disciplinary disputes, workplace injury claims, discrimination claims, leave disputes, overtime disputes, UIF disputes or employment-contract disputes between users.

30. Service Availability

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that the Job Search Service will always be available, uninterrupted, secure or error-free.

The service may be unavailable because of maintenance, upgrades, network failure, technical issues, cyber incidents, third-party failures or events beyond the platform’s control.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for missed job opportunities, failed applications, lost messages, delayed payments or loss of income caused by downtime or technical failure.

31. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, penalty, injury, death, expense or liability arising from:

job listings;

fake jobs;

scams;

non-payment;

poor work performance;

workplace injuries;

workplace accidents;

unlawful employment practices;

discrimination by users;

harassment by users;

unsafe workplaces;

failed interviews;

failed applications;

false documents;

user misconduct;

payment disputes;

tax or UIF issues;

labour disputes;

employment termination;

platform downtime;

third-party provider failure.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of profit, loss of opportunity, reputational harm or emotional distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific job transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

32. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s Job Listing;

the user’s application;

the user’s work arrangement;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

the user’s employment practices;

workplace injury or death;

non-payment or underpayment;

fraud or scams;

discrimination or harassment;

tax, UIF or payroll non-compliance;

misuse of personal information;

disputes with another user;

claims by employees, contractors, regulators, inspectors, payment providers or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

33. Suspension and Termination

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access immediately if the user:

breaches these Terms;

posts fake jobs;

requests upfront fees;

fails verification;

provides false information;

uploads false documents;

refuses to pay for completed work;

performs work dishonestly;

harasses or threatens another user;

posts unlawful or discriminatory listings;

misuses wallet payments;

creates legal, safety, reputational or financial risk.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may also suspend or terminate the Job Search Service, or any part of it, at any time.

34. Reporting Problems

Users must report suspicious jobs, fake listings, fraud, abusive behaviour, non-payment, unsafe work, illegal work or requests for upfront fees through the platform’s reporting tools or support channels.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may investigate reports but are not obliged to resolve every report in favour of the reporting user.

Users should report crimes, labour violations or serious safety issues directly to the relevant authorities.

35. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the Job Search Service after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

36. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled in accordance with South African law, subject to rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

37. Acceptance

By accessing or using the Job Search Service, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

agree to be bound by these Terms;

understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect are platform facilitators only;

understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not the employer, recruiter or labour broker unless expressly agreed in writing;

accept responsibility for their own listings, applications, work arrangements, payments, safety and legal compliance.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user applies for or posts a job:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Job Search Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform and are not responsible for the job, employer, applicant, worker, payment, workplace, safety, employment contract, labour dispute or any loss arising from my use of this service.

Suggested Job Poster Checkbox

Before a user posts a job:

I confirm that this job listing is true, lawful and not misleading. I accept full responsibility for the job, payment, working conditions, safety, legal compliance and any employment or contractor relationship created with the worker.

Suggested Worker / Applicant Checkbox

Before applying or accepting work:

I confirm that I understand the job details and accept that I apply for or accept this work at my own risk. I understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee the job poster, payment, workplace safety, employment terms or outcome of this opportunity.

VALUE-ADDED SERVICES TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Value-Added Services Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, browse, purchase, redeem, receive, send, pay for or otherwise use value-added services made available through the Inkolo Connect platform.

By using any Value-Added Service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Acceptable Use Policy, Community Terms and any service-specific terms, provider terms, network terms, merchant terms, utility terms or voucher terms applicable to the selected service.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools and service modules.

“VAS” or “Value-Added Services” means digital or electronic services, products, vouchers, tokens, payments or benefits made available through the Platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform or purchases, receives or redeems VAS.

“Service Provider” means any third-party provider, network operator, mobile network, electricity provider, municipality, utility provider, voucher issuer, payment provider, biller, merchant, aggregator, content provider or partner responsible for supplying or fulfilling a VAS product.

“VAS Transaction” means any purchase, payment, redemption, recharge, token generation, voucher issue, bill payment, subscription payment, top-up or other transaction relating to VAS.

“Wallet” means the digital wallet facility made available to registered users on the Platform.

2. Types of VAS Services

The Platform may allow users to purchase or access VAS, including but not limited to:

airtime;

mobile data;

SMS bundles;

voice bundles;

electricity tokens;

water or utility payments;

bill payments;

prepaid vouchers;

shopping vouchers;

fuel vouchers;

gaming vouchers;

streaming or entertainment vouchers;

education vouchers;

transport vouchers;

rewards or benefit vouchers;

subscription services;

digital content;

any other VAS introduced on the Platform from time to time.

Not all services will be available to all users, communities, churches, areas or devices.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect provides a digital platform through which users may access and pay for VAS supplied by third-party Service Providers.

Unless expressly stated otherwise in writing, Inkolo Connect is not the actual supplier, network operator, electricity provider, utility provider, municipality, voucher issuer, biller or merchant responsible for the underlying VAS.

The contract for the supply of the VAS is between the user and the relevant Service Provider, network, biller, utility, voucher issuer or merchant, unless expressly stated otherwise.

Inkolo Connect may facilitate:

service display;

user selection;

payment processing;

Wallet deduction;

transaction submission;

token or voucher display;

transaction history;

support routing;

refund or reversal requests where available.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee successful fulfilment by third-party Service Providers.

4. User Responsibility

The user is responsible for ensuring that all information entered for a VAS Transaction is correct before confirming payment.

This includes, where applicable:

mobile number;

network operator;

recipient name;

electricity meter number;

account number;

biller name;

municipality or utility;

voucher type;

voucher value;

product bundle;

subscription type;

beneficiary details;

payment amount.

Once a VAS Transaction is submitted, it may not be reversible, refundable or cancellable.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for losses caused by incorrect information entered by the user.

5. Airtime and Data

When purchasing airtime, data, SMS bundles, voice bundles or mobile products, the user must confirm:

the correct mobile number;

the correct network;

the correct product;

the correct bundle amount;

the correct recipient.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible if the user purchases airtime or data for the wrong number, wrong network or wrong recipient.

Airtime and data products are supplied according to the rules of the relevant mobile network operator.

Expiry dates, bundle rules, rollover rules, usage limits, promotional rules, network coverage and service quality are controlled by the mobile network operator and not by Inkolo Connect.

6. Electricity Tokens and Utility Payments

When purchasing electricity or making utility payments, the user must confirm:

the correct meter number;

the correct municipality or electricity provider;

the correct amount;

the correct tariff type;

the correct property or account details.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible where a user enters the wrong meter number, purchases for the wrong property, selects the wrong provider or pays the wrong account.

Electricity tokens and utility payments are supplied by the relevant municipality, utility provider, electricity provider, token provider, vending provider or aggregator.

Inkolo Connect does not control tariffs, fees, free basic electricity allocation, meter blocking, debt deductions, arrears deductions, municipal system errors or token acceptance by the meter.

The amount of electricity received may be less than the amount paid if the utility provider deducts arrears, service fees, levies, debt recovery, tariff charges or other permitted deductions.

7. Vouchers and Digital Codes

Voucher purchases may result in a digital code, PIN, token, barcode, reference number or redemption instruction being issued.

The user is responsible for keeping voucher codes confidential and secure.

Inkolo Connect is not liable if a voucher code is lost, stolen, shared, redeemed by another person or used incorrectly after it has been delivered to the user.

Voucher use is subject to the voucher issuer’s terms, including expiry dates, redemption locations, exclusions, value limits, product restrictions and refund rules.

Vouchers are generally not exchangeable for cash unless expressly permitted by the voucher issuer.

8. Bill Payments

Where bill payment services are made available, the user is responsible for ensuring that the correct biller, account number, reference number and payment amount are entered.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that the biller will allocate the payment correctly if the user provides incorrect details.

Bill payments may take time to reflect with the biller, and the user remains responsible for avoiding late-payment penalties, disconnections, interest, fees or service interruptions.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for late allocation, disconnection, penalties or account disputes caused by biller delays, incorrect user details or third-party processing delays.

9. Payment and Wallet Deductions

VAS may be paid for using:

the user’s Wallet;

card payment;

bank payment;

QR payment;

any other approved payment method made available on the Platform.

A VAS Transaction may only be processed if payment is successful and sufficient funds are available.

The user authorises Inkolo Connect to deduct the transaction amount, platform fee, service fee, payment fee or any applicable charge from the user’s Wallet or selected payment method.

Payment through the Platform does not guarantee successful delivery of the VAS where fulfilment depends on a third-party Service Provider.

10. Fees and Charges

Inkolo Connect, Payment Partners and Service Providers may charge fees for VAS Transactions.

Fees may include:

platform fees;

service fees;

transaction fees;

payment processing fees;

vending fees;

network fees;

utility fees;

biller fees;

voucher fees;

reversal or refund fees.

Fees may be included in the price or displayed separately before confirmation.

By confirming a VAS Transaction, the user accepts the applicable fees.

11. Transaction Confirmation

A VAS Transaction is considered submitted once the user confirms the transaction and payment is processed.

A transaction confirmation, receipt, reference number or status message does not always mean that the third-party Service Provider has fully completed fulfilment.

Some transactions may reflect as pending, processing, delayed, failed, reversed or disputed.

The Platform transaction history is provided for user convenience and reconciliation but may be subject to correction where technical errors, reversals or provider updates occur.

12. Failed, Delayed or Pending Transactions

VAS Transactions may fail, delay or remain pending due to:

incorrect user details;

network downtime;

mobile operator failure;

utility provider failure;

municipality system issues;

aggregator delays;

Payment Partner delays;

bank delays;

insufficient funds;

fraud checks;

compliance checks;

technical errors;

power outages;

internet failures;

system maintenance;

third-party provider downtime.

Inkolo Connect will make reasonable efforts to display the transaction status and assist with support queries, but Inkolo Connect does not guarantee instant fulfilment.

13. Refunds and Reversals

Refunds or reversals are not automatic.

A refund or reversal may be considered where:

payment was deducted but the transaction failed;

the Service Provider confirms non-fulfilment;

duplicate deduction occurred;

technical error occurred;

the transaction is eligible for reversal under provider rules.

A refund or reversal may not be available where:

the user entered incorrect details;

airtime or data was sent to the wrong number;

electricity was purchased for the wrong meter;

a voucher code was issued;

a voucher was redeemed;

the Service Provider fulfilled the transaction;

the product is non-refundable;

the user changed their mind;

the service provider rules do not allow reversal.

Inkolo Connect is not required to refund users from its own funds where the VAS was fulfilled by a Service Provider or where the error was caused by the user.

Approved refunds may be paid back to the user’s Wallet or original payment method, subject to provider rules, payment partner rules, fraud checks and processing timelines.

14. Incorrect Recipient, Number, Meter or Account

The user accepts full responsibility for checking recipient details before confirming a transaction.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

airtime sent to the wrong number;

data sent to the wrong number;

electricity bought for the wrong meter;

bills paid to the wrong account;

vouchers bought in the wrong amount;

payments made to the wrong biller;

products bought for the wrong network;

user typing errors;

user misunderstanding of product selection.

Inkolo Connect may try to assist where possible, but recovery or reversal is not guaranteed.

15. Product Availability

VAS products may be changed, removed, delayed, suspended or discontinued without notice.

Service availability may depend on:

mobile network availability;

electricity provider availability;

municipality system availability;

voucher issuer stock;

biller participation;

Payment Partner availability;

platform configuration;

user location;

user verification status;

service provider rules.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that every VAS product will always be available.

16. Product Pricing

VAS pricing may change at any time due to Service Provider pricing, network pricing, utility tariffs, voucher issuer pricing, fees, taxes, levies or platform charges.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for changes made by third-party Service Providers.

The price displayed at confirmation is the price applicable to that transaction, unless a technical or pricing error is identified.

Inkolo Connect may cancel, reverse or correct transactions affected by obvious pricing errors, system errors or provider errors.

17. Product Rules and Expiry

VAS products may have specific rules, including:

expiry dates;

usage limits;

network restrictions;

redemption restrictions;

geographic restrictions;

once-off use;

non-transferability;

no cash conversion;

minimum and maximum values;

promotional rules;

service provider terms.

The user is responsible for reading and complying with all product rules.

Inkolo Connect is not liable where a user fails to use a product before expiry or breaches provider rules.

18. Fraud Prevention and Compliance

Inkolo Connect may monitor VAS Transactions for fraud, abuse, suspicious activity, money laundering, chargeback risk, voucher abuse, account takeover, unauthorised card use or other unlawful activity.

Inkolo Connect may:

delay transactions;

block transactions;

reverse transactions;

freeze Wallet access;

request verification;

limit transaction values;

suspend accounts;

report suspicious activity;

refuse service.

Users may not use VAS to launder money, commit fraud, resell unlawfully, abuse promotions, exploit system errors, purchase prohibited products or assist unlawful activity.

19. Transaction Limits

Inkolo Connect, Payment Partners and Service Providers may impose minimum and maximum transaction limits.

Limits may apply per transaction, per day, per month, per user, per Wallet, per product, per network or per risk category.

Limits may be changed at any time for risk, compliance, operational or provider reasons.

20. No Resale Without Permission

Users may not resell VAS products, vouchers, tokens or digital codes unless expressly authorised by Inkolo Connect and the relevant Service Provider.

Unauthorised resale may result in suspension, forfeiture of benefits, reversal of transactions or legal action.

21. No Warranty by Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect does not warrant that:

any VAS will always be available;

any transaction will be instant;

any provider system will be online;

any product will meet the user’s expectations;

network coverage will be available;

electricity meters will accept tokens;

billers will allocate payments immediately;

voucher issuers will have stock;

third-party systems will operate without error.

VAS products are supplied subject to the applicable Service Provider’s terms and system availability.

22. User Support

Users may contact Inkolo Connect support for VAS Transaction queries.

The user may be required to provide:

transaction reference;

date and time;

product selected;

amount paid;

mobile number, meter number, biller reference or voucher reference;

proof of payment;

screenshots;

any other information reasonably required.

Inkolo Connect may need to refer the query to the relevant Service Provider, Payment Partner, network, municipality, utility or voucher issuer.

Resolution times may depend on third-party response times.

23. Service Provider Decisions

The relevant Service Provider may make final decisions regarding:

whether a transaction was fulfilled;

whether a token was generated;

whether airtime or data was delivered;

whether a voucher was issued;

whether a bill was paid;

whether a refund or reversal is allowed;

whether a product expired;

whether a code was redeemed.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for decisions made by Service Providers, networks, utilities, billers or voucher issuers.

24. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

user registration;

VAS purchases;

payment processing;

Wallet deductions;

transaction records;

fraud prevention;

support;

dispute resolution;

compliance;

reporting.

Personal information may be shared with Service Providers, Payment Partners, banks, networks, utilities, municipalities, billers, voucher issuers, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse another person’s mobile number, account number, meter number, identity information or payment information.

25. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee uninterrupted access to VAS.

The service may be unavailable because of:

scheduled maintenance;

emergency maintenance;

technical failures;

bank downtime;

Payment Partner downtime;

Service Provider downtime;

mobile network failure;

utility system failure;

municipality system failure;

internet failure;

power failure;

cyber incidents;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss caused by service interruption, downtime or delayed transactions.

26. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

incorrect mobile numbers;

incorrect meter numbers;

incorrect account numbers;

wrong network selection;

wrong product selection;

failed VAS Transactions;

delayed VAS Transactions;

provider system downtime;

mobile network failures;

electricity provider failures;

municipality failures;

biller allocation delays;

voucher expiry;

voucher theft after delivery;

voucher redemption by another person;

user error;

unauthorised account access;

fraud or scams;

payment reversals;

chargebacks;

Wallet deductions;

third-party provider decisions;

loss of income;

loss of opportunity;

emotional distress or inconvenience.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific VAS Transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

27. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s VAS Transaction;

incorrect details entered by the user;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

fraud or unlawful use;

unauthorised resale;

misuse of vouchers or tokens;

payment disputes;

chargebacks;

unauthorised card use;

disputes with recipients, networks, utilities, billers, municipalities, voucher issuers or Service Providers.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

28. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to VAS where the user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

commits fraud;

misuses vouchers or tokens;

performs excessive reversals or chargebacks;

uses incorrect or false information;

resells without permission;

creates legal, financial, operational, security, reputational or compliance risk;

violates Service Provider rules;

violates payment rules.

Inkolo Connect may also suspend or discontinue any VAS product or service at any time.

29. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these VAS Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of VAS after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

30. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

31. Acceptance

By using VAS on the Platform, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is a platform facilitator;

accept that VAS products are supplied by third-party Service Providers;

accept responsibility for checking all numbers, meter numbers, account numbers, products and amounts before confirming payment;

understand that VAS Transactions may not be reversible once submitted;

accept responsibility for their own use of vouchers, tokens, airtime, data, electricity and bill payments;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from user error, provider failure, incorrect details or misuse of the service.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user purchases any VAS product:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Value-Added Services Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect only facilitates the transaction and that the selected product is supplied by the relevant service provider, network, utility, biller or voucher issuer. I confirm that the number, meter number, account number, product and amount are correct before I proceed.

Suggested Airtime/Data Confirmation Text

Please confirm that the mobile number, network and product are correct. Airtime and data purchases may not be reversible once submitted. Inkolo Connect is not responsible if you send airtime or data to the wrong number or select the wrong network.

Suggested Electricity Confirmation Text

Please confirm that the meter number, provider and amount are correct. Electricity purchases may not be reversible once submitted. Inkolo Connect is not responsible if electricity is purchased for the wrong meter number.

Suggested Voucher Confirmation Text

Please keep your voucher code safe. Once a voucher code is issued, it may not be refundable or replaceable if lost, shared, stolen, expired or redeemed by another person.
