EDUU SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These EduU Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, register for, participate in, pay for, complete, receive, upload content to, communicate about or otherwise use educational programmes, courses, training, workshops, learning material, assessments, certificates or skills-development services made available through the EduU service on the Inkolo Connect platform.

By using EduU, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Acceptable Use Policy, Community Terms and any additional service-specific terms, training provider terms, course rules, programme rules, assessment rules or certification rules applicable to the selected educational programme.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, service modules, administrators, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools and service modules.

“EduU” means the education and training service module on the Platform that allows users to access, register for, participate in or complete educational programmes, training, courses, workshops, lessons, assessments, skills programmes or learning-related services.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses EduU, including learners, students, parents, guardians, facilitators, tutors, trainers, instructors, service providers and administrators.

“Learner” means a user who enrols in, participates in or completes an educational programme through EduU.

“Training Provider” means any third-party school, college, academy, institution, tutor, instructor, facilitator, content provider, assessment body, skills provider, employer partner, NGO, church, community organisation or other provider responsible for supplying educational content, training, instruction, assessment or certification.

“Course” means any educational programme, lesson, course, workshop, class, training session, learning path, module, webinar, assessment or skills programme made available through EduU.

“Course Material” means any videos, documents, notes, slides, worksheets, assessments, exams, quizzes, assignments, templates, resources, recordings or other learning content made available through EduU.

“Certificate” means any completion certificate, attendance certificate, skills certificate, statement of results, badge, record of participation or other proof of completion issued by Inkolo Connect, a Training Provider or a third-party certification body.

2. Purpose of EduU

EduU is intended to provide access to educational programmes, skills-development opportunities, online courses, community training, workshops and learning support.

EduU may include:

online courses;

in-person training;

hybrid learning programmes;

skills-development programmes;

church or community training;

youth development programmes;

entrepreneurship training;

digital skills training;

financial literacy training;

employment-readiness training;

second-chance education programmes;

vocational training;

exam preparation;

mentorship programmes;

workshops and seminars;

any other approved educational programme made available on the Platform.

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform and may facilitate access, registration, communication, payment, learning records and document delivery.

Unless expressly stated in writing, Inkolo Connect is not the school, university, college, accredited training provider, examination body, employer, tutor, instructor or certification authority.

3. Role of Inkolo Connect

Inkolo Connect acts as the Platform provider and education access facilitator.

Inkolo Connect may assist by:

displaying Courses;

allowing users to register for Courses;

collecting or facilitating payment;

providing access to Course Material;

enabling communication between Learners and Training Providers;

tracking enrolment and participation;

displaying certificates or completion records where available;

providing dashboards and reporting tools;

sending reminders and notifications;

supporting document uploads;

routing support queries.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee:

that a Course will meet the Learner’s expectations;

that a Learner will pass;

that a Learner will complete the Course;

that a Certificate will be accepted by an employer or institution;

that a Course is accredited unless expressly stated;

that a Course will lead to employment;

that a Course will lead to income;

that a Training Provider will always be available;

that every learning outcome will be achieved.

4. Third-Party Training Providers

Some Courses may be provided by third-party Training Providers.

Where a Course is provided by a Training Provider, the relationship for the educational content, instruction, assessments, course delivery and certification is between the Learner and the Training Provider, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

Training Provider conduct;

course quality;

instructor availability;

assessment standards;

course delays;

course cancellations;

certification delays;

accreditation claims made by the Training Provider;

employment promises made by the Training Provider;

disputes between Learners and Training Providers.

Training Providers remain responsible for their own content, instructors, course delivery, assessment processes, certifications, accreditation, compliance and learner support.

5. No Accreditation Guarantee

Not all Courses on EduU are accredited.

A Course is only accredited if this is expressly stated in the Course description and supported by the relevant Training Provider or accrediting body.

Unless clearly stated in writing, a Course should be treated as non-accredited training, personal development, skills development, community learning or informal education.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Course, Certificate, badge or completion record will be recognised by:

employers;

universities;

colleges;

schools;

government departments;

professional bodies;

SETAs;

regulators;

scholarship providers;

any other institution.

Users must check accreditation status before registering or paying.

6. No Guarantee of Results, Employment or Income

EduU is intended to support learning and skills development, but results depend on the Learner’s own effort, attendance, participation, ability, resources, device access, internet access and completion of required activities.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee:

academic success;

exam success;

assessment success;

employment;

promotion;

income;

business success;

acceptance into another institution;

qualification recognition;

scholarship approval;

funding approval;

job placement.

Any employment, income, business or career outcome remains the responsibility of the Learner and any relevant employer, institution or Training Provider.

7. User Eligibility

To use EduU, a user may be required to:

register on the Platform;

verify their mobile number;

verify their identity;

provide accurate personal information;

accept these Terms;

accept the applicable Course rules;

pay applicable fees;

meet Course entry requirements;

provide required documents;

have access to a suitable device and internet connection where required.

Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider may refuse, delay, suspend or cancel access where the user does not meet the relevant requirements.

8. Minors

Users under the age of 18 may require the consent and involvement of a parent or legal guardian to register for or participate in EduU.

Parents or guardians are responsible for:

approving the minor’s participation;

reviewing Course suitability;

paying applicable fees;

supervising online access where necessary;

ensuring the minor behaves appropriately;

understanding any risks related to online learning, communication and data use.

Inkolo Connect may restrict access to certain Courses for minors where required by law, policy, safety rules or Course requirements.

9. Course Registration

Registration for a Course may be subject to availability, eligibility, payment, verification and acceptance by Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider.

A Course registration is not confirmed until the platform or Training Provider confirms enrolment.

Inkolo Connect may cancel or reject registration where:

information is incomplete;

payment fails;

the Course is full;

the Course is cancelled;

the user is not eligible;

fraud is suspected;

the user breaches these Terms;

the Training Provider rejects the registration.

10. Course Fees and Payments

EduU Courses may be free, sponsored, subsidised or paid.

Where Course fees apply, the user is responsible for paying the fees according to the payment terms shown on the Platform or provided by the Training Provider.

Payments may be made through:

Wallet;

card payment;

bank payment;

voucher;

sponsor funding;

grant funding;

employer payment;

any other approved payment method.

Inkolo Connect may act only as a payment facilitator.

Payment through the Platform does not make Inkolo Connect responsible for Course delivery where the Course is supplied by a Training Provider.

11. Refunds and Cancellations

Refunds and cancellations are governed by the relevant Course rules, Training Provider terms, sponsor rules, payment terms and applicable law.

Refunds may not be available where:

the user has accessed Course Material;

the Course has started;

digital content has been delivered;

the user failed to attend;

the user did not complete the Course;

the user changed their mind;

the cancellation period has expired;

the Training Provider’s rules do not allow a refund.

Inkolo Connect is not required to refund users from its own funds where the Course is provided by a Training Provider.

Where a refund is approved, it may be processed to the Wallet, original payment method or other approved payment channel, subject to payment partner rules and processing timelines.

12. Course Changes, Delays and Cancellations

Inkolo Connect or the Training Provider may change, postpone, replace or cancel a Course where necessary.

This may happen because of:

low enrolment;

instructor unavailability;

technical issues;

venue issues;

funding changes;

sponsor changes;

Course content updates;

safety concerns;

regulatory issues;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss of income, travel costs, opportunity loss, disappointment or inconvenience caused by Course changes, delays or cancellations.

13. Learner Responsibilities

Learners must:

provide accurate information;

attend required sessions;

complete assignments honestly;

respect instructors and other Learners;

follow Course rules;

use Course Material lawfully;

not cheat or plagiarise;

not submit another person’s work;

not disrupt classes;

not harass or abuse others;

not share login details;

not record sessions without permission;

not redistribute Course Material without permission.

A Learner who breaches Course rules may be suspended, removed, failed, denied a Certificate or banned from EduU.

14. Training Provider Responsibilities

Training Providers are responsible for:

Course content;

Course accuracy;

instructor conduct;

learning outcomes;

Course delivery;

assessments;

Course schedules;

certificates issued by them;

accreditation claims;

learner support;

compliance with applicable laws;

suitability of Course Material;

safety of in-person training venues.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for failures, negligence, misrepresentations or disputes caused by Training Providers.

15. Assessments and Examinations

Some Courses may include assessments, tests, exams, assignments, practical tasks or participation requirements.

Assessment rules will be set by Inkolo Connect, the Training Provider or the relevant assessment body.

The user must comply with all assessment rules.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that a Learner will pass or that assessment results will be accepted by any third party.

Assessment results may be withheld where:

fees are unpaid;

fraud is suspected;

cheating is suspected;

identity verification fails;

required work was not completed;

the Training Provider has not released results;

administrative checks are incomplete.

16. Certificates and Completion Records

A Certificate may be issued where a Learner meets the completion requirements of a Course.

A Certificate may be an attendance certificate, completion certificate, informal certificate, platform certificate, provider certificate or accredited certificate, depending on the Course.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that any Certificate will be accepted by employers, institutions, regulators or other third parties unless expressly stated and supported by the relevant accrediting body.

Certificates may be withheld, cancelled or corrected where fraud, non-payment, cheating, incorrect information or administrative error is identified.

17. Course Material and Intellectual Property

All Course Material is protected by intellectual property rights.

Users may access Course Material only for their own personal learning or authorised educational use.

Users may not, without permission:

copy Course Material;

sell Course Material;

upload Course Material elsewhere;

share Course videos or documents;

reproduce assessments;

record live sessions;

distribute login access;

remove copyright notices;

use Course Material for commercial training.

Course Material remains the property of Inkolo Connect, the Training Provider, content owner or licensor.

Breach of this clause may result in suspension, legal action and loss of access.

18. User-Generated Content

Users may be allowed to upload assignments, comments, questions, posts, documents, videos, photos or other content.

The user is responsible for all content they upload.

Users must not upload content that is:

unlawful;

false;

defamatory;

abusive;

discriminatory;

sexually explicit;

violent;

plagiarised;

infected with malware;

infringing intellectual property rights;

harmful to children;

in breach of these Terms.

Inkolo Connect may remove or restrict content that breaches these Terms or creates legal, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

19. Online Learning and Technical Requirements

Users are responsible for ensuring that they have the required device, internet connection, data, software, email access, phone number and technical ability to participate in online Courses.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for a user’s inability to access a Course due to:

poor internet connection;

lack of data;

device failure;

outdated software;

incorrect login details;

email failure;

mobile network issues;

user technical problems.

Where a Course requires online participation, failure to connect or attend may affect completion.

20. In-Person Training

Where EduU includes in-person training, workshops, practical sessions or events, the Training Provider or event organiser is responsible for venue safety, attendance control, instructor availability and training delivery.

Users attend in-person training at their own risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

accidents;

injuries;

illness;

theft;

lost property;

transport issues;

venue problems;

food safety;

cancelled sessions;

personal disputes at the venue.

21. Code of Conduct

Users must behave respectfully in all EduU spaces, including online classes, chats, forums, workshops and in-person sessions.

Users may not:

harass others;

threaten others;

use hate speech;

bully others;

disrupt learning;

share offensive content;

send spam;

impersonate instructors or learners;

solicit money unlawfully;

misuse the learning environment.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or remove users who breach the code of conduct.

22. Communication

EduU may use platform notifications, SMS, WhatsApp, email, in-app messages, calls or other channels to communicate Course updates, reminders, schedules, results, payment notices and support messages.

Users are responsible for keeping their contact details updated.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed messages caused by incorrect contact details, phone issues, network problems or user failure to check messages.

23. Personal Information

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

registration;

identity verification;

Course enrolment;

attendance tracking;

assessments;

certificate issuing;

payment processing;

learner support;

sponsor reporting;

programme reporting;

compliance;

fraud prevention;

communication;

dispute handling.

Personal information may be shared with Training Providers, sponsors, employers, community partners, government funders, assessment bodies, certification bodies, payment partners, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users must not misuse another Learner’s, instructor’s or Training Provider’s personal information.

24. Sponsored, Funded or Grant-Based Programmes

Some EduU programmes may be funded, sponsored, subsidised or supported by third parties.

Participation in such programmes may be subject to additional rules, including:

eligibility requirements;

attendance requirements;

reporting requirements;

identity verification;

proof of completion;

demographic reporting;

sponsor approval;

funding availability;

programme audits.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that a user will be accepted into a funded programme or that funding will continue.

Where a sponsor, funder or grant provider changes or withdraws funding, the programme may be changed, suspended or cancelled.

25. No Medical, Legal, Financial or Professional Advice

Course content may include general educational information.

Unless expressly stated and provided by a properly authorised professional, EduU content does not constitute medical advice, legal advice, financial advice, investment advice, tax advice, counselling advice or professional advice.

Users should seek qualified professional advice before relying on Course content for important decisions.

26. Fraud, Cheating and Misrepresentation

Users may not:

submit false documents;

use another person’s identity;

cheat in assessments;

plagiarise work;

forge certificates;

manipulate attendance records;

falsely claim completion;

sell Course access;

hack or misuse systems;

create fake learner accounts.

Inkolo Connect may suspend users, cancel enrolments, withhold certificates, reverse rewards, notify Training Providers and take legal action where fraud or cheating is suspected.

27. Disputes

Users must first attempt to resolve Course-related disputes through the relevant support process.

Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing:

registration records;

payment records;

attendance records;

course access logs;

communication records;

assessment submissions;

Training Provider feedback.

Inkolo Connect is not a court, arbitrator, school authority, university, regulator or accreditation body.

Disputes about Course delivery, instructor conduct, assessment results or certificates issued by a Training Provider must be handled with the relevant Training Provider.

28. Platform Moderation Rights

Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove Course content;

suspend Course access;

suspend users;

restrict communication tools;

remove abusive content;

request verification;

withhold certificates;

block payments;

report fraud;

terminate access to EduU.

These rights may be exercised where Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, security, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

29. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that EduU will always be available, uninterrupted, secure or error-free.

EduU may be unavailable because of:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

internet failures;

mobile network failures;

power failures;

payment partner failures;

Training Provider system failures;

cyber incidents;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for missed classes, delayed access, lost progress, delayed certificates or failed submissions caused by downtime, unless caused by proven gross negligence.

30. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Training Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

Course dissatisfaction;

Course cancellation;

Course delay;

Training Provider failure;

instructor conduct;

failed assessments;

non-completion;

certificate rejection;

lack of accreditation;

employment not being obtained;

income not being earned;

technical access problems;

device or internet failure;

missed classes;

incorrect user information;

plagiarism or cheating by users;

in-person training incidents;

learner disputes;

sponsor or funder withdrawal;

third-party decisions;

platform downtime.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of opportunity, loss of employment, loss of profit, reputational harm or emotional distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific EduU transaction giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

31. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Training Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of EduU;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

false information supplied by the user;

plagiarism, cheating or fraud;

misuse of Course Material;

unauthorised sharing of content;

harassment or misconduct;

misuse of personal information;

disputes with Training Providers, instructors, Learners, sponsors or third parties;

any claim arising from the user’s participation in a Course, assessment, workshop or training event.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

32. Suspension and Termination

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate a user’s access to EduU where the user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

fails to pay fees;

abuses Course Material;

shares login details;

harasses others;

cheats or plagiarises;

submits false documents;

disrupts learning;

creates legal, financial, reputational, safety or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect may also suspend or discontinue EduU, any Course, or any part of the service at any time.

33. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these EduU Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of EduU after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

34. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

35. Acceptance

By using EduU, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is the Platform provider and education access facilitator;

accept that Courses may be provided by third-party Training Providers;

accept that Inkolo Connect does not guarantee accreditation, results, certificates, employment, income or acceptance by third parties unless expressly stated;

accept responsibility for reading Course details before registering or paying;

accept responsibility for their own participation, attendance, conduct, assignments and device access;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from Course participation, user conduct, provider disputes, assessments, certificates or educational outcomes.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user registers for a Course:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the EduU Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect provides the platform and may facilitate access to educational programmes, but does not guarantee accreditation, results, certificates, employment, income or acceptance by any employer, institution or third party unless expressly stated in writing.

Suggested Course Registration Warning

Before payment or enrolment:

Please read the Course details carefully before registering. Confirm the provider, cost, duration, accreditation status, refund rules, certificate type and requirements. Inkolo Connect is not responsible if you register for the wrong Course or if the Course does not meet your expectations.

Suggested Certificate Disclaimer

On certificates or completion pages:

This certificate or completion record confirms participation or completion only as described in the Course details. It does not guarantee employment, income, admission to another institution or professional recognition unless expressly stated by the relevant accredited provider.

Suggested Parent / Guardian Checkbox for Minors

Where the Learner is under 18:

I confirm that I am the parent or legal guardian of the minor Learner and I consent to their participation in this EduU programme. I accept responsibility for reviewing the Course suitability, supervising access where necessary and ensuring that the minor complies with the EduU Service Terms and Conditions.

legalAcceptances.documentSnapshot:
CATCH A RIDE SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Duranki Platform / Inkolo Connect

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These Catch a Ride Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users who access, request, offer, accept, provide, pay for, contribute towards, communicate about, cancel, complete or otherwise use the Catch a Ride service made available through the Duranki platform and/or Inkolo Connect.

By using the Catch a Ride service, the user agrees to be bound by these Terms and Conditions, together with the main Duranki Platform Terms and Conditions, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Community Rules, Safety Rules, and any additional policies published on the platform from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Duranki” means the Duranki digital platform, including its app, website, dashboards, wallet systems, payment systems, communication tools, location tools and related services.

“Inkolo Connect” means the community and church member-management service operated through Duranki.

“Platform” means Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any related digital application, dashboard, website, wallet, communication tool or service module.

“Catch a Ride” means the platform feature that allows registered members to request, offer, arrange, communicate about or contribute towards rides, lifts, transport assistance or member-to-member travel.

“Driver” means a registered user who offers or agrees to transport another user.

“Passenger” means a registered user who requests, accepts or receives a ride.

“Ride” means a lift, trip, journey, transport arrangement, shared route, pickup, drop-off or travel arrangement between users.

“Free Ride” means a ride where no payment, fee, reward or contribution is requested or paid.

“Cost-Sharing Ride” means a ride where the Passenger contributes towards actual trip-related costs such as fuel, tolls, parking or other reasonable expenses, without the Driver making a profit.

“Paid Ride” means any ride where the Driver receives payment, reward, profit, fee, fare, commission or compensation beyond reasonable cost-sharing.

“User” means any person who uses the Catch a Ride service as a Driver, Passenger or viewer.

2. Purpose of the Catch a Ride Service

The Catch a Ride service is intended to help registered members connect with each other for transport assistance within their community, church, organisation or approved platform network.

The service may be used for:

member-to-member lifts;

shared travel;

community ride coordination;

free lifts between members;

cost-sharing arrangements;

approved paid rides where legally permitted;

transport support for community activities;

approved future mobility services offered through the platform.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect provide the digital connection tool only. They do not own, operate or control the vehicles used by users.

3. Role of Duranki and Inkolo Connect

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a taxi operator, bus operator, transport company, chauffeur service, vehicle owner, employer, insurer, emergency service, tour operator, public transport operator or transport regulator, unless expressly stated in writing.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee:

that a Driver is available;

that a Passenger will accept a ride;

that a Driver will arrive;

that a Passenger will be ready;

that a vehicle is roadworthy;

that a Driver is licensed;

that a Driver has the required permit;

that a Driver has insurance;

that a ride will be safe;

that a route will be correct;

that the fare or contribution is fair;

that the Driver or Passenger will behave properly;

that the ride will be completed.

Any ride is arranged directly between the Driver and Passenger. Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform facility.

4. Important Legal Notice: Free, Cost-Sharing and Paid Rides

Users must understand the difference between free rides, cost-sharing rides and paid rides.

A Free Ride is a voluntary lift where the Driver receives no payment, reward or contribution.

A Cost-Sharing Ride is a private arrangement where the Passenger contributes only towards actual costs such as fuel, parking or toll fees.

A Paid Ride may be treated as passenger transport for reward and may require licences, permits, public operating authorisation, professional driving permits, insurance and compliance with transport laws.

South African law regulates public transport and e-hailing services, and paid passenger transport may require operating licences. The National Department of Transport has indicated that e-hailing is recognised as a transport service type and that operators must comply with applicable licensing requirements.

Each Driver is solely responsible for ensuring that they comply with all laws, permits, licences, insurance requirements and vehicle requirements before offering any ride, especially any paid ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that any Driver is legally authorised to provide paid transport.

5. No Unlawful Transport Services

Users may not use Catch a Ride to operate an unlawful taxi, e-hailing, shuttle, scholar transport, courier, delivery, tourism, public transport or commercial transport service.

A Driver must not offer paid rides unless the Driver has all legal authorisations required for that type of transport.

Where a Driver transports passengers for payment without the necessary authority, the Driver accepts full responsibility for all legal, financial, insurance, regulatory, criminal and civil consequences.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, block or remove any Driver or Passenger if the platform suspects unlawful transport activity.

6. User Eligibility

To use Catch a Ride, a user must:

be registered on the Duranki platform;

provide accurate personal information;

pass any required verification checks;

comply with platform rules;

comply with community rules;

comply with South African law;

not be suspended or banned from the service.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require identity verification, selfie verification, document uploads, vehicle documents, licence copies, insurance confirmation, proof of address or additional checks before allowing a user to act as a Driver.

7. Driver Requirements

A Driver must:

hold a valid driver’s licence for the vehicle used;

be legally entitled to drive in South Africa;

ensure the vehicle is licensed and roadworthy;

ensure the vehicle has valid licence discs and registration;

ensure the vehicle is safe and mechanically sound;

ensure the vehicle has enough fuel or charge for the trip;

comply with all road traffic laws;

comply with speed limits;

not drive under the influence of alcohol, drugs, medication or fatigue;

not overload the vehicle;

not transport more passengers than the vehicle is legally allowed to carry;

ensure all passengers use seatbelts where available;

treat passengers respectfully;

not harass, threaten, abuse or endanger passengers;

ensure they have suitable insurance cover;

ensure they hold any required permit, public operating licence or professional driving permit where applicable.

Drivers must check their motor insurance because carrying fare-paying passengers or driving for reward may affect insurance cover. South African insurance guidance commonly warns that lift clubs, paid passengers or commercial use may affect claims if not disclosed to the insurer.

8. Passenger Responsibilities

A Passenger must:

provide accurate pickup and drop-off information;

be ready at the agreed pickup time;

behave respectfully;

wear a seatbelt where available;

not damage the vehicle;

not distract the Driver;

not carry unlawful, dangerous or prohibited items;

not harass, threaten, abuse or endanger the Driver;

not request unsafe driving;

not request unlawful stops, routes or conduct;

pay any agreed lawful contribution or fee through the approved platform method where required;

check that the vehicle and Driver match the platform information before entering the vehicle.

Passengers use rides at their own risk and must apply reasonable personal safety precautions.

9. Vehicle Safety

Drivers are solely responsible for the safety, roadworthiness, maintenance and legal compliance of their vehicles.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not inspect vehicles unless expressly stated in writing.

Drivers must not use vehicles with:

faulty brakes;

defective tyres;

broken lights;

unsafe doors;

cracked windscreens affecting visibility;

steering defects;

fuel leaks;

engine problems that create danger;

expired licence discs;

known mechanical faults;

any condition that makes the vehicle unsafe or unlawful.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may request vehicle photos or documents, but this does not make them responsible for verifying safety or roadworthiness.

10. Driver Availability and Live Map

The platform may allow Drivers to switch availability on or off and may show available Drivers on a live map.

The live map, location display, estimated distance, estimated arrival time or route information may be inaccurate, delayed or unavailable.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not guarantee that:

the Driver’s location is accurate;

the Driver is still available;

the Driver will accept a request;

the Driver will arrive at the estimated time;

the route is correct;

the ride will be completed.

Users must not rely on Catch a Ride for emergencies, urgent medical transport or critical transport needs.

11. Ride Requests and Acceptance

A Passenger may request a ride from an available Driver.

A Driver may accept or reject a ride request.

A ride is not confirmed until the Driver accepts and the platform or the parties confirm the arrangement.

Drivers and Passengers may cancel rides, but repeated cancellations, misuse or abuse may result in restrictions or suspension.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for missed appointments, missed work, missed flights, missed events, loss of income or inconvenience caused by failed or cancelled rides.

12. Payments, Contributions and Wallet Use

Where Catch a Ride allows payment, contribution or wallet transfer, users must use the approved platform payment method unless otherwise permitted.

Payments may include:

fuel contribution;

toll contribution;

parking contribution;

agreed ride fee;

platform service fee;

wallet fee;

payment-processing fee;

cancellation fee, if applicable.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may charge platform fees, deduct service fees, delay settlement, hold funds, reverse payments or block wallet activity where fraud, disputes, chargebacks, unlawful activity or risk is suspected.

Payment through the platform does not mean that Duranki or Inkolo Connect guarantees the Driver, Passenger, vehicle, ride, safety, legal compliance or successful completion of the trip.

13. No Cash Payments Where Platform Payment Is Required

Where the platform requires wallet or in-app payment, users may not bypass the platform by arranging cash, EFT or outside payment.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for any loss, fraud, robbery, dispute or non-payment resulting from payment outside the platform.

Users who bypass platform payments may be suspended or permanently banned.

14. Insurance

Drivers are solely responsible for ensuring that their insurance policy covers the way they use their vehicle.

This includes cover for:

private use;

lift club or cost-sharing use;

fare-paying passengers;

commercial use;

public transport use;

e-hailing use;

passenger liability;

third-party liability;

accident damage;

theft or hijacking.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not provide vehicle insurance, passenger insurance, driver insurance, accident cover or public liability cover unless expressly stated in writing.

A Passenger should not assume that a Driver is insured.

A Driver should not assume that their normal private vehicle insurance covers paid or contribution-based rides.

15. Accidents, Injury, Death and Emergency Events

Users understand and accept that transport carries inherent risks, including accidents, injury, death, crime, hijacking, assault, theft, vehicle failure, unsafe driving, road hazards and third-party conduct.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for injury, death, loss, damage, trauma, medical costs, legal costs, repair costs or any other claim arising from a ride, except where liability cannot lawfully be excluded.

In the event of an emergency, users must contact emergency services, police, medical assistance or roadside assistance directly.

Catch a Ride is not an emergency response service.

16. Safety Rules

Drivers and Passengers must comply with the following safety rules:

do not drive or ride if you feel unsafe;

check the Driver, Passenger and vehicle details before the ride;

use the in-app communication tools where possible;

do not share unnecessary private information;

do not carry weapons or unlawful items;

do not use abusive, sexual, threatening or discriminatory language;

do not request unsafe driving;

do not overload the vehicle;

do not transport intoxicated or dangerous passengers where this creates risk;

do not transport minors without proper consent;

do not accept rides that appear suspicious;

report unsafe behaviour immediately.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend any user who breaches safety rules.

17. Minors and Children

Minors may not request, offer, accept or provide rides without the involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian.

Drivers may not transport children alone unless:

the child’s parent or guardian has authorised the ride;

the Driver is legally permitted to do so;

the arrangement complies with all platform rules;

the Driver complies with any child-safety requirements;

the platform permits that type of ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may prohibit child transport, scholar transport or minor-related rides unless additional verification, consent and compliance processes are completed.

18. Prohibited Conduct

Users may not use Catch a Ride to:

commit fraud;

rob, assault, threaten or harass another user;

transport illegal goods;

transport drugs, weapons or stolen property;

transport passengers unlawfully for reward;

bypass transport laws;

impersonate another person;

use a fake profile;

use a vehicle that does not match the profile;

use another person’s driver account;

create false ride requests;

submit fake complaints;

submit fake payment proof;

evade platform fees;

misuse location data;

stalk or track another user;

discriminate against users unlawfully;

damage the reputation of Duranki, Inkolo Connect or any community.

19. Ratings and Reviews

The platform may allow Drivers and Passengers to rate each other.

Ratings help improve community safety, but they are not a guarantee that a user is safe, honest, reliable or lawful.

Users must provide honest and fair ratings.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may remove false, abusive, defamatory or manipulated reviews.

Repeated poor ratings may result in account restrictions or suspension.

20. Background Checks and Verification

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may require identity checks, document checks, vehicle checks or criminal-record-related checks where legally permitted.

However, verification does not guarantee that a user is safe, trustworthy, insured, qualified or legally compliant.

Users remain responsible for their own safety decisions.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may refuse Driver access if verification is incomplete, expired, inconsistent, suspicious or failed.

21. Location Data

Catch a Ride may use GPS, live location, route data and trip information.

Users agree that location data may be processed for:

ride matching;

pickup and drop-off;

route display;

safety;

fraud prevention;

dispute resolution;

service improvement;

compliance;

support.

Location data may be inaccurate due to signal issues, phone settings, network delays or device failure.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for losses caused by inaccurate location data.

22. Privacy and Communication

Users may communicate through in-app chat, phone, notifications or other platform tools.

Users must not misuse another user’s personal information.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may monitor, store, review or disclose ride-related communications and data where necessary for safety, fraud prevention, legal compliance, dispute handling or enforcement of these Terms.

Personal information will be processed in line with the platform Privacy Policy and applicable data-protection laws.

23. Community and Church-Based Use

Where Catch a Ride is used within a church, community or Inkolo Connect environment, the service is intended to support trusted member-to-member assistance.

However, being part of the same church, branch, community or organisation does not guarantee that a Driver or Passenger is safe, honest, reliable, insured or legally compliant.

Churches, pastors, bishops, KZNCC, community leaders and administrators are not responsible for rides unless they personally provide, arrange or control the specific ride.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may allow community administrators to view certain reporting information, but this does not make them liable for the conduct of users.

24. Disputes

Drivers and Passengers must first try to resolve disputes directly and respectfully.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may assist by reviewing:

ride records;

payment records;

cancellation history;

GPS logs;

chat messages;

complaints;

ratings;

uploaded evidence.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not a court, arbitrator, insurer, transport regulator, police service or debt collector.

Any platform decision to refund, reverse, suspend, block or release funds is a risk-control decision and does not prevent users from pursuing legal remedies independently.

25. Service Suspension

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or terminate access to Catch a Ride at any time where a user:

breaches these Terms;

fails verification;

drives unlawfully;

drives unsafely;

receives serious complaints;

is suspected of fraud;

is suspected of criminal conduct;

misuses the wallet;

bypasses platform payments;

creates legal, safety, reputational or financial risk;

provides false documents;

uses another person’s account or vehicle.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may also suspend the entire Catch a Ride service, or certain functions, where required for compliance, safety, legal or operational reasons.

26. No Employment Relationship

Drivers are independent users.

Nothing in these Terms creates an employment relationship, agency relationship, partnership, joint venture or representative relationship between Duranki, Inkolo Connect and any Driver or Passenger.

Drivers are not employees of Duranki or Inkolo Connect.

Drivers are responsible for their own taxes, permits, licences, expenses, insurance, vehicle maintenance and legal obligations.

27. No Warranty

The Catch a Ride service is provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not warrant that:

the service will always be available;

the service will be error-free;

the map will be accurate;

the Driver will arrive;

the Passenger will pay;

the vehicle will be safe;

the ride will be lawful;

the ride will be comfortable;

the ride will be completed;

disputes will be resolved in a user’s favour.

28. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates are not liable for any claim, loss, damage, injury, death, cost or expense arising from:

any ride;

road accidents;

reckless or negligent driving;

unsafe vehicles;

unlawful transport;

lack of insurance;

failed pickups;

late arrivals;

cancelled rides;

non-payment;

payment disputes;

passenger behaviour;

driver behaviour;

theft, robbery, hijacking or assault;

property damage;

injury or death;

inaccurate GPS or location data;

platform downtime;

third-party provider failures;

breach of law by a user.

Duranki and Inkolo Connect are not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of business, missed appointments, missed flights, emotional distress or reputational harm.

Where liability cannot legally be excluded, Duranki and Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fee actually paid to Duranki or Inkolo Connect for the specific ride giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

29. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Duranki, Inkolo Connect, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, community administrators and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of Catch a Ride;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

the user’s driving;

the user’s passenger conduct;

an accident;

injury or death;

lack of licence, permit or insurance;

unlawful paid transport;

fraud or criminal conduct;

misuse of personal information;

disputes with another user;

claims by passengers, drivers, insurers, regulators, law enforcement or third parties.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the platform.

30. Reporting Safety Issues

Users must immediately report serious issues, including:

unsafe driving;

harassment;

assault;

theft;

fraud;

fake profiles;

vehicle mismatch;

intoxicated driving;

unlawful paid transport;

child-safety concerns;

dangerous conduct.

Where a crime, accident or emergency occurs, users must contact SAPS, emergency medical services or the relevant authority directly.

Reporting to Duranki or Inkolo Connect is not a substitute for reporting to law enforcement or emergency services.

31. Changes to These Terms

Duranki and Inkolo Connect may update these Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the platform or sent by notification.

Continued use of Catch a Ride after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

32. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

33. Acceptance

By using Catch a Ride, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Duranki and Inkolo Connect are only platform facilitators;

accept that rides are arranged directly between users;

accept that transport carries safety and legal risks;

accept responsibility for their own conduct, legal compliance and safety decisions.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before requesting or offering a ride:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the Catch a Ride Terms and Conditions. I understand that Duranki and Inkolo Connect only provide the platform connection tool and are not responsible for the driver, passenger, vehicle, route, safety, insurance, permits, payment disputes, accidents, injury, loss or damage arising from any ride.

Suggested Driver Checkbox Text

Before a user switches their driver availability on:

I confirm that I have a valid driver’s licence, that my vehicle is roadworthy and legally licensed, that I will comply with all traffic and transport laws, and that I am responsible for my own insurance, permits and legal compliance. I understand that paid transport may require additional licences or authorisations.

Suggested Passenger Safety Warning

On the ride request screen:

For your safety, confirm the driver, vehicle and registration number before entering the vehicle. Do not enter if anything feels unsafe. Duranki and Inkolo Connect do not operate the vehicle and do not guarantee the driver, passenger, vehicle or ride.

KZNCC SERVICE TERMS AND CONDITIONS

Inkolo Connect Platform

Effective Date: 10 June 2026 Version: 1.0

Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd - Registration number 2025/674136/07

These KZNCC Service Terms and Conditions apply to all users, members, churches, branches, church leaders, administrators, service providers and community participants who access, use, participate in or receive communication, services, benefits, records, dashboards, payments or support through the KZNCC service environment made available on the Inkolo Connect platform.

By accessing or using any KZNCC-related service, dashboard, community, church profile, member profile, payment tool, communication tool or service module on the Inkolo Connect platform, the user agrees to these Terms and Conditions, together with the Inkolo Connect Terms and Conditions of Use, Privacy Policy, Wallet Terms, Payment Terms, Community Terms, Service-Specific Terms and any additional policies published by Inkolo Connect from time to time.

1. Definitions

In these Terms:

“Inkolo Connect”, “Inkolo”, “we”, “us” or “our” means Inkolo Connect (Pty) Ltd, including its platform, systems, dashboards, applications, service modules, employees, contractors, agents and approved platform partners.

“Platform” means the Inkolo Connect digital platform, including its website, mobile application, dashboards, member profiles, wallet tools, payment tools, communication tools, document tools, community tools and service modules.

“KZNCC” means the KwaZulu-Natal Christian Council, including its authorised structures, office bearers, representatives, member churches, branches, administrators and approved delegates, where applicable.

“KZNCC Service” means the digital service environment made available through Inkolo Connect for KZNCC, its member churches, church leaders, administrators and members.

“Church” means any church, branch, congregation, denomination, ministry, fellowship, religious organisation or faith-based body linked to or participating under KZNCC through the Platform.

“Member” means any individual registered on the Platform and linked to KZNCC, a Church, branch, community or service.

“Church Leader” means any pastor, bishop, minister, reverend, priest, elder, deacon, administrator, committee member, board member, authorised officer or other person appointed or recognised by a Church or KZNCC.

“KZNCC Administrator” means any person authorised by KZNCC to access, view, manage or use KZNCC-related dashboards, records, communications or reports on the Platform.

“User” means any person who accesses or uses the Platform, including Members, Church Leaders, KZNCC representatives, administrators, service providers, merchants, donors, volunteers and community participants.

“Service Provider” means any third-party provider, insurer, bank, payment provider, VAS provider, funeral cover provider, education provider, connectivity provider, merchant, vendor or other approved partner whose service may be made available to KZNCC, Churches or Members through the Platform.

2. Purpose of the KZNCC Service

The KZNCC Service is intended to provide digital tools to assist KZNCC, its member Churches and Members with platform-based administration, communication, member registration, digital community management, service access and reporting.

The KZNCC Service may include:

KZNCC dashboard access;

Church and branch dashboards;

member registration tools;

digital member profiles;

communication tools;

event tools;

document upload and storage tools;

Wallet and payment facilitation tools;

service activation tools;

reports and member counts;

access to approved paid and free services;

access to third-party services;

benefit communication;

revenue-share reporting where applicable;

any other approved platform service made available from time to time.

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform and related technology tools only.

Inkolo Connect does not control, manage or take responsibility for KZNCC’s internal governance, decisions, leadership, membership, communication content, pastoral affairs, church disputes, member disputes or KZNCC’s relationship with its Churches and Members.

3. Inkolo Connect as Platform Provider Only

The user understands and agrees that Inkolo Connect is the platform provider only.

Unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement, Inkolo Connect is not:

KZNCC;

a Church;

a Church Leader;

a religious authority;

a governing body of KZNCC;

a governing body of any Church;

an employer of KZNCC staff or Church staff;

a representative of any individual Member;

a decision-maker in KZNCC internal affairs;

a guarantor of any KZNCC benefit, service or decision;

responsible for disputes between KZNCC and Members;

responsible for disputes between KZNCC and Churches;

responsible for disputes between Churches and Members.

Inkolo Connect provides digital access, platform infrastructure, dashboards, communication tools, payment facilitation tools and service access tools.

Inkolo Connect does not assume liability for the conduct, decisions, omissions, statements, disputes or obligations of KZNCC, its Churches, Church Leaders, administrators or Members.

4. Relationship Between KZNCC and Members

Any relationship between KZNCC and a Member remains directly between KZNCC and that Member.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

whether a Member is accepted by KZNCC;

whether a Member is recognised by a Church;

whether a Member qualifies for any KZNCC programme;

whether a Member receives a benefit;

whether a Member is included or excluded from a KZNCC activity;

whether a Member is disciplined, suspended or removed by a Church or KZNCC;

any promise, representation or decision made by KZNCC or a Church;

any disagreement between KZNCC and a Member.

Any dispute between KZNCC and a Member must be resolved directly between KZNCC and the Member, according to KZNCC’s rules, Church rules, applicable mandates, policies, constitutions, dispute procedures and South African law.

5. Relationship Between KZNCC and Churches

Any relationship between KZNCC and its member Churches remains directly between KZNCC and those Churches.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

whether a Church is accepted by KZNCC;

whether a Church remains affiliated with KZNCC;

whether a Church is removed from KZNCC;

whether KZNCC recognises a Church Leader;

whether KZNCC recognises a branch;

disputes between KZNCC and Churches;

disputes between Churches;

branch recognition disputes;

leadership recognition disputes;

mandate disputes;

governance disputes;

disciplinary disputes;

financial disputes between KZNCC and Churches.

Where a dispute exists between KZNCC and a Church, Inkolo Connect may restrict dashboard changes, administrator access, communication functions or payment changes until proper written authority or legal clarity is provided.

6. KZNCC Dashboard and Reporting

The Platform may provide KZNCC with dashboards, reports and information relating to member churches, branches, member counts, service activation, paid services, revenue-share information, communication records and other approved data.

Dashboard information is dependent on data captured by users, Churches, KZNCC administrators, Service Providers and platform systems.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that dashboard information will always be complete, accurate, current or error-free.

KZNCC and authorised administrators are responsible for reviewing and verifying information used for decision-making.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for internal KZNCC decisions, Church decisions, financial decisions, member decisions or service decisions based on dashboard information unless a proven platform fault directly caused the error.

7. KZNCC Administrators

KZNCC may appoint authorised administrators to access and manage certain KZNCC-related platform functions.

KZNCC is responsible for ensuring that its administrators are properly authorised, trained, trustworthy and mandated.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for any loss, dispute, unauthorised instruction, incorrect message, incorrect member update, payment dispute, data change, reputational harm or internal conflict caused by a KZNCC administrator.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or restrict administrator access where there is suspected fraud, conflict, unauthorised use, leadership dispute, legal risk, security risk, privacy risk or breach of these Terms.

8. Church and Branch Selection

Members may be required to select their Church, branch or KZNCC-linked community from the Platform.

The Member is responsible for selecting the correct Church or branch.

KZNCC and Church administrators may assist with correcting incorrect selections.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for incorrect communication, incorrect dashboard allocation, missed announcements, wrong service allocation, wrong payment allocation or disputes caused by incorrect Church or branch selection by the Member or administrator.

9. Communications from KZNCC and Churches

The Platform may allow KZNCC, Churches, Church Leaders and authorised administrators to send announcements, messages, event notices, documents, service notices, benefit notices or other communications to Members.

KZNCC and the relevant Church are responsible for the content of their communications.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for:

incorrect messages;

unauthorised messages;

misleading messages;

offensive messages;

late messages;

failed delivery;

internal political messages;

doctrinal messages;

pastoral messages;

member reactions to messages;

disputes arising from communications sent by KZNCC or Churches.

Inkolo Connect may remove, restrict or block communications that are unlawful, abusive, discriminatory, defamatory, fraudulent, misleading, threatening, unsafe, reputationally harmful or in breach of Platform rules.

10. KZNCC Services and Benefits

KZNCC may use the Platform to communicate, introduce, promote or manage services, programmes, benefits or opportunities for Members.

These may include free services, paid services, funeral cover, training, connectivity, community benefits, vouchers, job opportunities, wallet services, VAS services or other approved services.

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that:

any Member will qualify for a benefit;

any benefit will be available permanently;

KZNCC will approve a Member;

a Church will approve a Member;

a Service Provider will approve a Member;

any benefit will be paid;

any service will continue;

any programme will succeed;

any specific outcome will be achieved.

Where a service is provided by a third-party Service Provider, the contract is between the Member and that Service Provider, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing.

11. Payments, Donations, Tithes, Offerings and Contributions

The Platform may allow payments relating to KZNCC, Churches, branches, services, events, donations, tithes, offerings, contributions, subscriptions or benefits.

Unless expressly stated otherwise, such payments are made to the relevant KZNCC structure, Church, Service Provider or recipient and not to Inkolo Connect.

Inkolo Connect may act only as a payment facilitator, Wallet provider, payment channel, technology provider or reporting tool.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for how KZNCC or a Church uses funds received from Members.

KZNCC and Churches are responsible for their own:

financial governance;

bank accounts;

accounting records;

tax treatment;

receipts;

reporting;

use of funds;

refunds;

member queries;

compliance with their constitutions and applicable law.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes about donations, tithes, offerings, KZNCC contributions, Church funds, alleged misuse of funds, leadership disputes over money, donor dissatisfaction or financial decisions made by KZNCC or Churches.

12. Revenue Share and Service Fees

Certain paid services may include agreed revenue-share structures, platform fees, service fees, administration fees, operating fees or partner fees.

Revenue-share arrangements are governed by the relevant mandate, annexure, commercial agreement, service provider agreement or written arrangement.

Unless expressly stated otherwise, Members do not acquire any right to revenue-share payments between Inkolo Connect, KZNCC, Churches or Service Providers.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes between KZNCC and Churches about revenue-share allocation, internal distribution, use of funds, budgets, salaries, expenses or financial governance.

13. Third-Party Service Providers

The Platform may give Members access to services provided by third-party Service Providers.

These may include:

funeral cover providers;

insurers;

banks;

payment providers;

VAS providers;

connectivity providers;

education providers;

job providers;

transport providers;

property providers;

merchants and vendors;

other approved partners.

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for the Service Provider’s approval, rejection, pricing, delivery, quality, claims, refunds, cancellations, failures, delays or terms.

The Member must read and accept the Service Provider’s own terms before using the service.

14. Internal KZNCC, Church and Member Disputes

Inkolo Connect is not responsible for any internal dispute involving KZNCC, Churches, Church Leaders, administrators or Members.

This includes disputes about:

leadership;

membership;

branch control;

church recognition;

KZNCC affiliation;

removals;

discipline;

doctrine;

governance;

finances;

donations;

community benefits;

communication;

events;

service allocation;

pastoral matters;

family disputes;

political or factional disputes.

Inkolo Connect may preserve records, restrict access or suspend certain functions where necessary to reduce platform risk, but Inkolo Connect does not become responsible for resolving the dispute.

15. Leadership and Authority Disputes

Where there is uncertainty or dispute about who has authority to act for KZNCC, a Church, branch or community, Inkolo Connect may require proof of authority before making platform changes.

Proof may include:

KZNCC resolution;

Church resolution;

board resolution;

constitution;

mandate;

minutes of meeting;

letter from authorised leadership;

court order;

legal opinion;

any other document reasonably required.

Until Inkolo Connect is satisfied, it may freeze or restrict:

administrator changes;

payment changes;

dashboard changes;

communication rights;

member imports;

Church profile changes;

revenue-share settings;

service activation changes.

Inkolo Connect does not decide who the lawful Church Leader or KZNCC representative is. Inkolo Connect only applies reasonable platform risk controls.

16. No Religious, Doctrinal or Pastoral Responsibility

Inkolo Connect does not control KZNCC’s or any Church’s doctrine, sermons, teachings, faith practices, spiritual guidance, pastoral counselling, religious events, rituals or community decisions.

Any religious, spiritual, doctrinal or pastoral content is the responsibility of KZNCC, the relevant Church or the user who provides it.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for religious disagreement, spiritual harm claims, offence, doctrinal disputes, pastoral disputes, member dissatisfaction or emotional distress arising from KZNCC or Church conduct.

17. Events, Meetings and Programmes

KZNCC and Churches may use the Platform to advertise, coordinate or manage events, meetings, training sessions, services, outreach programmes or community activities.

The relevant organiser is responsible for the event or programme.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for:

cancelled events;

event injuries;

event accidents;

venue safety;

transport to or from events;

crowd control;

security;

food safety;

child supervision;

lost property;

event disputes;

incorrect event information.

Users attend KZNCC or Church events at their own risk, unless otherwise stated by the event organiser.

18. User Conduct

Users must use the KZNCC Service respectfully, lawfully and responsibly.

Users may not:

harass KZNCC representatives;

harass Church Leaders;

harass Members;

threaten or intimidate others;

post defamatory content;

publish private disputes;

spread false allegations;

misuse religious or community platforms;

impersonate KZNCC or Church Leaders;

upload false documents;

misuse personal information;

create conflict through platform misuse;

commit fraud;

interfere with Platform security;

use the Platform for unlawful purposes.

Inkolo Connect may suspend or remove users who misuse the Platform or create legal, safety, reputational, operational or community risk.

19. Personal Information and Data

Inkolo Connect may process personal information for:

user registration;

KZNCC member records;

Church and branch allocation;

communication;

dashboard reporting;

service activation;

payment processing;

Wallet services;

document storage;

fraud prevention;

compliance;

support;

dispute handling;

legal obligations.

Personal information may be shared with KZNCC, authorised KZNCC administrators, Churches, Church administrators, Service Providers, Payment Partners, verification providers, regulators or law enforcement where necessary, lawful or consented to.

Users, KZNCC and Churches must not misuse personal information obtained through the Platform.

20. Data Accuracy

KZNCC, Churches, administrators and Members are responsible for ensuring that information captured on the Platform is accurate and updated.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for disputes or losses caused by inaccurate, incomplete, outdated or false information supplied by KZNCC, Churches, administrators, Members or Service Providers.

Inkolo Connect may correct, restrict, archive or preserve data where necessary for platform integrity, legal compliance or dispute handling.

21. Platform Moderation Rights

Inkolo Connect may, at any time and without prior notice:

remove content;

restrict access;

suspend administrator rights;

restrict payment functions;

restrict communication tools;

request proof of authority;

preserve records;

block abusive users;

hide disputed information;

suspend a Church or KZNCC-related dashboard;

report unlawful conduct.

These actions may be taken where Inkolo Connect reasonably believes there is legal, financial, operational, security, reputational, safety, governance, privacy or compliance risk.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss, embarrassment, income loss, donation loss, reputation loss or opportunity loss caused by moderation actions taken in good faith.

22. No Endorsement or Guarantee

The presence of KZNCC, a Church, Church Leader, service, benefit or programme on the Platform does not mean Inkolo Connect guarantees, endorses or certifies:

KZNCC’s internal decisions;

Church governance;

Church leadership;

Church finances;

member conduct;

service provider conduct;

benefit availability;

religious content;

financial outcomes;

any specific result.

Users must make their own decisions about participation, services, payments, donations and benefits.

23. Complaints

Complaints about KZNCC decisions, Church conduct, leadership, doctrine, membership, discipline, donations, events, pastoral care, governance or internal disputes must be directed to KZNCC, the relevant Church or the applicable authority.

Complaints about technical platform issues, Wallet errors, dashboard access, platform misuse, privacy concerns or suspected fraud may be submitted to Inkolo Connect support.

Inkolo Connect is not obliged to resolve disputes that are internal to KZNCC, a Church, branch or Member relationship.

24. Service Availability

Inkolo Connect does not guarantee that the KZNCC Service, dashboards, communication tools, payment tools or service modules will always be available, uninterrupted, secure or error-free.

The service may be unavailable due to:

maintenance;

upgrades;

technical failures;

network failures;

payment partner failures;

service provider failures;

cyber incidents;

power failures;

mobile network failures;

events beyond reasonable control.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for loss caused by downtime, delayed messages, failed access, delayed payments or service interruptions.

25. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates are not liable for any loss, damage, claim, expense or liability arising from:

disputes between KZNCC and Members;

disputes between KZNCC and Churches;

disputes between Churches and Members;

disputes between Church Leaders;

leadership disputes;

governance disputes;

membership disputes;

disciplinary disputes;

doctrine or religious disputes;

pastoral disputes;

communication disputes;

donation disputes;

tithe or offering disputes;

revenue-share disputes;

use of KZNCC or Church funds;

KZNCC administrator actions;

Church administrator actions;

incorrect information supplied by KZNCC, Churches or Members;

service provider failures;

rejected third-party services;

failed benefits;

event incidents;

internal political or factional disputes;

privacy breaches caused by users, KZNCC or Churches;

platform restrictions imposed in good faith;

platform downtime or technical failures.

Inkolo Connect is not liable for indirect, special, consequential or economic losses, including loss of income, loss of donations, loss of opportunity, reputational harm, emotional distress or spiritual distress.

Where liability cannot lawfully be excluded, Inkolo Connect’s liability will be limited to the platform fees actually paid to Inkolo Connect for the specific platform service giving rise to the claim, unless a higher amount is required by law.

26. Indemnity

The user indemnifies and holds harmless Inkolo Connect, Duranki, their directors, shareholders, employees, contractors, agents, partners, churches, communities, administrators, Service Providers and affiliates against any claim, loss, damage, penalty, legal cost, liability or expense arising from:

the user’s use of the KZNCC Service;

the user’s breach of these Terms;

the user’s breach of law;

disputes between the user and KZNCC;

disputes between the user and a Church;

disputes between the user and another Member;

disputes between KZNCC and Churches;

disputes between Church Leaders;

donations, tithes, offerings or contributions;

user content, messages or documents;

defamatory or unlawful statements made by the user;

misuse of personal information;

incorrect information supplied by the user;

claims arising from KZNCC events, Church events or community activities;

any internal KZNCC, Church or Member dispute involving the user.

KZNCC, Churches and administrators using the Platform also indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from their own decisions, communications, governance, funds, administrator actions, member disputes, leadership disputes and internal processes.

This indemnity continues after the user stops using the Platform.

27. Suspension and Removal

Inkolo Connect may suspend, restrict or remove a user, administrator, Church or KZNCC-related access where:

these Terms are breached;

false information is provided;

fraud is suspected;

authority is disputed;

administrator access is misused;

personal information is misused;

unlawful content is posted;

payments are disputed;

platform security is threatened;

there is legal, financial, operational, reputational, privacy, safety or compliance risk;

a lawful instruction, court order, regulator, Service Provider or authorised mandate requires action.

Suspension from the Platform does not necessarily determine membership status in KZNCC or a Church. Membership status remains a matter between the relevant Member, Church and/or KZNCC.

28. Changes to These Terms

Inkolo Connect may update these KZNCC Service Terms from time to time.

Updated Terms may be published on the Platform, sent by notification or made available through the user dashboard.

Continued use of the KZNCC Service after updated Terms are published means the user accepts the updated Terms.

29. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

Any dispute will be handled according to South African law, subject to any rights that may not lawfully be excluded.

30. Acceptance

By using the KZNCC Service on the Inkolo Connect Platform, the user confirms that they:

have read these Terms;

understand these Terms;

accept that Inkolo Connect is the Platform provider only;

accept that Inkolo Connect is not KZNCC, a Church, a Church Leader or a religious authority;

accept that Inkolo Connect is not responsible for what happens between KZNCC and Members;

accept that Inkolo Connect is not responsible for what happens between KZNCC and Churches;

accept that disputes between Members, KZNCC, Churches and Church Leaders must be resolved by those parties;

accept responsibility for their own conduct, information, payments, communications and service choices;

agree to indemnify Inkolo Connect against claims arising from KZNCC, Church and Member disputes.

Suggested Platform Checkbox Text

Before a user joins or uses the KZNCC service environment:

I confirm that I have read and accepted the KZNCC Service Terms and Conditions. I understand that Inkolo Connect is only the digital platform provider and is not responsible for disputes, decisions, communication, governance, payments, donations, benefits, discipline, leadership issues or any matter between KZNCC, churches, church leaders and members.

Suggested Short Disclaimer for KZNCC Dashboard

Inkolo Connect provides the digital platform only. KZNCC and its member churches remain responsible for their own governance, members, communication, decisions, payments, donations, events, benefits and internal disputes.

Suggested Member Notice

Your relationship with KZNCC and your selected church remains between you, KZNCC and that church. Inkolo Connect only provides the technology platform and is not responsible for internal KZNCC, church or member disputes.
